title: iOS: historical assistant messages spoken on session load (subscribe replay)
description:
When opening a session, backend sends session_history containing every message past the client's last_seq cursor. On a fresh install, after CoreData prune, or after long absence the cursor lags and the reply contains the entire conversation. SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload (SessionSyncManager.swift:385-585) feeds every assistant message whose (sessionId, seq) row is newly inserted into newAssistantTexts (line 461-467), then speaks them all because isActiveSession is true (line 554-570). Result: hours-old assistant text gets read aloud the moment the user opens the session.

ROOT CAUSE: 'inserted = true' from upsertMessage means 'not yet in CoreData', not 'newly produced'. v0.4.0 unified pull-subscribe and live-push into one envelope per docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md, but the unification didn't add a 'this is the catch-up window, don't speak it' gate.

FIX (this issue): subscribe-snapshot cursor 'liveFromSeq' persisted on CDBackendSession.

- Add liveFromSeq: Int64 to CDBackendSession (CoreData migration).
- Capture liveFromSeq once per app session per CDBackendSession from the FIRST is_complete:true session_history payload that lands AFTER subscribe was sent (using the existing incompleteChainCursors state to detect chain settle).
- In handleSessionHistoryPayload, only append to newAssistantTexts when wireMessage.seq >= session.liveFromSeq.
- liveFromSeq is reset on app launch so a fresh app start treats every cached session as 'historical until subscribe settles' — matches the user's expectation that voice only speaks messages produced AFTER they opened the session.
- Survives is_complete:false re-subscribe chain because we wait for is_complete:true before capturing.

Does NOT cover the related bug 'in-flight TTS keeps playing after session switch' (filed as tmux-untethered-0sz). That requires speak-time tagging + ActiveSessionManager observer — different layer.
close_reason: Implemented in this commit. CDBackendSession.liveFromSeq Int64 cursor captured from first session_history reply's nextSeq, reset on app launch (PersistenceController batch update) and on unsubscribe (SessionSyncManager.clearLiveFromSeq). handleSessionHistoryPayload gates newAssistantTexts on seq >= liveFromSeq, suppressing both initial catch-up and is_complete:false chain replies. Added 4 tests in SmartSpeakingTests covering catch-up suppression, live-push pass-through, chain suppression, and cursor reset.

title: Backend: connect handler missing 'connected' confirmation — iOS isAuthenticated stays false, subscribe never sends
description: Backend's connect handler in server.clj never sent the 'connected' confirmation message after authentication. iOS sets isAuthenticated=true only on receipt of 'connected' (VoiceCodeClient.swift:780-786), so the auth gate in subscribe() at line 1483 deferred every subscribe to .desired without sending. Prompts went out and were processed by the backend, but the iOS client wasn't subscribed, so session_history pushes and turn_complete never reached it — the user saw 'no response to my test message'. Fix mirrors commit 894bfa1e from the react-native branch which was never carried over to main/tmux-untethered.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit. Backend now sends connected confirmation per STANDARDS.md after authenticate-connect! succeeds.

title: iOS: subscribe wire send never fires; activeSubscriptions set lies about confirmation
description:
## Symptom

Across three device-log captures the user collected, iOS never sends `subscribe` over the wire — for new sessions OR resumed sessions. Outgoing messages are limited to `connect`, `unsubscribe`, `prompt`. Yet `unsubscribe` later fires for the same session id, proving `activeSubscriptions` is being populated locally without a wire send ever happening.

Backend's `session_history` push is gated on `(repl/is-subscribed? session-id)` and per-channel `:subscribed-sessions` set membership (replication.clj:1887, server.clj:1411). With no subscribe received, no broadcast goes out. Net effect: assistant replies land in the JSONL file on the host, but the iPhone never sees them.

## Root cause

`VoiceCodeClient.subscribe()` (line 1416) inserts into `activeSubscriptions` *before* `guard isAuthenticated`. If the call lands during the brief window between WebSocket open and `← connected` (e.g. ConversationView.onAppear racing the handshake on cold launch or reconnect), the set entry sticks but the wire send never fires. The set is now lying — it claims membership without confirmation.

Two recovery paths exist and both fail:

1. `restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect()` (line 1398, fired from `case "connected"` at line 771) re-iterates `activeSubscriptions` and calls `subscribe()`. This works ONCE, on the auth handshake immediately following the poisoned insert. But on a *resumed* session cold-launch where the user is already on ConversationView and the prior socket dropped silently, the flow is: onAppear fires → subscribe() inserts pre-auth → reconnect → connected → restore loops the set → subscribe() inserts again → wire send fires. Actually this case is fine. The failing case is when the user navigates to a session AFTER `connected` has already arrived but before the socket is fully ready, and there's no second `connected` to re-trigger restore.

2. `session_ready` handler (line 967) and `turn_complete` fallback (line 992) check `!activeSubscriptions.contains(sessionId)` before calling `subscribe()`. The contains-check returns true because the set was poisoned, the call is skipped, and these handlers — which are the *only* path that fires after the backend confirms the session exists — silently no-op.

ConversationView.swift:724 deliberately skips the first onAppear subscribe for new sessions (`isLocallyCreated && messageCount == 0`), relying on the session_ready handler to fire the subscribe. The contains-check guard makes this recovery silently fail.

## Fix (functional design)

Replace the single `activeSubscriptions: Set<String>` with a single source of truth that distinguishes intent from wire-confirmation:

```swift
private enum SubscriptionState { case desired; case confirmed }
private var subscriptions: [String: SubscriptionState] = [:]
```

- `subscribe(sessionId:)` always sets `subscriptions[sid] = .desired`, then sends the wire message and on send-success transitions to `.confirmed`. (Or simpler: `.desired` is set unconditionally, `.confirmed` is set only inside the `isAuthenticated` branch after `sendMessage` returns.)
- `unsubscribe(sessionId:)` sets `subscriptions[sid] = nil` (clears both).
- `restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect()` iterates entries and re-sends for any in `.desired` (post-reconnect everything reverts to desired and re-confirms on send).
- `session_ready` / `turn_complete` handlers always call `subscribe()` (no contains-guard) because `subscribe()` itself is now idempotent on `.confirmed` and re-fires the wire send if the entry is `.desired`.
- `unsubscribe` wire send fires regardless of state (defensive — backend tolerates unknown unsubscribe).

The invariant becomes checkable: every `.confirmed` entry has had a wire `subscribe` successfully sent. Pre-auth poisoning can no longer happen because the only thing pre-auth can produce is `.desired`, and the contains-check moves to `.confirmed`.

## Acceptance

- iOS sends `→ subscribe sess=X` exactly once per (re)attach to a session, on every reconnect, and within ~100ms of receiving `← session_ready` or `← turn_complete` for a session not yet `.confirmed`.
- Backend's `is-subscribed?` returns true within the same window.
- Verified: assistant replies arrive on iOS for both new sessions and resumed sessions, including across socket flaps.
- Regression test: assert `→ subscribe sess=X` is sent within N ms of `← session_ready` for `X` (or upon ` connected` for a session that was `.desired` pre-auth).

## Out of scope (file separately)

The first connection attempt going to `ws://100.71.95.127:8080` (legacy 0.2.0 server) and being rejected is its own bug. iOS is carrying a stale port URL — likely a Settings value or hardcoded fallback.

title: iOS: ConversationView messageCount>0 gate skips subscribe on resume
description: ConversationView.loadSessionIfNeeded gates client.subscribe() on session.messageCount > 0 (lines 720-725). For any session whose iOS-side messageCount is 0 at navigation time (sessions that arrived via session_list but were never opened, or whose CDBackendSession messageCount lags the backend), iOS skips the wire subscribe entirely. The user then sends a prompt (resume_session_id), backend writes the assistant reply to JSONL, but broadcast-session-history! is gated on per-channel :subscribed-sessions and silently skips this client. Same gate on turn_complete (server.clj:1504-1510). Net result: iPhone never sees any reply from the model. Confirmed in backend/server.out across multiple reconnect cycles: only connect/unsubscribe/prompt/unsubscribe wire messages, no subscribe ever. Fix: drop the messageCount gate. Backend v0.4.0 handles subscribe with last_seq=0 on any session id (returns empty session_history if nothing yet). The 'avoid session not found' rationale in the comment is from a pre-v0.4.0 protocol.
acceptance_criteria: iOS sends subscribe message when ConversationView appears for any session, regardless of local messageCount. Backend log shows 'Client subscribing to session' for resumed sessions. Assistant replies arrive on the iPhone.
close_reason: Fixed: ConversationView gate now skips subscribe only for locally-created sessions with no messages (the original 'no backend file yet' intent). Backend-known sessions with messageCount=0 now correctly subscribe. Added wire-trace logging on both sides (concise → / ← lines per message, silenced for pong/ping/command_output to preserve the iOS 15K-char copy buffer). Added 'fan-out skipped — no subscribers' INFO log to make this bug class visible from server logs alone. Tests: NewSessionSubscribeTests rewritten to pin the new gate; VoiceCodeClientWireSummaryTests new (15 tests pinning summarizer output and silencing).

title: iOS: isActiveSession read once before CoreData save — TTS dispatched with stale active-session snapshot
description: In SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload, ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) is read once at the top and cached as isActiveSession. The CoreData background save and all async work happen afterward. TTS is dispatched using this stale boolean. If the user switches sessions during the async gap (common with large saves), TTS fires for the session that was active at snapshot time, not the one currently showing. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift. Fix: re-check ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) immediately before calling voiceManager.speak(), as a second gate.

title: iOS: TTS fires for background sessions — activeSubscriptions not unsubscribed on session switch
description: When user switches from session A to session B, the client subscribes to B but never unsubscribes from A. Both sessions remain in activeSubscriptions indefinitely. When session A receives any message (e.g. a background command completes), handleSessionHistoryPayload fires for A. If isActiveSession was true at the snapshot moment, TTS speaks A's response while the user is looking at B. This is the primary driver of the reported wrong-session audio bug. Fix: when the active session changes, unsubscribe from the previous session and remove it from activeSubscriptions. Only the currently active session should remain subscribed.
close_reason: False positive: ConversationView.onDisappear (line 634-635) calls client.unsubscribe(sessionId:) when the user navigates away from a session, which removes it from activeSubscriptions. The claim that subscriptions are never cleared on session switch is incorrect — the standard back-navigation path triggers onDisappear and unsubscribes. The real TTS stale-snapshot bug is tracked in 7pp/gp5.

title: iOS: pruned-gap banner dismissal does not block subsequent session_history merges — stale messages mix with live data
description: When didDetectPrunedGap fires, the delegate callback is dispatched async. Before it runs, a new session_history push for the same session arrives and is merged normally. Result: old messages (seqs 1-50) and new post-gap messages (seqs 200+) are mixed in the store with no visual break. Design doc AC5 says no automatic merge should happen after a pruned gap, but there is no flag preventing it. Files: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift handleSessionHistoryPayload, ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift. Fix: add prunedSessions: Set<String> flag to SessionSyncManager; refuse to merge payloads for sessions in the set until flag is explicitly cleared.

title: iOS: multiple offline optimistic messages collide on seq=0 upsert key — one orphaned row per extra prompt
description: Optimistic user messages are inserted with seq=0. Upsert is keyed on (sessionId, seq). If the user submits two prompts before either echoes back, both create CDMessage rows with seq=0 in the same session. Backend echoes first prompt with seq=1; upsert fetches (S, seq=0).first and updates it. The second seq=0 row is orphaned permanently. Reproduction: dictate two commands rapidly before the first response arrives. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift, upsertMessage. Fix: assign a deterministic negative seq to optimistic messages (e.g. -abs(UUID.hashValue)) so multiple offline messages have distinct keys.

title: Backend: next-seq lost on crash — seq collision after restart if watcher re-parses migrated content
description: assign-seq! updates the in-memory session-index atom; save-index! flushes to disk debounced. Crash between these: disk has old next-seq. migrate-session-seqs! re-scans JSONL on restart and fixes next-seq, but if file-positions atom is not also restored the watcher starts from byte 0 and re-parses already-assigned messages, calling assign-seq! again and producing duplicate seqs (101-200 for messages that already had seqs 1-100). Fix: after migration, set file-positions for each session to the actual file length so the watcher skips already-parsed content.
close_reason: Test added in replication_test.clj covers crash-restart scenario; migrate-session-seqs! now restores file-positions for Claude/Copilot byte-cursor providers.

title: Backend: JSONL cursor not reset when file shrinks after compaction — all post-compaction messages silently skipped
description: parse-jsonl-incremental returns the old cursor position unchanged when current-size < last-pos (file shrank). When the file grows again from new appends, the cursor still points past the end and all new messages are skipped forever. Triggered by claude --compact rewriting the JSONL. File: backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj, parse-jsonl-incremental. Fix: reset cursor to 0 and log a warning when file is smaller than tracked position.
close_reason: Test added for cursor reset on JSONL shrink (4ug); same change fixes the underlying bug (df2) by detecting current-size<tracked-pos and resetting last-pos to 0 with a warning log. Filed tmux-untethered-d9j for the same bug class in parse-copilot-events-incremental.

title: Backend: next-seq in broadcast payload captured after concurrent assign-seq! — creates false apparent gap
description: broadcast-session-history! reads next-seq from session metadata after computing last-seq from the messages vector. A concurrent assign-seq! call between these two reads can advance next-seq beyond last-seq of the current broadcast, making the payload look like {:last_seq 55 :next_seq 61} — a 5-seq apparent gap. All subscribers immediately resubscribe to backfill. Fix: snapshot next-seq atomically with the message vector before building the broadcast payload using (get-in @session-index [session-id :next-seq]).

title: Backend: build-session-history-response packed branch produces nil first-seq/last-seq when no messages fit budget
description: When pack-within-budget returns an empty :included vector (single message larger than budget, client not yet caught up), the packed branch calls (first []) and (last []) which both return nil. Response has nil first-seq and last-seq with is-complete:false — client cannot advance cursor and enters infinite resubscribe loop. Fix: guard with (if (empty? included) return explicit nil-seq map, else normal path).

title: Fix: ConversationView loading spinner persists 5s after messages arrive
description: onChange(of: messages.count) that sets isLoading=false is a modifier on the List, which is only rendered when isLoading==false. When messages arrive from the backend while the spinner is showing, the @FetchRequest updates but the List (and its onChange) is not in the view hierarchy, so isLoading is never cleared until the 5-second timeout fires. Fix: add onChange(of: messages.count) at the outer VStack level so it fires regardless of which branch is displayed.

title: Fix: command_error leaves executeCommand caller hung indefinitely
description: When the backend sends a command_error in response to execute_command, the CheckedContinuation stored in commandExecutionContinuations[commandId] is never resumed. The caller's Task (CommandMenuView.executeCommand) awaits forever and the UI never navigates to the command execution view. Additionally, disconnect() does not drain pending continuations, so any in-flight executeCommand hangs after a reconnect cycle.\n\nFix: resume the continuation with nil in the command_error handler; drain all continuations with nil in disconnect(); change the return type of executeCommand to String? and guard against nil in CommandMenuView before navigating.

title: CDMessage.displayText cache keyed by text.count causes stale display on same-length text change
description: CDMessage.displayText has a cache keyed by text.count (character count). If the text property changes to a different string of the same character count, the cached (wrong) display text is returned. This can happen during optimistic reconciliation (upsertMessage sets message.text = wireMessage.text on existing rows) or after a file_replaced recovery where messages are re-read. The cache key should be content-based, not length-based.

title: v0.5.0 optimistic message offset=0 collides with first confirmed message
description:
In createOptimisticMessage (SessionSyncManager.swift:1288), message.seq is set to a negative sentinel via optimisticSeq(for:) to prevent v0.4.0 seq=0 collisions, but message.offset is not set and defaults to 0 (the NSManaged Int64 default).

In v0.5.0, confirmed messages are keyed by (sessionId, offset). The first confirmed message in every session has offset=0. When a session_history payload arrives, upsertMessage(WireMessageV5) fetches ANY message with sessionId + offset == wireMessage.offset. If wireMessage.offset == 0 (the first historical message) and there is also a new optimistic message (offset=0 default), the lookup may return the optimistic row, overwrite its role/text/status with historical content, and lose the user's pending prompt from the display.

Fix: set message.offset = Self.optimisticSeq(for: messageId) in createOptimisticMessage alongside message.seq, so v0.5.0 offset lookups never match optimistic rows. The role+text+status=sending optimistic fallback path is unaffected.
close_reason: Fixed: set message.offset = Self.optimisticSeq(for: messageId) in createOptimisticMessage. Three new tests verify negative offset on creation, distinct offsets, and end-to-end no-collision with offset=0 confirmed message.

title: Fix messageCount not updated after pruning in SessionSyncManager
description: SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload (both v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 paths) sets session.messageCount before pruning, then prunes without updating the count. SessionsView shows the stale inflated count. Fix: after pruneOldMessages returns deletedCount > 0, subtract from session.messageCount before the second save.

title: Fix duplicate case 'error' dead code in VoiceCodeClient.handleMessage
description: VoiceCodeClient.swift has two case 'error': labels in the same switch statement. The second handler at line 1291 is dead code — Swift executes the first match only. The dead handler was intended to suppress the error banner for file upload failures and let ResourcesManager timeout instead. Currently, file upload errors incorrectly set currentError, showing an error banner in the conversation view.

title: sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe uses subscribe (no-op when confirmed) — large sessions load truncated history
description:
## Problem

sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe fires when: (1) a gap is detected, (2) is_complete=false chain, (3) save-failure recovery. It calls subscribe(sessionId:), which is a no-op when subscriptions[sessionId] == .confirmed (the idempotency guard). Since the subscription is always .confirmed by the time the chain fires, no second subscribe wire is sent.

Result: sessions whose history exceeds the server's 100KB byte budget (large code sessions) show only the first batch. The remaining historical messages are never requested.

## Expected Behavior

sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe should call refreshSubscription (which demotes .confirmed → .desired before calling subscribe) so a fresh subscribe wire is sent with the updated cursor.

## Root Cause

VoiceCodeClient.sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe calls subscribe(sessionId:) instead of refreshSubscription(sessionId:). subscribe is idempotent on .confirmed — correct for duplicate UX calls, wrong for a chain step.

## v0.5.0 Companion Bug

The v0.5.0 handleSessionHistoryPayload handler has no chaining at all for endOfFile=false. Large sessions in v0.5.0 mode never get subsequent batches.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe sends a new subscribe wire when called while .confirmed
2. v0.5.0 handler chains when endOfFile=false (dispatches sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe on main)
3. Tests verify both behaviors
close_reason: Fixed: refreshSubscription instead of subscribe in sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe, added incompleteChainCursorsV5 stall guard, and v0.5.0 end_of_file:false chaining. Tests cover all three paths.

title: iOS: in-flight TTS keeps playing after session switch
description:
When session A is actively speaking an assistant message via TTS and the user switches to session B, the AVSpeechSynthesizer utterance keeps playing until natural completion (or until session B coincidentally produces a new assistant message that triggers self-preempt at VoiceOutputManager.swift:148).

ROOT CAUSE: TTS dispatch is decoupled from user attention. There is no observer on ActiveSessionManager.activeSessionId that calls voiceOutput.stop() when the active session changes. ConversationView.onDisappear (ConversationView.swift:630-640) clears ActiveSessionManager and unsubscribes but does NOT call voiceOutput.stop(). The only auto-cancel path is the line-148 self-preempt inside speakWithVoice — fires only when a NEW message triggers speak.

WHY FIX A (liveFromSeq enqueue-time gate, tmux-untethered-cr8) DOES NOT COVER THIS: Fix A is enqueue-time. By the time the user switches sessions, the utterance has already been correctly enqueued (it was a fresh live message in session A). AVSpeechSynthesizer doesn't re-evaluate; once speak(utterance) is queued, only stopSpeaking(at:) interrupts it. liveFromSeq has nothing to filter at this point — it's already speech-in-flight, not a queued enqueue.

PROPOSED FIX: Two-part change.

1. Tag each speak(...) call with the originating session UUID. Store it on VoiceOutputManager (e.g. inFlightSessionId: UUID?). Set on enqueue, clear in didFinish/didCancel delegate callbacks.

2. Add an observer on ActiveSessionManager.$activeSessionId. When activeSessionId changes (or becomes nil), if inFlightSessionId != nil and inFlightSessionId != activeSessionId, call synthesizer.stopSpeaking(.immediate).

Either VoiceOutputManager subscribes to ActiveSessionManager (using Combine $activeSessionId.sink), or a small coordinator object holds the wire. The Combine sink runs on whatever queue the publisher emits on; setActiveSession is called from main thread (ConversationView.onAppear/onDisappear), so the sink fires on main and synthesizer.stopSpeaking can be called inline.

ALTERNATIVE (rejected): hook ConversationView.onDisappear to call voiceOutput.stop() directly. Simpler but coupled to view lifecycle and fragile under navigation gestures, multi-window on iPad, or any future code path that flips activeSessionId without a view transition.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Manual: with TTS playing in session A, navigate to session B → speech stops within ~100ms.
- Manual: with TTS playing in session A, navigate to home (clearActiveSession) → speech stops.
- Unit test: VoiceOutputManager exposes inFlightSessionId; setActiveSession to a different UUID triggers stopSpeaking; setActiveSession to the same UUID does not.

DEPENDENCY: tmux-untethered-cr8 lands the enqueue-time gate first; this issue layers in-flight cancellation on top.

title: TTS: investigate cross-session speech leak after restart + open unrelated session
description:
User report (tmux-untethered branch): "Right now, I hear active messages being read even if I'm not in the session related to those messages. I tried restarting it and then clicked on an unrelated session. I'm still hearing the messages from another active, unrelated session."

## Diagnosis

Root cause is the protocol v0.4.0 migration in commit 287581b9, which introduced SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload as the unified merge path for both initial subscribe replies and live pushes. The new path was added without the isActiveSession-guarded auto-speak / unread-count / priority-queue / pruning / notification fan-out logic that previously lived in handleSessionUpdated. With backend/resources/config.edn at :message-stream-version :v0.4.0, all messages now flow through the new path. The legacy handleSessionUpdated handler is still in the file but is unreachable.

There is an uncommitted working-tree fix to SessionSyncManager.swift that ports the missing logic across (isActiveSession snapshot at top of handler, async dispatch to main queue, speak only if snapshot was true). That fix mirrors what already-filed tmux-untethered-7pp / gp5 describe.

## Why the existing tasks may not fully cover this

- tmux-untethered-7pp (P0) and tmux-untethered-gp5 (P1) are near-duplicates. Both describe the stale isActiveSession snapshot race: snapshot taken at top of handleSessionHistoryPayload, CoreData saves async, voiceManager.speak() fires on main queue, and the user can switch sessions in the gap. Their stated fix (re-check ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) immediately before voiceManager.speak()) covers the in-session-switch race.
- That fix does NOT cleanly explain the user's reported scenario. On cold launch, activeSessionId starts nil; opening session B sets it to B; a payload arriving for session A should snapshot false and not speak. So either:
  1. Something restores or mis-sets activeSessionId to A on launch, or
  2. The snapshot is taken before B's ConversationView.onAppear -> setActiveSession runs, racing the first watcher tick after reconnect resubscribes to all activeSubscriptions, or
  3. There is a session-id mapping mismatch — handleSessionHistoryPayload translates the payload's provider/Claude session_id to an iOS CoreData UUID, and a stale or duplicate mapping could make A's payload resolve to B's UUID (or vice versa), making isActive return true for the wrong session.
- tmux-untethered-tj1 (P1) auto-subscribes on session_ready regardless of foreground state. Relevant to subscription noise but not directly to TTS — subscriptions do not speak on their own; speech is gated by isActive.
- tmux-untethered-n54 (P1) is unrelated (subscribe-before-connect drop).

## Recommended follow-ups (revisit when picking this up)

1. Consolidate 7pp + gp5. Close one as superseded; they describe the same bug.
2. Investigate post-restart active-session resolution. Trace the payload.session_id -> CoreData UUID lookup in handleSessionHistoryPayload after a cold launch. Is the mapping picking the wrong session (stale claudeSessionId shared between sessions, first-match lookup, etc.)?
3. Defense-in-depth speak gate. Require BOTH activeSubscriptions.contains(payload.sessionId) AND isActive(sessionUUID) at speak-dispatch time on the main queue, with sessionUUID re-resolved at that point (not snapshot from earlier). Subsumes 7pp.
4. Audit backend broadcast-session-history! (server.clj:1235) — it filters @connected-clients only by is-session-deleted-for-client?, not by per-channel subscription. is-subscribed? is a global session-level set in replication.clj (~:1580). Per-channel gating is comment-claimed but not enforced; clients receive pushes for sessions they never asked for, relying on the iOS isActive gate alone for TTS suppression.

## Files / line refs at time of writing

- ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift handleSessionHistoryPayload around line 311 (committed) / 345-451 (uncommitted partial fix)
- ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift handleSessionUpdated around 664-862 (legacy v0.3.0, unreachable)
- ios/VoiceCode/Managers/ActiveSessionManager.swift singleton, in-memory only
- ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift setActiveSession at ~689, clearActiveSession at ~632
- ios/VoiceCode/Managers/NotificationManager.swift postResponseNotification does NOT auto-speak (confirmed)
- backend/src/voice_code/server.clj:1235 broadcast-session-history!
- backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj:~1580 is-subscribed?
- backend/resources/config.edn :message-stream-version :v0.4.0

## State caveat

Filed 2026-04-29 while the beads backlog is being actively worked. By the time this is picked up, 7pp / gp5 / tj1 / n54 may have been closed, renamed, or superseded. Re-check bd status of those IDs before acting on the consolidation recommendation, and re-read the SessionSyncManager files — the uncommitted working-tree fix may already be merged or replaced.
notes: Backend fix landed: broadcast-session-history! now gates by per-channel :subscribed-sessions so a watcher tick on session A no longer pushes a session_history payload to a channel that only subscribed to session B. Tests added in server_test.clj cover both v0.4.0 and v0.3.0-rollback paths. Closed gp5 as duplicate of (already-closed) 7pp. Items still open from the recommended follow-ups: deeper investigation of post-restart active-session resolution (item 2) — left as future work since the per-channel gate eliminates the most plausible mechanism for cross-session push delivery.
close_reason: Backend per-channel subscription gating added to broadcast-session-history!. Push body (session_history / session_updated) now goes only to clients with this session in :subscribed-sessions; recent_sessions still fans out to all non-deleted clients so the Recent panel keeps refreshing for cross-session activity. Closed gp5 as duplicate of (already-closed) 7pp. Filed tmux-untethered-44u for the same bug shape in on-session-created.

title: iOS: auto-subscribe on session_ready adds to activeSubscriptions regardless of whether session is still foreground
description: When session_ready arrives, the client calls subscribe(sessionId:) which always inserts the session into activeSubscriptions. If the user has already navigated away from that session before session_ready fires (race between session creation and navigation), the session still gets added and will receive future pushes and potentially trigger TTS. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift, session_ready handler. Fix: only auto-subscribe on session_ready if the session matches the currently active session in ActiveSessionManager.
close_reason: Fixed: session_ready handler now gates auto-subscribe on ActiveSessionManager.isActive(). Filed follow-up tmux-untethered-b6v for the same race in turn_complete fallback.

title: iOS: CoreData v4 seq attribute may lack standalone index — maxCachedSeq queries degrade on large sessions
description: The v4 model defines a composite fetch index bySessionAndSeq (sessionId ASC, seq DESC) but the seq attribute itself may lack indexed=YES. On sessions with 50k+ messages, maxCachedSeq sort-and-limit queries may not use the composite index efficiently, causing multi-second cursor lookups on reconnect. File: ios/VoiceCode/VoiceCode.xcdatamodeld/VoiceCode 4.xcdatamodel/contents. Fix: verify the xcdatamodel XML; if seq lacks indexed=YES add it and create a v5 lightweight migration.
close_reason: False positive: the bySessionAndSeq composite fetch index on (sessionId ASC, seq DESC) in VoiceCode 4.xcdatamodel/contents is the correct and sufficient index for the maxCachedSeq query (predicate: sessionId == X, sort: seq DESC, fetchLimit: 1). CoreData NSFetchIndex entries create actual SQLite B-tree indexes; the composite index covers this exact query pattern efficiently. The indexed=YES attribute flag is an older mechanism and is not needed when a fetch index already covers the column.

title: iOS: session_history decode failure resumes refresh continuation as success — caller proceeds with stale data
description: If SessionHistoryPayload decoding fails, the error is logged but sessionRefreshContinuations[sessionId]?.resume() is still called unconditionally. The awaiting caller has no indication that the refresh failed and proceeds with stale state. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift, session_history message handler. Fix: only resume the continuation inside the successful decode path; let the 5-second timeout fire on failure.

title: iOS: isActiveSession snapshot stale before TTS dispatch — TTS fires for wrong session after user switches
description: In handleSessionHistoryPayload, ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) is read once at the top and stored as isActiveSession. CoreData save and async work happen afterward. TTS is dispatched using the stale boolean. The user can switch sessions during the async gap but TTS still fires for the old session. This is the direct mechanism for hearing messages from a session you are not viewing. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift. Fix: re-check ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) immediately before calling voiceManager.speak(), not before the save.
close_reason: Duplicate of tmux-untethered-7pp (closed). Both describe the same isActiveSession snapshot/race; 7pp's fix (re-check on main thread before voiceManager.speak()) is committed in 4e8d8923 and covers gp5 verbatim.

title: iOS: is_complete:false resubscribe chain has no loop guard — infinite resubscribe if server keeps returning false
description: When isComplete==false, sessionSyncNeedsResubscribe is fired with no rate limit, retry cap, or progress check. If the server keeps returning is_complete:false for the same cursor (e.g. a single message larger than the budget), the client and server spin at maximum speed. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift, handleSessionHistoryPayload. Fix: track cursor from the last is_complete:false response; if the next response carries the same nextSeq without advancing, abort the chain and surface an error.

title: iOS: activeSubscriptions never cleared on session switch — background sessions trigger TTS
description: When the user switches from session A to session B, the client subscribes to B but never unsubscribes from A. Both stay in activeSubscriptions. Any message arriving for session A triggers handleSessionHistoryPayload, which may fire TTS if isActiveSession was true at snapshot time. This is the primary cause of hearing TTS from a session that is not currently open. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift. Fix: unsubscribe from the previous active session when switching, and remove it from activeSubscriptions.
close_reason: False positive: duplicate of 3be. ConversationView.onDisappear (line 634-635) calls client.unsubscribe(sessionId:) when navigating away, removing the session from activeSubscriptions. The claim that activeSubscriptions is never cleared on session switch is incorrect for the standard navigation path.

title: iOS: subscribe() before connect() silently drops the subscribe message
description: sendMessage uses optional chaining (webSocket?.send(...)). If the WebSocket has not been created yet, the send is a no-op with no error or retry. Callers that subscribe before connect() completes or before hello is received silently lose the subscription. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift, subscribe and sendMessage. Fix: guard on isConnected in subscribe(). If not connected, the session ID is still added to activeSubscriptions so restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect picks it up.
close_reason: Fixed: subscribe() now defers wire send when isAuthenticated=false, buffering session in activeSubscriptions for reconnect-time restoration. Added isAuthenticated reset on disconnect/socket-failure to keep auth state socket-scoped.

title: iOS: newestCachedSeq reads stale viewContext before merge — subscribe sends incorrect last_seq cursor
description: newestCachedSeq fetches from viewContext. Background saves from SessionSyncManager post NSManagedObjectContextDidSave which merges into viewContext on the main queue. If subscribe() is called in the same run-loop cycle as the notification, the merge has not yet been applied and the client reads a stale seq (e.g. 42 instead of 99), sending last_seq=42. Backend never resends messages 43-99. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift, newestCachedSeq. Fix: use newBackgroundContext() with performAndWait to read the latest persistent store state.

title: Backend: failed channel sends swallowed — dead channels accumulate as zombie subscribers
description: When http/send! throws (network failure, closed socket), the exception is caught, logged, and swallowed. The channel is not removed from connected-clients or unsubscribed from sessions. broadcast-session-history! iterates all subscribed channels on every message. With enough churn the set fills with dead channels, making each broadcast progressively slower and leaking memory. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj, send-to-client!. Fix: on send failure invoke the same cleanup logic used by the WebSocket close handler to remove the channel from all subscriptions.
close_reason: Added test-cleanup-on-failed-channel-send (13 assertions) and the underlying fix in send-to-client! / broadcast-to-all-clients! that invokes unregister-channel! on send failure. Both tasks resolved together since the test requires the fix to pass.

title: Backend: assign-seq! creates incomplete stub index entries — downstream code receives nil for provider/file/name
description: When assign-seq! is called for a session not yet in the session index (race between watcher and ensure-session-in-index!), it creates a stub entry with only :session-id, :min-available-seq, and :next-seq. Downstream code that reads :provider, :file, or :name receives nil. The stub is persisted via save-index! creating an incomplete on-disk record that survives restarts. File: backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj, assign-seq!. Fix: throw instead of creating a stub when the session is absent, requiring callers to call ensure-session-in-index! first.

title: Backend: pack-within-budget 200-byte overhead constant underestimates gap response envelopes
description: The fixed 200-byte overhead in pack-within-budget does not account for long session IDs (more than 50 chars add 50+ bytes) or gap objects (~80 bytes). For a pruned-gap response with a long session ID, actual envelope is 250-280 bytes. pack-within-budget may include one extra message, sending a response that exceeds the client max_message_size. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj, pack-within-budget. Fix: compute envelope overhead dynamically by measuring a skeleton envelope, or raise the constant to 300.

title: Backend: min-available-seq hardcoded to 1 in subscribe handler — pruned-gap detection never fires after compaction
description: The subscribe handler passes min-available-seq=1 literally to build-session-history-response regardless of what the session index contains. If messages were pruned after compaction (e.g. seqs 1-150 removed), the pruned-gap check always evaluates false. Clients requesting from last_seq=0 receive seqs 151-200 with gap=nil, silently missing 150 messages. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj. Fix: read :min-available-seq from the session index entry instead of hardcoding 1.

title: iOS: prevent silent message drops on background save constraint conflict
description: handleSessionHistoryPayload silently dropped wire messages when CoreData throws on (CDMessage.id) uniqueness constraint conflicts. Background contexts inherited NSErrorMergePolicyType (only viewContext had NSMergeByPropertyObjectTrumpMergePolicy), so any id collision aborted the save and the catch block returned without posting sessionHistoryDidUpdate, advancing local_last_seq, or firing the gap-backfill chain. Restart didn't help because resubscribe replayed the same conflicting payload. Fixed by setting merge policy on background contexts, surfacing the actual error via privacy:.public logging, and triggering recovery resubscribe from the catch block. Regression test asserts wire row survives a wire UUID collision with an existing row.

title: iOS: route session_history through v0.4.0 payload handler; restore auto-speak
description: VoiceCodeClient.swift routes session_history through the legacy handleSessionHistory(sessionId:messages:) which never sets message.seq and doesn't chain on is_complete:false; handleSessionHistoryPayload is dead code. Auto-speak is wired only to handleSessionUpdated which the v0.4.0 backend no longer sends. Fix: wire session_history to the payload handler, port auto-speak, drop session_updated dispatch, add tests.
close_reason: Routed session_history through handleSessionHistoryPayload; ported auto-speak/unread/priority-queue/notifications into the payload handler; added optimistic-message reconciliation to upsertMessage. Full iOS suite passes.

title: Fix hang on new-session creation when wait-for-ready times out
description: New sessions in cold directories (e.g. ~/assist) hang forever because tmux/wait-for-ready has a 3s default timeout; when Claude TUI takes longer to render its 'bypass permissions' banner, start-window! silently drops the initial prompt. Fix: bump default timeout to something generous (e.g. 20s), and throw on timeout so the existing catch-Exception path in the prompt handler sends a {type: error, session_id} envelope to iOS to unlock the session.

title: Recipe callback reads stale assistant text on spurious turn-complete
description: Under tmux-untethered, on-turn-complete is driven by JSONL file observations. If a non-turn file event (e.g. user interrupt, permission-mode marker) fires on-turn-complete after a new recipe step is dispatched but before the provider has written a fresh assistant response, read-last-assistant-text returns the PRIOR step's response. The orchestrator then validates the prior step's outcome against the NEW step's expected outcome set, producing a false outcome-mismatch. Seen in session 149055f3-38e3-4518-881a-0155808fd500 where a :review response ({outcome: issues-found}) was re-parsed as a :fix response, yielding the error 'Outcome issues-found not in expected outcomes: #{other complete}'. Fix: capture the last-assistant UUID at dispatch time, store it with the callback in recipe-turn-callbacks, and only fire the callback when a strictly-newer assistant message exists. Also fix the contradictory retry prompt opener in orchestration/get-outcome-reminder-prompt.

title: Dismiss claude --resume confirmation dialog in wait-for-ready
description: First tmux-untethered test of a stale/large session: respawning with 'claude --resume <uuid>' on an old session (days old, >100k tokens) opens an interactive confirmation dialog ('Resume from summary / Resume full session as-is / Don't ask me again') that the readiness-predicate does not match. wait-for-ready then times out, start-window! skips nudge!, and the user's prompt is silently dropped. Fix: inside wait-for-ready, when pane contents show the dialog, send '3' + Enter ('Don't ask me again') once, then continue polling for 'bypass permissions'. Observed in production: session 36895c49-fb43-4ef5-b185-a3fb06839fa5, backend/server.out line 50755.
close_reason: Fix shipped in 5a2e3d16; verified end-to-end with two old/large sessions

title: Add full-factory end-to-end WebSocket round-trip integration test
description: Strengthen AC #10 coverage by writing an in-process test that boots ReplServerFactory.make with a real in-memory DependencyContainer, opens a URLSessionWebSocketTask client, and drives the full journal-entry-round-trip scenario from design doc spec 407 over the wire.
acceptance_criteria: Test file exists at hunt910Tests/Testing/ReplServer/ with scenario: subscribe -> CreateHuntArea -> HuntAreaCreatedEvent push -> CreateWaypoint -> WaypointCreatedEvent push -> AssignWaypointToArea -> projection visible via WaypointsByArea -> LogSighting -> SightingLoggedEvent push -> DeleteWaypoint -> projection gone -> unsubscribe -> meta/list-subscriptions empty. Test passes against real use cases and real event fanout (no stubs).
notes: Landed round-trip coverage: ReplServerRoundTripIntegrationTests.swift has test_journalEntryRoundTrip_factoryStackOverWebSocket (full scenario over live WebSocket against ReplServerFactory.make on in-memory DependencyContainer) + test_metaListCommands_overWebSocket_matchesFactoryRegistry (locks AC #8 catalog parity). All green; ReplServerIntegrationTests/ReplServerFactoryTests also still pass.
close_reason: AC #10 in-process round-trip test landed; verification gap closed

title: REPL harness slice 4f: register AllSightings + SightingById queries
description: Adds symmetric read-side support for sightings: LogSighting command already exists in the harness, but agents cannot query sightings back out. This slice adds AllSightings and SightingById queries to ReplServerFactory, schemas, DTOs, and tests. Also registers SightingProjection + WeatherSnapshotProjection in the test-container schema so LogSighting->AllSightings round-trips work in tests.
notes: Implemented slice 4f: AllSightings + SightingById queries registered in ReplServerFactory with matching schemas/DTOs. Added SightingProjection + WeatherSnapshotProjection to DependencyContainer.test() schema so LogSighting->AllSightings round-trips verify in tests. 5 new tests in ReplServerFactoryTests cover empty list, LogSighting->AllSightings correlation, hit, miss-returns-null, and malformed-UUID rejection. All 38 factory tests plus ReplServerTests, ReplServerIntegrationTests, and Clojure harness tests pass.

title: REPL harness slice 4e: register LogSighting command + event
description: Extend the agent REPL testing harness (spec 407) with the LogSighting command, output DTO, sightingLoggedPayload schema, SightingLoggedEvent in the event catalog, and integration tests. Expands the agent surface toward the spec's E2E journal-entry-round-trip scenario (AC#10 step 5). DEBUG-only; Release builds unaffected.
notes: Slice 4e complete. Registered LogSighting command in the REPL command registry with LogSightingInput/LogSightingWeatherInput DTOs (decodes enum fields with readable ReplError on bad values; applies LogSightingCommand defaults; supplies an estimated-weather snapshot when 'weather' is omitted). Added LogSightingOutput (sightingId, loggedAt). Added .logSighting/.logSightingResult/.sightingLoggedPayload JSON schemas to Schemas.swift plus six reusable enum raw-value helpers (species/observationType/animalBehavior/confidenceLevel/visibilityCondition/huntingPressure). Registered SightingLoggedEvent in the eventCatalog so the fanout advertises the sighting wire shape. Handler unwraps LogSightingUseCaseError.eventStoreFailed(LogSightingCommandError) so domain validation failures surface as validation_error instead of command_failed. Added 5 new ReplServerFactoryTests: tracks-sighting success (no animalCount), visual-sighting success (with count+behavior+weather), unknown-species rejection, visual-without-animalCount rejection, and event-emission via CapturingObserver. Updated catalog set-equality tests. All 33 ReplServerFactoryTests plus ReplServerIntegrationTests, EventFanoutTests, ReplServerTests, DependencyContainerObserverTests pass. make build green.

title: Bootstrap Clojure harness (tools/harness/)
description: Implement the Clojure-side REPL harness for slice 5a of the agent REPL testing harness (spec 407). Create tools/harness/ with deps.edn, hunt910.harness.protocol (pure envelope logic), hunt910.harness.core (connect/command/query/subscribe/events/await-event using java.net.http.WebSocket), hermetic tests, and a make test-harness target. This unblocks AC #3 (Clojure connect in under 500ms) and all subsequent AC that depend on a working client.
notes: Bootstrapped tools/harness/ with pure protocol layer (protocol.clj), connection/state layer (core.clj) using java.net.http.WebSocket, and hermetic clojure.test suite (25 tests, 63 assertions passing). Added make test-harness target. deps.edn uses only cached deps: clojure 1.12.0 + cheshire 5.13.0 + core.async 1.6.681. See tools/harness/README.md for quick-start.

title: REPL harness slice 4a: ReplServerFactory + app init hook
description: Wire ReplServerFactory that registers real use cases against DependencyContainer. Populate meta/list-events catalog with waypoint event descriptors. Gate server startup in hunt910App.init behind HUNT910_REPL env var. Unlocks AC #1 #4 #5 #6 #8 from 407-agent-repl-testing-harness.md
close_reason: Landed ReplServerFactory + Schemas.swift + app-init env-gate + 10 factory tests (all green). Commands: CreateWaypoint, DeleteWaypoint. Queries: AllWaypoints, WaypointById. Event catalog: WaypointCreatedEvent, WaypointUpdatedEvent, WaypointDeletedEvent. Observer seam: container.registerEventObserver(fanout). App startup: HUNT910_REPL=1 + optional HUNT910_REPL_PORT. Also propagated thrown ReplError through CommandRegistry and QueryRegistry so handlers can emit specific error codes.

title: Wire EventObserver into SwiftDataEventStore (REPL harness slice 2b)
description: Connect the EventFanout/EventObserver seam (slice 2a) to SwiftDataEventStore.append() so successfully persisted DomainEvents are forwarded to a DEBUG-only observer. Add registerEventObserver on DependencyContainer. Cover with integration tests. Spec: specs/400-testing/407-agent-repl-testing-harness.md (slice 2b / AC #4, #5).
close_reason: Slice 2b complete: SwiftDataEventStore now forwards persisted events to DEBUG-only weak EventObserver; DependencyContainer.registerEventObserver wired. 11 new integration tests pass (8 SwiftDataEventStoreObserverTests + 3 DependencyContainerObserverTests). make build succeeds.

title: Add EventObserver protocol and EventFanout class (REPL harness slice 2a)
description:
Second slice of the agent REPL testing harness (spec: specs/400-testing/407-agent-repl-testing-harness.md). Adds EventObserver.swift under hunt910/Testing/ReplServer/ with:
- @MainActor protocol EventObserver
- @MainActor final class EventFanout conforming to EventObserver, with subscribe(_:)->UUID, unsubscribe(UUID), didAppend(event:), subscriptionCount.

All code #if DEBUG gated. Zero integration surface — no changes to SwiftDataEventStore, DependencyContainer, or any use case. Ships with EventFanoutTests (9 tests). Slice 2b (next turn) wires the observer into SwiftDataEventStore under DEBUG and adds DependencyContainer.registerEventObserver.
close_reason: Slice 2a landed: hunt910/Testing/ReplServer/EventObserver.swift + 9 passing tests (EventFanoutTests). Zero integration surface — no changes to SwiftDataEventStore or DependencyContainer. Full make test suite green.

title: Implement ReplServer protocol types and command/query registries (slice 1)
description: Foundation slice of the agent REPL testing harness (spec: specs/400-testing/407-agent-repl-testing-harness.md). Lands Protocol.swift (envelope types), JSONValue.swift (carrier + JSONSchema), CommandRegistry.swift, QueryRegistry.swift under hunt910/Testing/ReplServer/ all #if DEBUG gated. Ships with ReplEnvelopeCodingTests, CommandRegistryTests, QueryRegistryTests. No networking, no app integration. Sets up the surface for later slices (EventObserver seam, WebSocketTransport, ReplServer, UICoordinator, ReplServerFactory, Clojure harness).

Known divergence from design: JSONSchema.items dropped for now because Swift structs cannot recursively contain themselves; needs boxing via class wrapper when first array-typed schema is added. Next slices: EventObserver seam on SwiftDataEventStore + EventFanout; WebSocketTransport; ReplServer + UICoordinator; ReplServerFactory wiring; Clojure harness. ReplError added Error conformance for Result<JSONValue, ReplError>.
close_reason: Slice 1 landed in 6420043: 4 source files + 4 test files + 48 passing tests + design doc updated (JSONSchema.items deferral).

title: Resolve Cursor turn-complete marker (design §3.5 ambiguity)
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.5, third bullet (Cursor). Also `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` §5.3.

## Context
Design §3.5 explicitly states the original Cursor turn-complete assumption was wrong (verified 2026-04-18). It offers two unresolved alternatives:
1. File-mtime debounce (>2s stable after last write) — only valid if agent-transcripts are written in TUI mode (unverified).
2. `--print --output-format stream-json` with `type:"result"` signal — reliable but means Cursor doesn't run as a TUI under tmux (would deviate from the rest of the design).

Until this is resolved, tmux-untethered-44v (per-provider turn-complete detection) cannot fully land for Cursor.

## Requirements
- [ ] Run `cursor-agent` interactively (not under tmux yet — just at a terminal) and capture: do agent-transcripts files appear and grow during a TUI session, or only when invoked with `--print`?
- [ ] If TUI writes transcripts: prototype the file-mtime debounce approach. Document threshold (2s? 5s?) chosen and why.
- [ ] If TUI does not write transcripts: document the implication — Cursor either uses `--print` (one-shot, breaks the mid-session-steering goal for Cursor specifically) or we need a different approach (e.g., scrape pane output for a known terminal-state string).
- [ ] Update design doc §3.5 with the resolved approach.
- [ ] Update `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` §5.3 with verified marker + sample file fragment.

## Technical Approach
- This is a design / investigation task, not an implementation task. The output is a documented decision in the design doc, not Clojure code.
- Files to modify:
  - `docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md` (resolve §3.5).
  - `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` (§5.3).
- Dependencies: none — pure investigation. Blocks tmux-untethered-44v.

## Verification
- [ ] Design doc §3.5 Cursor bullet rewritten with a single concrete approach.
- [ ] Sample JSONL or other-format fragment in provider-cli-reference.md showing the marker.
- [ ] Sanity-check the chosen approach against a real Cursor session (run the sample, observe the marker fires).

## Acceptance Criteria
Cursor approach in design doc is unambiguous; tmux-untethered-44v can implement it without further investigation.

## Parallelization
Independent. Blocks tmux-untethered-44v.
close_reason: Resolved §3.5 Cursor turn-complete ambiguity: mtime-debounce approach with 3s threshold and last-record role:assistant check. TUI write path source-verified from cursor-agent@2026.02.13-41ac335. Docs updated in both design plan (§3.5) and provider-cli-reference (§5.3 with edge cases, version note, and sample fragment). Live-runtime sanity-check tracked as tmux-untethered-58q (informs, does not block 44v).

title: Tmux-untethered provider invocation
description:
## Design Document
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md

## Overview
Move provider CLI invocation (Claude, Copilot, Cursor, OpenCode) from one-shot non-interactive subprocesses (`--print --output-format json`) to interactive sessions running inside tmux windows. Prompts are delivered as tmux `send-keys` nudges into a live pane, structured messages are still read from the provider session JSONL file (the source of truth), and provider lifecycle is decoupled from backend lifecycle so backend restarts no longer kill in-flight turns.

The foundational tmux machinery (`backend/src/voice_code/tmux.clj` — `start-window!`, `deliver!`, `evict-if-needed!`, `sweep!`, `scan-existing-windows!`, `*tmux-invoker*`, `live-windows`) and `providers/cli-path` are already shipped (commits `tmux-untethered-9hi.1` through `.7`, `.9`, `.14`). Backend startup wires `scan-existing-windows!` and the sweeper.

What this epic delivers is the **wiring** from the WebSocket protocol layer into that machinery, plus the protocol changes the design specifies. Today `tmux/deliver!` has zero production callers; `server.clj` still calls `providers/invoke-provider-async` for both prompts and recipe steps; per-session locks and `session_locked` are still in place; `set_directory`, `kill_session`-via-Process.destroy(), and `usage`/`cost` fields all still ship.

## Why now
Two forcing functions called out in the design (§2):
1. **Mid-turn user steering.** Long tool-heavy turns (>30 min) are common; mobile-first users have no escape hatch under the current model.
2. **Backend restart kills in-flight turns.** Frequent during development. Tmux makes provider processes survive backend lifecycle.

## Scope summary
Backend: prompt handler + recipe orchestrator → tmux/deliver!; `session_locked` + per-session locks deleted (compaction lock kept separate); `set_directory` deleted; `available_commands` lifecycle moves to connect+session-create/resume; `kill_session` reroutes through tmux/kill-window with synthesized `turn_complete{aborted:true}`; turn-completion derived from JSONL writes per provider; `usage`/`cost` removed; dead code (`invoke-provider-async`, `run-provider-process`, `active-provider-processes`) deleted.

iOS: `setWorkingDirectory` call site + method deleted; `lockedSessions` set + locked-state UI removed; `usage`/`cost` display + Codable fields removed; `turn_complete` decoder accepts optional `aborted:Bool`.

Tests: end-to-end concurrent-prompt test, backend-restart continuity test, eviction tests already covered by `tmux_integration_test.clj` pattern, `set_directory` returns "Unknown message type", `available_commands` lifecycle assertions, per-provider turn-complete unit tests.

Docs: STANDARDS.md WebSocket protocol section needs updating (currently still documents `session_locked` and `set_directory`).

## Acceptance Criteria
Verbatim from design doc §4:

1. Sending two concurrent `prompt` messages to the same session UUID never produces a `session_locked` response.
2. **Backend-restart continuity.** (a) create a session with UUID U in working directory W; wait for its `start-window!` to return. (b) Record `window-before = (-> @live-windows (get U) :tmux-window)`. (c) Kill and restart the backend (same JVM args). (d) After startup, assert `(= window-before (-> @live-windows (get U) :tmux-window))`. (e) Send a prompt "ACCEPT2-MARKER" over WebSocket with `resume_session_id = U`. (f) Within 2 s, assert the marker appears in `tmux capture-pane` of the same window. No new window may be created.
3. Starting a 5th session in a working directory where all 4 existing windows show `last-activity-ms` older than 15 minutes causes the oldest-by-last-activity window to be killed before the new one is created.
4. Starting a 5th session in a working directory where all 4 existing windows are "processing" creates the 5th window without killing any. When the first of the 4 crosses the 15-minute-idle threshold, it is reaped.
5. A session whose window has been evicted, when sent a new `prompt`, is respawned with `--resume <uuid>` and receives the nudge without any `session_resumed` message to iOS.
6. The `usage` and `cost` fields are absent from every `response` message observed during the full test suite.
7. `set_directory` is removed from the backend; tests assert that sending it returns an `error` with `"Unknown message type"`.
8. `available_commands` is sent automatically within 500 ms of `connected` when no directory is set, and is resent on every session creation/resume with project commands populated from the session's working directory.
9. A window idle for > 2 days is killed by the sweeper within the next sweeper pass.
10. `tmux-agent`-style manual attach (`tmux attach -t <session> ; select-window -t <window>`) works concurrently with backend nudges; the user sees their typing and the backend's nudges interleaved in the same pane.

## Open questions (from design §6 / Open questions)
- Cursor turn-complete marker — design §3.5 says original assumption was wrong; choose between file-mtime debounce and `--print --output-format stream-json`. Tracked in tmux-untethered-yj3.
- Compaction lock — design implies session locks go away, but `claude --compact` still spawns a one-shot CLI that would corrupt JSONL if interleaved with a live nudge. Resolve before deleting locks. Tracked in tmux-untethered-krt.
- Recipe orchestrator (server.clj:761) is a second `invoke-provider-async` site the design doesn't explicitly address — does each step share one tmux window or get its own? Tracked in tmux-untethered-1vt.
- Whether `aborted:true` gets a distinct iOS UI badge (currently flagged optional).

## Child tasks (13 total)
**Foundation / design resolution (P1):**
- tmux-untethered-ao0 — Wire prompt handler to tmux/deliver!
- tmux-untethered-yj3 — Resolve Cursor turn-complete marker (design §3.5 ambiguity)

**Core protocol changes (P1–P2):**
- tmux-untethered-2xl — Remove per-session locks + session_locked
- tmux-untethered-44v — JSONL-watcher-based turn-completion detection (per provider)
- tmux-untethered-1pe — Remove set_directory; auto-send available_commands
- tmux-untethered-ahs — Re-route kill_session through tmux/kill-window; emit aborted turn_complete
- tmux-untethered-1vt — Migrate recipe orchestrator (server.clj:761) to tmux/deliver!
- tmux-untethered-krt — Resolve compaction-lock open question

**Cleanup / docs / verification (P2–P3):**
- tmux-untethered-yer — Remove usage/cost fields end-to-end
- tmux-untethered-a1y — Delete dead code (invoke-provider-async, run-provider-process, active-provider-processes)
- tmux-untethered-m3k — Update STANDARDS.md WebSocket protocol section
- tmux-untethered-09b — Backend-restart continuity end-to-end test (also covers AC #3, #4, #5, #10)
- tmux-untethered-t9l — iOS protocol updates (setWorkingDirectory, locked-state UI, usage/cost, aborted:Bool)

## Acceptance-criteria coverage map
- AC #1 → ao0 + 2xl
- AC #2 → 09b
- AC #3, #4, #5 → 09b (extended) — see task body
- AC #6 → yer + m3k

title: JSONL-watcher-based turn-completion detection
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.5 (Turn-completion detection per provider). Also `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` §5 for verified markers and fixture files.

## Context
Today `turn_complete` is fired when the one-shot CLI process exits. Under tmux there is no exit — the TUI process stays alive across turns. Derive `turn_complete` from JSONL writes instead, per provider:
- **Claude**: `type:"assistant"` with `message.stop_reason ∈ {"end_turn","stop_sequence"}` and `isSidechain:false`. Verified in tmux-untethered-9hi.9 (commit 4132786c).
- **Copilot**: `type:"assistant.turn_end"` event in `events.jsonl` where the preceding `assistant.message` has empty `toolRequests`.
- **Cursor**: ambiguous — design §3.5 says original assumption was wrong. Resolution depends on tmux-untethered-curs (Cursor marker investigation).
- **OpenCode**: part file `type:"step-finish"` with `reason:"stop"`; streaming NDJSON equivalent `type:"step_finish"` with `part.reason:"stop"`.

The per-provider parser already lives in `replication.clj` for message extraction; extend each parser to also emit a "turn complete" observation that the watcher translates into a `turn_complete` WebSocket message.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add multimethod `providers/turn-complete? :provider raw-msg` returning `boolean` for each provider.
- [ ] Wire the watcher (`replication.clj` per-message handler) to call the multimethod and emit `{:type :turn-complete :session-id ...}` to all subscribers when it returns true.
- [ ] Stop emitting `turn_complete` based on CLI process exit (currently in the prompt-handler success branch, server.clj:1463).
- [ ] Document each provider's marker in `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` §5 with sample JSONL fragment.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/providers.clj` — add `turn-complete?` multimethod and per-provider implementations.
  - `backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj` — extend per-message watcher dispatch to call `turn-complete?` and emit the WebSocket message.
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — remove the post-process-exit `turn_complete` send at line ~1463 (the watcher now owns this).
  - `docs/provider-cli-reference.md` — §5 documentation update.
- Dependencies:
  - tmux-untethered-ao0 must land first (otherwise the process-exit signal is still load-bearing for the only call site).
  - tmux-untethered-curs must resolve to fix the Cursor marker.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test per provider: feed a fixture JSONL message representing a terminal turn, assert `turn-complete?` returns true.
- [ ] Unit test per provider: feed an intermediate (tool_use) message, assert `turn-complete?` returns false.
- [ ] Integration test: subscribe to a session, append a terminal-turn line to the watched JSONL, assert subscriber receives `{:type :turn-complete :session-id ...}` within 500ms.
- [ ] End-to-end: send a prompt → mock provider writes JSONL with a terminal turn marker → iOS receives `turn_complete` without any process exit.

## Acceptance Criteria
The watcher emits `turn_complete` from JSONL markers within 500ms of the marker appearing on disk, for all four providers. Markers documented per provider with sample fragments.

## Parallelization
Can run alongside tmux-untethered-2xl and tmux-untethered-ahs after tmux-untethered-ao0 + tmux-untethered-curs.

Implementation hook: extend the existing replication.clj watcher's per-message handler with a multimethod (providers/turn-complete? :provider raw-msg) that returns true for the terminal message of a turn.
acceptance_criteria: When a session's JSONL file shows the provider-specific turn-complete marker, the watcher emits turn_complete with the session-id to all subscribers within 500ms. Markers documented per provider with sample JSONL fragments. End-to-end test: send prompt, observe turn_complete arrives without process exit.

title: Remove per-session locking and session_locked message
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (Removed message types).

## Context
Per-session locks exist solely because the old one-shot CLI invocation forks the session if two `--resume` processes overlap. Once `tmux/deliver!` nudges into a live tmux pane (tmux-untethered-ao0), nudges naturally serialize through the TUI's stdin and the lock has no purpose.

The compaction handler also currently uses `acquire-session-lock!` (server.clj:1553). `claude --compact` still spawns a one-shot CLI that would corrupt the JSONL if interleaved with a live nudge — see open question resolved by tmux-untethered-comp (compaction-lock decision). Recommendation: introduce `compaction-locks` (separate atom) for that path and leave `session-locks` deleted.

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete `session-locks` atom from `replication.clj` (after replacing compaction's use with a separate lock).
- [ ] Delete `acquire-session-lock!` and `release-session-lock!` (or rename to `compaction-lock-*` and scope to the compaction path).
- [ ] Delete `send-session-locked!` (server.clj:529).
- [ ] Delete the lock-acquire/reject branch in the prompt handler (server.clj:1487 area) — it should be unreachable after tmux-untethered-ao0 anyway.
- [ ] Delete the lock-acquire branch in `kill_session` (server.clj:1599 area is fine; just don't block on a session lock).
- [ ] Stop emitting the `session_locked` WebSocket message anywhere in the codebase.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj` — `session-locks` atom, acquire/release helpers.
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — `send-session-locked!`, prompt handler else branch, compaction handler.
- New files: none. (Compaction lock atom can be added inline in `replication.clj` or `server.clj`.)
- Dependencies:
  - tmux-untethered-ao0 must land first (otherwise concurrent prompts would actually fork).
  - tmux-untethered-comp (compaction-lock decision) must resolve first to know what to do with the compaction call site.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `acquire-session-lock!` and `release-session-lock!` no longer exported (compile error if any test imports them).
- [ ] Existing tests in `replication_test.clj` referencing `session-locks` are removed or rewritten.
- [ ] Integration test: send two `prompt` messages with the same `resume_session_id` 50ms apart; assert no `session_locked` response on the WebSocket and both prompts appear in the pane in order.
- [ ] Manual verification: smoke test in the simulator; mid-turn prompt is no longer rejected.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criterion #1 from design doc §4: two concurrent `prompt` messages never produce `session_locked`.

## Parallelization
Can run alongside tmux-untethered-44v and tmux-untethered-ahs after tmux-untethered-ao0 + tmux-untethered-comp land. Blocks tmux-untethered-t9l.
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (Removed message types). Compaction handler still uses the lock today — decide whether compaction needs its own lock (likely yes, since it shells out to claude --compact and would corrupt JSONL if interleaved with a live nudge) or if it can be sequenced through tmux too. Recommend: keep a separate lock just for compaction, named compaction-locks, and leave session-locks deleted.
acceptance_criteria: session-locks atom and acquire/release helpers removed from replication.clj. send-session-locked! removed from server.clj. session_locked message type no longer produced. Documentation in CLAUDE.md / STANDARDS.md mentioning session_locked is removed or updated to call out that it has been removed.
notes: Implementation complete. Test suite: 528 tests, 3070 assertions, same 2 pre-existing failures as HEAD (test-load-config & test-process-orchestration-response-invalid-json-retry), 0 new failures. Not committed - awaiting code review per recipe instructions.
close_reason: Per-session prompt lock removed; session_locked message removed. Compaction-only lock (compaction-locks) retained to guard the one-shot claude --compact CLI path. Kill-window handshake synthesizes aborted turn_complete before compacting so iOS unlocks. AC #1 asserted in test-prompt-never-sends-session-locked. Contract test guards all four removed symbols. 528 tests, 3071 assertions, only the 2 pre-existing baseline failures.

title: Wire prompt handler to tmux/deliver! instead of invoke-provider-async
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.4 (Component interactions, prompt lifecycles), §3.3 (start-window!, deliver! code), §4 (Acceptance criterion #1).

## Context
The central wiring change. Today the WebSocket prompt handler in `server.clj` (line 1437) calls `providers/invoke-provider-async` which spawns a one-shot non-interactive subprocess. Two prompts to the same session UUID can't overlap, which is why per-session locking exists. Once this lands and prompts are nudged into a live tmux pane, the lock has no purpose (see tmux-untethered-2xl).

The tmux machinery (`tmux/start-window!`, `tmux/deliver!`, `tmux/respawn-and-deliver!`) is already shipped and wired into `-main` for startup rediscovery — see `backend/src/voice_code/tmux.clj`. This task is purely the call-site swap.

## Requirements
- [ ] For new sessions (`new_session_id` present): call `tmux/start-window!` with `:session-uuid`, `:session-name`, `:provider`, `:workdir`, `:initial-prompt`, `:resume? false`.
- [ ] For resumed sessions (`resume_session_id` present): call `tmux/deliver! session-uuid prompt-text`. `deliver!` itself handles live-vs-evicted (respawn-and-deliver! with `--resume`).
- [ ] Remove the `providers/invoke-provider-async` call at server.clj:1437.
- [ ] Remove the surrounding lock-acquisition + lock-release wrapping (deferred to tmux-untethered-2xl if cleaner to split).
- [ ] Keep ack-on-receipt behavior identical so iOS still gets immediate `ack`.
- [ ] Turn-completion still arrives via the existing watcher path (real signal swap is tmux-untethered-44v).

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — prompt handler around line 1437.
- New files: none.
- Read first: `backend/src/voice_code/tmux.clj` (entry-point arities for `start-window!` and `deliver!`).
- Dependencies: none — foundation task. Other backend tasks layer on top.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: handler dispatches a `prompt` with `new_session_id` to a stubbed `tmux/start-window!` with the expected keyword args.
- [ ] Unit test: handler dispatches a `prompt` with `resume_session_id` to a stubbed `tmux/deliver!`.
- [ ] Integration test (`backend/test/voice_code/tmux_integration_test.clj` pattern with disposable tmux server): full WebSocket prompt → window appears → mock-provider script in pane sees the nudge text.
- [ ] Manual verification: send two prompts in quick succession to the same session UUID; both land in the pane in order.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criterion #1 from design doc §4: sending two concurrent `prompt` messages to the same session UUID never produces a `session_locked` response. (Note: until tmux-untethered-2xl lands, the lock still exists; this task makes it harmless because nothing in the tmux path acquires it.)

## Parallelization
Foundation. Blocks 2xl, 44v, ahs, and the new recipe-orchestrator-migration task.
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.4 (lifecycle) and §4 (verification). For new sessions: tmux/start-window! creates the window, then tmux/deliver! nudges the initial prompt. For existing live windows: tmux/deliver! nudges directly. For evicted sessions: respawn-and-deliver! handles --resume transparently.
