Sending two concurrent prompt messages to the same session UUID never produces a session_locked response (acceptance criterion #1 from design doc).

title: ActiveSessionManager.isActive() is not thread-safe — data race with background upsert threads
description: ActiveSessionManager.isActive(sessionId:) reads activeSessionId from background dispatch queues (inside SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload's upsertQueue.async { ... }.performAndWait). Meanwhile setActiveSession() and clearActiveSession() write activeSessionId from the main thread. The @Published property has no synchronization. This is a data race: the background thread's isActive() check can read a partially-written or stale UUID, causing incorrect unreadCount increments for already-opened sessions.

title: stalledChains published but never surfaced in UI — silently broken history
description:
VoiceCodeClient publishes @Published var stalledChains: [String: Int64] and sessionSyncDidStallChain populates it, but no view reads stalledChains or calls dismissStalledChain. The comment on the field says 'Views observe this so they can surface a banner', but that banner was never wired up.

When an is_complete:false chain stalls (e.g., a single message exceeds the byte budget), SessionSyncManager fires sessionSyncDidStallChain, VoiceCodeClient records it in stalledChains, but nothing is displayed to the user. The conversation view silently stops loading older history with no indication that content is missing.

The prunedGap pattern (PrunedGapBanner in ConversationView, dismissPrunedGap in VoiceCodeClient, tested in SessionSyncManagerPrunedGapTests) is the exact same shape and should be replicated for stalledChains.
close_reason: StalledChainBanner added to ConversationView; 6 tests in StalledChainBannerTests.swift all pass

title: macOS sidebar sort order and folder badges stale after session_history update
description: SessionSidebarView listens to sessionListDidUpdate but not sessionHistoryDidUpdate. When new messages arrive via session_history (updates lastModified, messageCount, unreadCount on CDBackendSession), the sidebar cachedRecentSessions and cachedSessionsByDirectory are NOT recomputed. This means: (1) sessions do not reorder in the Recent section after a new message arrives, and (2) the per-folder unread badge in the Projects section does not update because SessionSidebarView does not re-render (only individual SessionSidebarRow instances do, via @ObservedObject). Fix: add .onReceive for sessionHistoryDidUpdate that calls loadSessions().
close_reason: Fixed: added .onReceive(sessionHistoryDidUpdate) handler to SessionSidebarView that calls loadSessions() to recompute cachedRecentSessions and cachedSessionsByDirectory; added 2 tests verifying sort order and unread count update correctly after session property changes; also updated stale comments in VoiceCodeClient.swift about v0.5.0 dispatch

title: Fix CommandOutputDetailView: stale output and early spinner dismiss
description:
CommandOutputDetailView has two bugs in loadOutput():

1. Stale data on open: when the view appears, client.commandOutputFull may still hold output from a previously-viewed command. The view renders under a loading spinner so the user doesn't see it directly, but the wrong data is set on client before the response arrives.

2. Early spinner dismiss: loadOutput() schedules a 0.5s timer to set isLoading=false regardless of whether command_output_full has arrived. On a slow or loaded server (response time > 0.5s), the view transitions to isLoading=false with commandOutputFull still nil or stale, showing 'Failed to load output'. When the real response eventually arrives, commandOutputFull is updated and SwiftUI re-renders — but the user saw an error state for that window.

3. Missing session ID match: the view renders any commandOutputFull without verifying its commandSessionId matches the requested session. If two views are open concurrently or a response from a previous request arrives late, wrong output can render.

Fix: (a) clear commandOutputFull before the request, (b) use onChange(of: client.commandOutputFull) to set isLoading=false when a matching response arrives, (c) add commandSessionId verification in the body, (d) keep a longer fallback timeout for the no-response case.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit: clear stale commandOutputFull before request, use onChange to dismiss spinner on matching response, add commandSessionId guard in body

title: CDMessage.fetchMessages: add offset as secondary sort for v0.5.0 ordering
description: fetchMessages sorts only by timestamp. For v0.5.0 protocol messages with identical timestamps (possible for rapid exchanges), display order is indeterminate. The canonical v0.5.0 ordering is by offset (JSONL line number). Adding offset as a secondary sort key fixes ties while preserving correct v0.4.0 behavior (all v0.4.0 messages have offset=0, so secondary sort is a no-op for them).

title: Remove prune block from loadSessionIfNeeded
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/conversation-refresh-and-prune-fix.md#fix-2--remove-prune-from-loadsessionifneeded

## Context
loadSessionIfNeeded() calls CDMessage.pruneOldMessages() on every session open with no needsPruning gate. This deletes cached rows the moment the session opens, before the backend's session_history reply arrives. The @FetchRequest renders each CoreData change, so users see a visible count drop followed by rows being added back — the "messages disappear then reload" bug. Pruning already happens correctly in handleSessionHistoryPayload (both v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 paths), gated by CDMessage.needsPruning(). The fix is to remove the redundant, ungated prune from the view-open path.

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete the entire performBackgroundTask { pruneOldMessages ... } block (lines 719–732) from loadSessionIfNeeded()
- [ ] No replacement needed — the SessionSyncManager prune path remains unchanged
- [ ] The logger calls inside the deleted block are also removed (no orphan log statements)

## Technical Approach
**File to modify:** `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift` lines 719–732

Delete this block entirely:
```swift
// Prune old messages on background context before loading
// iOS only needs recent messages; backend retains full history
let sessionId = session.id
PersistenceController.shared.performBackgroundTask { backgroundContext in
    let pruneStart = Date()
    let deletedCount = CDMessage.pruneOldMessages(sessionId: sessionId, in: backgroundContext)
    let pruneMs = Int(Date().timeIntervalSince(pruneStart) * 1000)
    if deletedCount > 0 {
        try? backgroundContext.save()
        logger.info("⏱️ Pruned \(deletedCount) messages in \(pruneMs)ms")
    } else {
        logger.info("⏱️ No pruning needed (\(pruneMs)ms)")
    }
}
```

- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: none

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Add hasSubscribedThisAppear guard to scenePhase handler"

## Verification
- [ ] `grep -n "pruneOldMessages" ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift` returns no results
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] Unit tests in PruneOnOpenRegressionTests pass (written in the test task)
- [ ] Manual: open a session with 55+ cached messages; count does not drop before the first session_history log line appears

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC2: Opening a session with 55 cached messages displays 55 rows immediately; no rows deleted until first session_history reply
- AC3: loadSessionIfNeeded no longer contains a CDMessage.pruneOldMessages call
- AC4: handleSessionHistoryPayload v0.5.0 still prunes when count exceeds 60 (existing behavior preserved)
close_reason: Deleted the performBackgroundTask pruneOldMessages block (lines 719–732) from loadSessionIfNeeded(). All tests pass.

title: Add hasSubscribedThisAppear guard to scenePhase handler
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/conversation-refresh-and-prune-fix.md#fix-1--gate-the-scenephase-handler-on-hassubscribedthisappear

## Context
The onChange(of: scenePhase) handler fires for every ConversationView alive in the NavigationStack, not just the visible one. Views whose onDisappear already fired have hasSubscribedThisAppear = false but still pass through the handler and call refreshSubscription() — re-subscribing sessions the user navigated away from. This fix gates the handler on the flag that already encodes the correct signal.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `guard hasSubscribedThisAppear else { return }` immediately before `hasSubscribedThisAppear = false` in the onChange(of: scenePhase) handler
- [ ] The guard must appear after the two existing guards (newPhase == .active and !isLocallyCreated) and before the flag reset
- [ ] hasSubscribedThisAppear is reset to false and refreshSubscription() is called only when the guard passes

## Technical Approach
**File to modify:** `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift` lines 655–660

Current:
```swift
.onChange(of: scenePhase) { oldPhase, newPhase in
    guard newPhase == .active, oldPhase != .active else { return }
    guard !(session.isLocallyCreated && session.messageCount == 0) else { return }
    hasSubscribedThisAppear = false
    client.refreshSubscription(sessionId: session.id.uuidString.lowercased())
}
```

After:
```swift
.onChange(of: scenePhase) { oldPhase, newPhase in
    guard newPhase == .active, oldPhase != .active else { return }
    guard !(session.isLocallyCreated && session.messageCount == 0) else { return }
    guard hasSubscribedThisAppear else { return }
    hasSubscribedThisAppear = false
    client.refreshSubscription(sessionId: session.id.uuidString.lowercased())
}
```

- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: none

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Remove prune block from loadSessionIfNeeded"

## Verification
- [ ] Build succeeds with no warnings on the modified file
- [ ] Unit tests in ScenePhaseSubscriptionGuardTests pass (written in the test task)
- [ ] Manual: background app while on Session B (having previously visited Session A); foreground — only Session B receives a subscribe wire message in logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: Foregrounding after Session A → Session B navigation sends exactly one subscribe (for B, not A)
- AC6: hasSubscribedThisAppear is false after the handler fires for the visible session
- AC7: The handler is a no-op for ConversationViews whose onDisappear has fired

title: Fix conversation refresh spurious re-subscribe and prune flicker
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/conversation-refresh-and-prune-fix.md

## Overview
Two iOS bugs affect ConversationView reliability on session open and foreground return:

1. **Spurious background re-subscriptions**: The `onChange(of: scenePhase)` handler fires for every ConversationView in the NavigationStack, causing sessions the user navigated away from to be re-subscribed on foreground return.
2. **Message flicker on open**: `loadSessionIfNeeded()` calls `CDMessage.pruneOldMessages()` unconditionally, deleting cached rows immediately on open. The subsequent `session_history` reply then adds rows back, producing a visible N → N−pruned → N+delta jump.

Fix 1 adds a `guard hasSubscribedThisAppear else { return }` gate to the scenePhase handler. Fix 2 removes the prune block from `loadSessionIfNeeded()`, leaving pruning exclusively in `handleSessionHistoryPayload` where it is already gated by `CDMessage.needsPruning()`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Foregrounding the app after navigating Session A → Session B sends exactly one subscribe wire message (for Session B). No subscribe is sent for Session A.
2. Opening a session with 55 cached messages displays 55 rows immediately; no rows are deleted until the first session_history reply arrives.
3. `loadSessionIfNeeded` no longer contains a `CDMessage.pruneOldMessages` call.
4. `handleSessionHistoryPayload` (v0.5.0 path) still prunes when total count exceeds 60 after a batch insert. Existing pruning tests pass unchanged.
5. All existing `VoiceCodeClientProtocolVersionTests` pass.
6. `hasSubscribedThisAppear` is `false` after the scenePhase handler fires for the visible session.
7. The scenePhase handler is a no-op for any ConversationView whose `onDisappear` has fired.

title: Byte-prefix compaction probe (resolves OPEN claude --compact semantics)
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§1 (the OPEN item on `claude --compact` semantics) and §6 R2

## Context
The §1 OPEN item is the load-bearing question for R2 scope: does `claude --compact` rewrite the JSONL prefix (making compaction a routine file-replaced event for every iOS client), or does it pure-tail-append (in which case the existing shrink-recovery branch defends against a phantom)? Travis's stated belief and the existing code disagree. A naive sha256 of the whole file can't distinguish "rewrote prefix" from "appended summary line" — both change the hash. The §1 recipe is a prefix-comparison procedure. This task is the probe + recipe execution, not unresolved scope.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create a probe script (Clojure or bash, lives under `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/scripts/`) that:
  1. Records `pre-length = (file-length file)` and `pre-sha = (sha256 (slurp-bytes file))` of a target JSONL.
  2. Pauses for the operator to run `claude --compact` against the matching session.
  3. After operator resumes, computes:
     - `post-length`
     - `post-prefix-sha = sha256` of the first `pre-length` bytes of the post-compact file.
  4. Reports the outcome:
     - `post-length > pre-length AND post-prefix-sha == pre-sha` → "TAIL-APPEND" (compact is append-only; R2 path is corruption-only).
     - `post-prefix-sha != pre-sha` → "PREFIX-REWRITE" (compact rewrites; R2 is the routine compact recovery surface).
     - `post-length < pre-length` → "TRUNCATE" (existing shrink-recovery branch fires; strong-rewrite signal).
- [ ] Run the probe against at least 2 real Claude sessions (different sizes; e.g. one small, one ≥10k lines).
- [ ] Run the corroborating production-log check: `grep "JSONL file shrank below tracked cursor" backend/server.out*` across recent logs. If never-fired, that's empirical evidence the invariant holds in production today.
- [ ] Write up findings in a brief note under `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/docs/probes/` (or as a `bd remember` entry) documenting:
  - Probe results per session tested (TAIL-APPEND / PREFIX-REWRITE / TRUNCATE).
  - The production-log grep result.
  - Recommendation: which §1 path the rest of the design should plan around — "shrink-recovery is dead code, delete it" vs. "compact rewrites; R2 is routine; keep the recovery surface hot."
- [ ] If the recommendation is "shrink-recovery is dead code," file a follow-up `bd create` for the deletion (not part of this v0.5.0 epic — out-of-cycle cleanup).

## Technical Approach
- Files to create: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/scripts/probe-compact-prefix.{clj,sh}` and a findings doc.
- The probe should refuse to run if the target file isn't quiescent (no growing during the pre-snapshot — wait for `mtime` to stabilize for ~3 seconds).
- The probe should NOT modify the JSONL — it's pure observation.
- Dependencies: none — this is a verification probe, independently runnable from any implementation task. Should run EARLY so the findings can inform R2 design decisions.

## Verification
- [ ] Probe script runs end-to-end against at least 2 real sessions.
- [ ] Findings doc committed with concrete TAIL-APPEND vs PREFIX-REWRITE answer.
- [ ] If a follow-up cleanup ticket is warranted, it's filed and linked from the findings doc.

## Acceptance Criteria
Does NOT directly satisfy any of the 8 epic ACs; this is the OPEN item from §1 that informs the design's R2 cost/frequency estimate. Resolving it sharpens AC8 — if compact rewrites, the AC8 path is hit per-compact in production; if it doesn't, AC8 is corruption-only.

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: everything. Should be done EARLY so the findings can inform decisions on whether to keep or remove the shrink-recovery branch in T2 / T5 / T10.
close_reason: Probe script (scripts/probe-compact-prefix.sh) with --self-test (4/4 pass) + findings doc (docs/probes/2026-05-12-compact-prefix-probe.md) + corroborating production-log audit (0 shrink-recovery firings across 242,121 lines / ~36MB across 6 live worktrees) delivered. Per-session probe rows remain operator-driven and are staged as TBD in the findings doc; a templated follow-up bd-create line for the dead-code deletion sits at the bottom of the doc to fire if the eventual rows resolve to TAIL-APPEND.

title: Dual-protocol matrix integration test (v0.4.0 + v0.5.0 + rollback)
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§4.4 (AC1, AC2, AC4) and §6 R1 / R5 (rollback path)

## Context
AC1 requires both wire protocols to be exercised in tests: v0.5.0 is the default; v0.4.0 round-trips at least one full subscribe/push cycle in a dual-protocol integration test. This is the rollback path of §6 R1 — clients on app version N-1 must keep working while N is in App Store review. Per-channel protocol selection from T7 means a single server serves both versions concurrently. This task is the integration test that proves the dispatch actually fans out as designed AND that v0.5.0 path never emits `is_complete: false` while v0.4.0 path still does.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add integration test under `test/voice_code/server_integration_test.clj` (or a new `dual_protocol_test.clj`) that:
  - Spins up the in-process server with two fake channels connected.
  - Channel A sends `connect` with `protocol_version: "0.4.0"`.
  - Channel B sends `connect` with `protocol_version: "0.5.0"`.
  - Assert each channel's `@connected-clients` entry has the correct `:negotiated-protocol-version`.
  - Both subscribe to the same session.
  - Watcher fires after the JSONL fixture grows by N lines.
  - Assert Channel A receives a v0.4.0-shaped `session_history` (with `:seq`, `:first_seq`, `:last_seq`, `:next_seq`, possibly `:is_complete: false` if window splits — this is the LEGACY behavior).
  - Assert Channel B receives a v0.5.0-shaped `session_history` (with `:offset`, `:next_offset`, `:end_of_file`, `:file_signature` — NO `:is_complete`, NO `:first_seq`/`:last_seq`, NO `:gap`).
- [ ] Add assertion (AC2 spot-check): the v0.5.0 fan-out branch's code path does NOT pass through `assign-seq!`. Easiest check: instrument `assign-seq!` with a counter or `(when *test-tap* (swap! *test-tap* update :assign-seq-calls (fnil inc 0)))`; assert counter is non-zero for the v0.4.0 channel's payload and unchanged when the watcher fires for the v0.5.0-only fan-out path. (If both channels share the same watcher tick, the counter should reflect only the v0.4.0 arm's invocation.)
- [ ] Add assertion (AC4): NO `:is_complete` field appears on the v0.5.0 channel's payload across the full subscribe → push → reconnect cycle.
- [ ] Add an additional sub-test for the rollback ceiling (covers T8): with `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0` (or the equivalent atom-set in the test), a channel announcing `0.5.0` is silently downgraded to `:v0.4.0` and receives v0.4.0-shaped replies.
- [ ] Add an additional sub-test for the reconnect-after-drop happy path (AC5): on a v0.5.0 channel, append 200 lines while the channel is "disconnected" (drop from `@connected-clients`), reconnect, single subscribe with `from_offset = pre-drop cursor` returns all 200 lines in one payload, `end_of_file: true`. No second subscribe needed.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: under `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/test/voice_code/`
- Use existing test helpers for fake channels (org.httpkit.server channel mocks should already exist in the test surface — verify and reuse).
- Spy on `assign-seq!` via a dynamic var or `with-redefs`. The assertion is "v0.5.0-only fan-out does not increment", not "no calls anywhere" — the v0.4.0 channel legitimately triggers it.
- Dependencies: T7 (negotiation), T8 (VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL), T9 (subscribe handler), T10 (push-to-subscribers!).

## Verification
- [ ] All sub-tests above pass.
- [ ] Test run included in `make test` default target.
- [ ] Log output (during test) of v0.5.0 channel shows ZERO occurrences of `:is_complete`.
- [ ] Test fails fast and clearly when the dispatch regresses (e.g. if someone accidentally routes both channels through the same path).

## Acceptance Criteria
Verifies AC1 (dual-protocol matrix), AC2 (assign-seq! on v0.4.0 only), AC4 (no `is_complete` on v0.5.0), AC5 (reconnect single round-trip).

## Parallelization
Sequential — requires T7, T8, T9, T10 complete.
close_reason: Added backend/test/voice_code/dual_protocol_test.clj with 5 deftests / 65 assertions covering AC1 (distinct wire shapes per channel from a single watcher tick), AC2 (v0.5.0 fan-out does not invoke assign-seq! — spied via with-redefs counter), AC4 (no :is_complete across full subscribe → push → reconnect cycle), §6 R1 / T8 (VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0 silently downgrades a 0.5.0 client to v0.4.0 wire), and AC5 / §6 R5 (200-line backfill after drop in a single subscribe reply with end_of_file=true). All 5 tests pass; full backend suite at 4 baseline failures (no net new failures).

title: iOS subscribe() v5 + handleSessionHistoryPayload v5 + purgeMessagesAtOrAbove + file_replaced recovery
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.3 (iOS handleSessionHistoryPayload) and §3.5 (purgeMessagesAtOrAbove) and §6 R2 (file_replaced recovery)

## Context
This is the load-bearing iOS simplification. The current `handleSessionHistoryPayload` (SessionSyncManager.swift:385) does 3-case gap detection (`firstSeq > localLastSeq + 1` triggers a backfill re-subscribe), tracks `incompleteChainCursors` and `prunedSessions`, and walks the cursor-stall heuristic in the `is_complete: false` chain handler at lines 624-666. The v5 path collapses to: optional file_replaced recovery → (optional) cursor-reset on `next_offset < lastOffsetMerged` → TTS-boundary capture (with the `payload.nextOffset > 0` gate) → idempotent (sessionId, offset) upsert → `lastOffsetMerged = max(...)`. The order of operations inside the `file_replaced` branch is load-bearing: persist the new signature BEFORE re-subscribing or the next subscribe re-sends the stale signature and the server replies `file_replaced: true` again, looping forever.

## Requirements
- [ ] Update `subscribe()` in `VoiceCodeClient.swift:1482` to send v0.5.0 shape when the channel's negotiated protocol is `.v0_5_0` (set by T6 from the server's connect response):
  - Read `fromOffset = lastOffsetMerged(sessionId:context:)` (a single CoreData fetch).
  - Read `lastFileSignature(sessionId:context:)`; include `file_signature_seen` only when non-nil.
  - Build the dict `["type": "subscribe", "session_id": sessionId, "from_offset": fromOffset]` and conditionally `message["file_signature_seen"] = sig`.
  - Branch on negotiated protocol: v0.4.0 channels keep the existing `last_seq` build path (which reads `newestCachedSeq` / `CDBackendSession.nextSeq`).
- [ ] Add helper functions `lastOffsetMerged(sessionId:context:)` and `lastFileSignature(sessionId:context:)` — one-line CoreData fetches each.
- [ ] Implement `handleSessionHistoryPayload(_:)` v0.5.0 entry point in `SessionSyncManager` per §3.3 sketch:
  - `file_replaced == true` branch FIRST: log warning; reset `session.lastOffsetMerged = 0`, `session.liveFromOffset = 0`, `session.lastFileSignature = payload.fileSignature`; call `purgeMessagesAtOrAbove(offset: 0, ...)`; reset `session.messageCount = 0`; `ctx.save()`; on success, dispatch to main and call `delegate?.sessionSyncRequestsResubscribeFromZero(sessionUUID)`. ORDER IS LOAD-BEARING — save MUST complete before re-subscribe is dispatched.
  - Persist `session.lastFileSignature = payload.fileSignature` on every non-mismatch reply with non-nil signature (matches T10's push-path emission of `file_signature` on every reply).
  - "I was ahead; reset" branch: when `payload.nextOffset < session.lastOffsetMerged`, log + set `lastOffsetMerged = payload.nextOffset` + `purgeMessagesAtOrAbove(offset: payload.nextOffset, ...)`.
  - TTS gate: `if session.liveFromOffset == 0 && payload.nextOffset > 0 { session.liveFromOffset = payload.nextOffset }` — the `nextOffset > 0` guard is load-bearing (prevents empty caught-up reply from pinning `liveFromOffset=0`, which would re-speak historical messages on subsequent payloads).
  - Idempotent upsert keyed on `(sessionId, offset)` via existing `upsertMessage`.
  - TTS extraction: only emit `didReceiveAssistantText` for messages where `inserted == true AND role == "assistant" AND liveFromOffset > 0 AND wireMessage.offset >= liveFromOffset`.
  - Advance `session.lastOffsetMerged = max(payload.nextOffset, session.lastOffsetMerged)` (max-guard absorbs out-of-order delivery).
- [ ] Add `purgeMessagesAtOrAbove(offset:session:in:)` per §3.5 spec:
  - Implementation: `NSBatchDeleteRequest` with predicate `sessionId == %@ AND offset >= %@`, plus `mergeChanges(fromRemoteContextSave:)` to keep view-context observers in sync.
  - Caller responsibility documented (cursors set BEFORE call, messageCount recompute AFTER).
- [ ] Add `sessionSyncRequestsResubscribeFromZero(_:)` to `SessionSyncManagerDelegate`. Concrete implementation in `VoiceCodeClient` calls `subscribe(sessionId:)` — same entry point as `restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect`. No new socket plumbing.
- [ ] **AC3 audit — `CDBackendSession.nextSeq` reads on the v0.5.0 path.** Before landing, grep `nextSeq` across the iOS codebase and verify EVERY read is either (a) inside a `.v0_4_0` conditional branch, (b) inside the v0.4.0 handler that's untouched, or (c) demonstrably dead on the v0.5.0 path. Document the grep result and the audit verdict inline in this task's notes (or as a checklist item complete with file:line refs). Confirmed call sites to clear: `newestCachedSeq()` derivation at `VoiceCodeClient.swift:1482` (becomes v0.4.0-only after this task), the `maxCachedSeq()` derivation at `SessionSyncManager.swift:706` (the cursor-derived second cursor), any reads in `restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect`.
- [ ] DELETE on the v0.5.0 path only: 3-case gap decision, `localLastSeq` snapshot, `firstSeq > localLastSeq + 1` backfill trigger, `incompleteChainCursors` map references, `prunedSessions` set references, cursor-stall heuristic at lines 624-666. The v0.4.0 handler keeps all of these — they're only dead on the v0.5.0 arm.
- [ ] `restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect` at VoiceCodeClient.swift:1437-1465 needs no separate change — it iterates `subscriptions` and calls `subscribe()`, which inherits the v0.5.0 behavior.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift` (subscribe v5 + delegate handler)
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift` (handleSessionHistoryPayload v5 + purgeMessagesAtOrAbove + new delegate method)
- The v0.4.0 handler MUST be preserved for the rollback window. Wrap dispatch on the channel's negotiated protocol.
- Dependencies: T6 (WireMessageV5 / SessionHistoryPayloadV5 structs + negotiated-version state) and T11 (lastOffsetMerged, lastFileSignature, liveFromOffset, CDMessage.offset).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `subscribe()` v5 sends `["type": "subscribe", "session_id": ..., "from_offset": N]` with no `file_signature_seen` when `lastFileSignature` is nil.
- [ ] Unit test: `subscribe()` v5 includes `file_signature_seen` when CDBackendSession has a non-nil `lastFileSignature`.
- [ ] Unit test (AC8): seed `lastFileSignature` to a known-non-matching value; trigger subscribe; receive a `session_history` with `file_replaced: true`; assert (a) outbound msg contained `file_signature_seen=seeded`, (b) `CDMessage` rows for session deleted, (c) `lastOffsetMerged=0` and `liveFromOffset=0`, (d) `lastFileSignature` updated to server's value, (e) second outbound subscribe with `from_offset: 0` observed within one run-loop turn.
- [ ] Unit test: matching `file_signature_seen` → normal merge path, no purge.
- [ ] Unit test: TTS gate — empty first reply (`messages: [], nextOffset: 0`) does NOT set `liveFromOffset`; subsequent reply with `nextOffset > 0` sets it. AC6 regression.
- [ ] Unit test: "I was ahead" reset — `payload.nextOffset < lastOffsetMerged` triggers purge of `offset >= payload.nextOffset`.
- [ ] Unit test: idempotent upsert — replaying the same `WireMessageV5` does NOT re-fire TTS or duplicate the row.
- [ ] Unit test: `purgeMessagesAtOrAbove(offset: 5, ...)` deletes rows with `offset >= 5` and leaves rows `< 5`.
- [ ] Unit test: SmartSpeakingTests (the existing 4 tests) pass with the `liveFromOffset` substitution.
- [ ] **AC3 verification:** `grep -rn 'nextSeq' ios/VoiceCode/` produces only matches inside v0.4.0 conditional arms or the legacy `WireMessage`/`SessionHistoryPayload` types. Document the grep output and the verdict in task notes.
- [ ] Unit test: `lastFileSignature` updates after a non-mismatch push reply that carries `file_signature` (regression coupling with T10).
- [ ] Manual E2E: reconnect-after-drop → one `subscribe` ↔ `session_history` round-trip, NO `is_complete: false` log entries (AC5).
- [ ] Manual E2E: file_replaced flow triggered by manually rewriting the JSONL prefix → purge + re-subscribe + re-fetch.

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC3, AC5, AC6, AC8.

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T9, T10, T13, T14. (Depends on T6, T11.)

title: Implement push-to-subscribers! tailored fan-out with watcher snapshot fast path
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.3 (push-to-subscribers! sketch) and §3.4 (component interactions point 2) and §6 R2 (file_signature on every reply)

## Context
`broadcast-session-history!` (server.clj:~1400) today sends one fixed payload to every subscriber for a session, which is what forced the windowed `is_complete: false` chain protocol. The v0.5.0 push is per-subscriber tailored: each subscriber's `[:session-offsets session-id]` is the slice point. To avoid re-parsing the file once per subscriber per tick, the watcher feeds its already-parsed snapshot in (`parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw` + `stamp-offsets` from T5). Subscribers whose offsets sit inside that snapshot get served from memory; subscribers behind it fall back to `read-from-offset` for the gap. Per §6 R2, every push reply also carries the current `file_signature` so iOS keeps `lastFileSignature` fresh between subscribes.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `push-to-subscribers!` to `server.clj`. Signature: `[session-id provider file-path snapshot snapshot-from-offset file-end-line-count current-sig]`. The `current-sig` is computed ONCE per watcher tick by the caller (in `replication.clj`'s watcher hook), via `compute-file-signature` (T3), and passed through to every subscriber so we don't recompute per-subscriber.
- [ ] Helper `subscribers-for`: walks `@connected-clients`, returns `[[channel client-info] ...]` for clients that are authenticated, subscribed to `session-id`, NOT marked-deleted for this client, AND have `:negotiated-protocol-version = :v0.5.0`. v0.4.0 channels are excluded — they continue to receive from the existing `broadcast-session-history!` via the watcher's per-protocol dispatch.
- [ ] For each subscriber:
  - Read `client-offset = (get-in client-info [:session-offsets session-id] 0)` and `max-bytes`.
  - Fast path (`client-offset >= snapshot-from-offset` AND `snapshot-from-offset` non-nil): slice `snapshot` via `(drop-while #(< (:offset %) client-offset) snapshot)`; pack via `truncate-budget`; compute `next-offset = (if (seq budget) (inc (:offset (peek budget))) client-offset)`; compute `end-of-file? = (and (= (count budget) (count sliced)) (= next-offset file-end-line-count))` (the comparison against `file-end-line-count`, NOT `(count snapshot)`, is the pass-2 fix).
  - Backfill path (`client-offset < snapshot-from-offset`): call `repl/read-from-offset provider file-path client-offset limit` where `limit = (estimate-line-limit max-bytes)`.
  - If `(seq messages)` OR `current-sig` differs from the last-emitted sig for this channel (rare; signal a sig refresh on caught-up subscribers): emit `{:type :session-history :session-id ... :messages [...] :next-offset N :end-of-file end-of-file? :file-signature current-sig}` and `(swap! connected-clients assoc-in [channel :session-offsets session-id] next-offset)`.
  - **NOTE (signature on every reply):** include `:file-signature current-sig` on every emitted payload (matches iOS expectation in §3.3 — "Persist the latest file_signature on every non-mismatch reply"). Subscribe replies (T9) already do this; push replies must too. Otherwise iOS's `lastFileSignature` only updates on subscribe and drifts stale on long-open sessions.
- [ ] Empty-snapshot guard: when `snapshot = []` but `snapshot-from-offset` and `file-end-line-count` are set (all-filtered tick from T5), still walk subscribers — anyone at-or-past `snapshot-from-offset` gets a zero-message reply with `next-offset = file-end-line-count, end-of-file? true, file-signature current-sig`. Subscribers behind it fall into the backfill path normally.
- [ ] Watcher per-protocol dispatch: at the existing `assign-seq! → broadcast-session-history!` site, tee per channel protocol. v0.4.0/v0.3.0 keep calling `broadcast-session-history!` (UNTOUCHED — design doc forbids modifying it). v0.5.0 calls `push-to-subscribers!` with the snapshot from T5's pipeline AND `current-sig` from T3. The watcher computes `current-sig` once per tick.
- [ ] Idempotency: concurrent push and subscribe writes to `[:session-offsets session-id]` resolve via assoc-in's last-write-wins; the (sessionId, offset) upsert on the iOS side absorbs any duplicate delivery. Document this in a comment on the swap site.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` (new fn + watcher tee), `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj` (the watcher hook that calls into the per-protocol fan-out — also where `current-sig` is computed and threaded through).
- The `subscribers-for` helper mirrors `broadcast-session-history!`'s filter chain at server.clj:1424-1430 with the `:v0.5.0` filter added.
- The watcher tee is the only place v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 fan-out is gated; it MUST NOT modify `broadcast-session-history!` itself.
- `current-sig` is cheap (file length + first-line UUID); computing once per tick is the right cost model.
- Dependencies: T3 (compute-file-signature; called once per tick by the watcher hook, passed in), T4 (read-from-offset for backfill), T5 (snapshot input shape), T7 (channel-protocol filter), T9 (the subscribe handler seeds `[:session-offsets]` so the fast path has something to read).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: snapshot fast path with one subscriber at `client-offset = snapshot-from-offset` → receives the whole snapshot, `next-offset = file-end-line-count` when snapshot reaches EOF, AND `file_signature` field present in payload.
- [ ] Unit test: snapshot fast path with one subscriber at `client-offset` strictly inside the snapshot → receives only the tail-slice, with `file_signature` present.
- [ ] Unit test: backfill path with one subscriber behind `snapshot-from-offset` → falls into `read-from-offset` for the gap; cache advanced to the returned `next-offset`; `file_signature` present.
- [ ] Unit test: two subscribers at different offsets on the same session → each gets a correctly-tailored slice in a single tick.
- [ ] Unit test (the pass-2 EOF fix): snapshot is a partial view (file-end-line-count > snapshot-end) → `end-of-file?` is correctly `false` for the in-memory path.
- [ ] Unit test: empty snapshot with non-nil `snapshot-from-offset` → caught-up subscribers get a zero-message `end-of-file? true` reply with `next-offset = file-end-line-count` and `file_signature` present.
- [ ] Unit test: v0.4.0 subscriber on the same session is NOT included in `subscribers-for`; the watcher tee routes it to `broadcast-session-history!` instead (regression for AC2).
- [ ] Unit test: `current-sig` is computed by the watcher hook (T3 helper) ONCE per tick, not per subscriber.
- [ ] Integration test: append → push round-trip end-to-end on a fake connected-clients atom; iOS-side `payload.fileSignature` populated.
- [ ] Integration test (no chains): v0.5.0 reply NEVER carries `is_complete: false` (AC4 regression).

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC1, AC2, AC4, AC5.

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T6, T11, T14. (Depends on T3, T4, T5, T7.)

title: CoreData v6 -> v7 lightweight migration (lastOffsetMerged, liveFromOffset, lastFileSignature, offset)
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§6 R1 (CoreData lightweight migration) and §6 R2 (lastFileSignature)

## Context
v0.5.0 requires three new persistent fields on iOS: `CDBackendSession.lastOffsetMerged`, `CDBackendSession.liveFromOffset`, `CDBackendSession.lastFileSignature`, plus `CDMessage.offset`. A naive deploy would make every subscribe send `from_offset=0` (re-fetching every session) AND re-anchor `liveFromOffset=0` (re-speaking historical assistant messages via TTS). The fix is a lightweight v6→v7 migration that seeds the new fields from `max(0, oldField - 1)`. Plus `PersistenceController.init`'s existing `liveFromSeq` batch-reset is extended to also reset `liveFromOffset` so the migrated value is overwritten before the first reply lands, with the `payload.nextOffset > 0` gate in `handleSessionHistoryPayload` (T12) re-capturing the boundary.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create CoreData model version v7 of `VoiceCode.xcdatamodeld` set as the active model version.
- [ ] Add to `CDBackendSession`:
  - `lastOffsetMerged: Int64` (default 0, non-optional)
  - `liveFromOffset: Int64` (default 0, non-optional)
  - `lastFileSignature: String?` (optional, default nil)
- [ ] Add to `CDMessage`:
  - `offset: Int64` (default 0, non-optional)
- [ ] Define a compound index `(sessionId, offset)` on `CDMessage` — required for `purgeMessagesAtOrAbove` predicate performance AND the `(sessionId, offset)` upsert path.
- [ ] Keep existing fields (`nextSeq`, `liveFromSeq`, `seq`) for one App Store cycle, marked `#deprecated`. Per §6 R5 they ride along for rollback. v0.4.0 dispatch path reads them; v0.5.0 path ignores them.
- [ ] Lightweight migration mapping (in the v6→v7 mapping model or inferred):
  - `CDBackendSession.lastOffsetMerged = max(0, nextSeq - 1)`
  - `CDBackendSession.liveFromOffset = max(0, liveFromSeq - 1)`
  - `CDMessage.offset = max(0, seq - 1)`
  - `lastFileSignature` defaults to nil (server will populate on first reply).
- [ ] The `max(0, …)` clamp is load-bearing — `nextSeq=0` means "never received a reply" (Int64 default) and must map to `lastOffsetMerged=0` ("fresh subscribe from start"), NOT `-1`.
- [ ] Extend `PersistenceController.init`'s existing batch-reset of `liveFromSeq` (introduced by tmux-untethered-i2n) to ALSO batch-reset `liveFromOffset` to 0 on every app launch. This is what makes the migration mapping safe — the migrated value is overwritten before the first reply lands.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Models/VoiceCode.xcdatamodeld/` — add v7 xcdatamodel
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Models/CDBackendSession+CoreDataProperties.swift` (or auto-generated) — new properties
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Models/CDMessage+CoreDataProperties.swift` — new property
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Persistence/PersistenceController.swift` — `liveFromOffset` reset hook
- Lightweight migration is preferred over a custom mapping model — the field additions and `max(0, oldField - 1)` mapping should be expressible via Apple's inference rules + a small custom NSEntityMigrationPolicy if inference can't handle the `max(0, x-1)` expression. If inference fails on the `max(0, x-1)`, write a custom `NSEntityMigrationPolicy` subclass that overrides `createDestinationInstances`.
- Dependencies: independent of all server work; can ship in parallel with T1-T10. T12 (handleSessionHistoryPayload v5) depends on this for the new fields to exist.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: open an in-memory store with v6 schema, populate sessions with various `nextSeq` values (including 0, 1, 50), trigger v7 migration, assert `lastOffsetMerged` values are `[0, 0, 49]`.
- [ ] Unit test: `liveFromSeq = 5` → `liveFromOffset = 4` after migration; then `PersistenceController.init` resets it to 0.
- [ ] Unit test: `CDMessage.seq = 100` → `CDMessage.offset = 99` after migration; row count unchanged.
- [ ] Unit test: `lastFileSignature` is nil for all sessions post-migration.
- [ ] Migration test against a snapshot of a real v6 store (harvested from a TestFlight build): no crash, no row loss, every session is queryable.
- [ ] Verify compound index `(sessionId, offset)` exists by inspecting the SQLite store schema.
- [ ] Verify rollback: with the v0.4.0 dispatch path still active, `nextSeq` is still readable and consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC3 (v0.5.0 path reads `lastOffsetMerged`) and AC7 (lightweight migration completes on real v6 data).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: all backend tasks (T1-T10), T6, T14. T12 depends on this.
notes:
## Gap fix 2026-05-12 — v6 fixture sourcing (flag for Travis)
AC7 (lightweight migration succeeds against a real v6 store) requires a real v6 `.sqlite` fixture committed to the test bundle. The current iOS test suite does not appear to ship one. Options:

1. Capture one off a TestFlight build at v6 (preferred — represents real user state including any drift from manual schema tweaks).
2. Synthesize one by booting the app at v6 schema via a one-off branch checkout, exercising the populate path, then copying `Library/Application Support/.../*.sqlite{,-wal,-shm}` into the test bundle.
3. Build a synthetic v6 SQLite directly via a one-off seeder script.

**Decision required from Travis before this task can verify AC7.** Default if no answer: option 2, on the grounds that it produces a schema-faithful fixture without exposing real user content. Flag this in INFLIGHT once decided so future agents don't re-litigate.
close_reason: CoreData v7 schema + lightweight migration + post-migration backfill landed with full test coverage. AC1-6 satisfied; AC7 (real-v6 TestFlight fixture) deferred pending Travis decision per task notes.

title: Implement v0.5.0 subscribe handler with file-signature mismatch branch
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.3 (subscribe handler sketch) and §6 R2 (file-signature mismatch path)

## Context
The subscribe handler at server.clj:1653 today is ~165 lines of seq-based dispatch — `last-seq-val`, `last-message-id`, `build-session-history-response`, the pruned/client-ahead/caught-up/packed cases, `assign-seq!`. The v0.5.0 arm collapses to ~50 lines: read `from_offset`, optional `file_signature_seen`, call `compute-file-signature` (T3); if signatures mismatch, emit the `file_replaced: true` R2 envelope; otherwise call `read-from-offset` (T4) and emit the message slice. Dispatch is gated on `(channel-protocol channel) = :v0.5.0` from T7. The v0.4.0 arm at server.clj:1656-1820 stays UNTOUCHED for one App Store cycle.

## Requirements
- [ ] Wrap the existing subscribe handler body in `(case (channel-protocol channel) :v0.4.0 ... :v0.5.0 ...)`. The `:v0.4.0` arm calls into a `handle-subscribe-v0-4-0` function holding the existing body verbatim.
- [ ] Implement the `:v0.5.0` arm per the §3.3 sketch:
  - Read `:session-id`, `:from-offset` (default 0), `:file-signature-seen` (nilable) from the data map.
  - Validate `:session-id` present; validate `:from-offset` is a non-negative integer; reject with `{:type "error" :code "invalid_subscribe"}` otherwise.
  - Log the subscribe attempt with `{:protocol :v0.5.0 :from-offset N :file-signature-seen sig}`.
  - Call `repl/subscribe-to-session!`, add `session-id` to the channel's `:subscribed-sessions`.
  - Look up session metadata; if missing, send the existing "Session not found" error.
  - Send `available_commands` for the working directory (existing helper).
  - Compute `current-sig` via `compute-file-signature` (T3).
  - If `sig-seen` is non-nil AND `(not= sig-seen current-sig)`: emit the R2 reply `{:type :session-history :session-id ... :messages [] :next-offset 0 :end-of-file true :file-replaced true :file-signature current-sig}`. Emit metric `(emit-metric! :counter :replication.file-replaced {:session-id session-id})`. Reset the per-channel cache `[:session-offsets session-id]` to 0.
  - Otherwise: compute `max-bytes` via `get-client-max-message-size-bytes`, derive a `line-limit` via a new `estimate-line-limit` helper, call `read-from-offset`, run `truncate-budget` (existing or new helper that mirrors `pack-within-budget`), tag each message with `:session-id`, emit `{:type :session-history :session-id ... :messages [...] :next-offset N :end-of-file? bool :file-signature current-sig}`. Seed `[:session-offsets session-id]` to the returned `next-offset` — this is what lets `push-to-subscribers!` (T10) do per-subscriber tailoring.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- The existing handler body at server.clj:1656-1820 is extracted (NOT modified) into a private `handle-subscribe-v0-4-0` function. The wrapper `(case ...)` calls it for the v0.4.0 arm.
- `estimate-line-limit` is a new helper: given `max-bytes`, return a conservative line count that fits (e.g. `(quot max-bytes avg-line-size)`); the final `truncate-budget` handles the precise cap.
- `truncate-budget` either reuses `pack-within-budget` (existing) verbatim or is a thin wrapper renamed for the offset path.
- The reset-`:session-offsets` write on the R2 branch is critical: without it, the next push would re-slice from offset 0 and re-flood the client.
- Dependencies: T3 (compute-file-signature), T4 (read-from-offset), T7 (channel-protocol dispatch).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: fresh subscribe with `from_offset=3` → reply has `next_offset=N, end_of_file=bool`, each message has `:session-id` and `:offset`, and `[:session-offsets session-id]` is seeded to `next-offset`.
- [ ] Unit test: invalid `from_offset` (non-integer, negative) → `{:type "error" :code "invalid_subscribe"}` reply.
- [ ] Unit test: `session_id` missing → existing "session_id required" error.
- [ ] Unit test: `session-id` unknown → existing "Session not found" error.
- [ ] Unit test (R2 mismatch): subscribe with `file_signature_seen` ≠ current signature → reply has `messages=[], next_offset=0, end_of_file=true, file_replaced=true, file_signature=<new>`. Metric `:replication.file-replaced` emitted. `[:session-offsets session-id]` reset to 0.
- [ ] Unit test: subscribe with matching `file_signature_seen` → normal slice (no `file_replaced`).
- [ ] Integration test: v0.4.0 channel still hits the legacy handler (regression guard via outer `case` dispatch).
- [ ] Integration test: NO `is_complete` field on any v0.5.0 reply (AC4).

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC1, AC4, AC5, AC8.

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T5, T10, T6, T11, T14. (Depends on T3, T4, T7.)
notes:
## Gap fix 2026-05-12 — v0.3.0 sub-case + truncate-budget reuse
Two clarifications for the implementation:

1. **v0.3.0 sub-case rides inside the v0.4.0 arm.** The per-channel dispatch in T7 produces only `:v0.4.0` or `:v0.5.0`. The legacy v0.3.0 vs v0.4.0 distinction is preserved inside the v0.4.0 handler via the existing `v0-4?` switch (server.clj subscribe path) — no new branch is needed in this task. Only the `:v0.5.0` arm is new code.

2. **`truncate-budget` is the existing `pack-within-budget` helper.** Do NOT introduce a new packer. Reuse the existing budget-truncation function used by `broadcast-session-history!` (renamed-in-place is fine, but the algorithm — pack messages until the next would exceed max-bytes, then stop — is unchanged). This keeps the v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 paths bound to one well-tested packer.

title: Honor VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0 as server-side negotiation ceiling
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§6 R5 (rollback plan) and §3.2 (server-side ceiling)

## Context
The hard-rollback surface is `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0` plus `make backend-restart`. This caps the negotiation outcome at v0.4.0 for every channel regardless of what the client announces — clients announcing 0.5.0 are silently downgraded (NOT rejected; rejecting would lock every v0.5.0 iOS install out). Earlier doc revisions had this as an inverted-floor; pass-2 reframes it as a per-connect ceiling read by the `min(client, server-max)` step.

## Requirements
- [ ] On server start, read `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL` env var. If unset OR set to anything other than `"0"`, leave `server-max-protocol-version` at `:v0.5.0`. If set to `"0"`, set it to `:v0.4.0`.
- [ ] The negotiation step in the connect handler (T7) reads this global and applies `min(client-version, server-max-protocol-version)`. Under the rollback ceiling, a v0.5.0-announcing client gets `:v0.4.0` assigned to its channel.
- [ ] DO NOT extend `enforce-protocol-version!` to reject v0.5.0; the variable's effect is purely the ceiling on negotiation, not a new floor.
- [ ] The existing v0.4.0/v0.3.0 reject-if-too-old behavior at server.clj:623-635 stays unchanged by `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0`. Only the ceiling moves.
- [ ] Log a clear startup banner when the rollback ceiling is active (e.g. `WARN VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0 set; negotiation ceiling pulled to :v0.4.0`) so operators can confirm via logs that the rollback took effect.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- The env var read happens once during `start-replication-system!` (or wherever globals are initialized). It is NOT re-read per connection — operators must `make backend-restart` to flip it.
- The startup banner should be `log/warn` not `log/info` so it's hard to miss in a routine logs review.
- Dependencies: T7 (negotiation ladder needs to exist before the ceiling has a place to plug in).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: with `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL` unset, a v0.5.0-announcing client negotiates `:v0.5.0`.
- [ ] Unit test: with `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0`, a v0.5.0-announcing client negotiates `:v0.4.0` (silent downgrade, no error).
- [ ] Unit test: with `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0`, a v0.4.0-announcing client still negotiates `:v0.4.0` (no change in steady state).
- [ ] Unit test: with `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0`, below-floor clients still rejected by `enforce-protocol-version!` (floor unchanged).
- [ ] Manual verification: `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0 make backend-restart` shows the warn-level banner in `backend/server.out`.

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC1 (rollback half of the dual-protocol matrix). No direct AC for the env var itself; it's safety infrastructure.

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T1-T6, T11, T14. (Depends on T7.)

title: Add per-channel :negotiated-protocol-version selection on connect
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.2 (per-channel protocol selection) and §6 R1 (rollback plan)

## Context
The current `seq-migration-enabled?` gate (server.clj:140-144) flips the protocol server-wide via the `message-stream-version` atom — there is no per-channel selection. Carrying that forward means an in-the-wild v0.4.0 client (on the App Store while v0.5.0 is in review) cannot connect to a v0.5.0-flipped server. The redesign keeps the existing `hello`/`connect` handshake shape but adds per-channel `:negotiated-protocol-version` driven by `connect.protocol_version`, AND emits the negotiated value back to the client so iOS knows which wire shape to use (otherwise a rollback-induced silent downgrade leaves iOS sending the wrong subscribe shape).

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `server-max-protocol-version` global (atom or def) defaulting to `:v0.5.0`. The `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0` rollback lowers this cap (handled in T8).
- [ ] Keep the existing `message-stream-version` atom as the floor (lowest accepted protocol) and as the default when client omits `protocol_version`.
- [ ] Bump server's `hello` reply (server.clj:2749) to advertise `"version": "0.5.0"` (the server's preferred version; client may negotiate lower).
- [ ] Add per-channel field `:negotiated-protocol-version` ∈ `#{:v0.4.0 :v0.5.0}` on `connected-clients[channel]`. Set it in the connect handler (server.clj:1583-1605) immediately after `enforce-protocol-version!` returns true, according to this ladder (first match wins):
  1. Client omits `protocol_version` → use the global floor (today: `:v0.4.0`).
  2. Client announces a version below the floor → reject via existing `enforce-protocol-version!` (unchanged).
  3. Client announces ≥ floor → negotiate `min(client-version, server-max-protocol-version)`.
- [ ] **Emit `negotiated_protocol_version` back to the client.** Two equally-acceptable shapes — pick one and document in the handler:
  - Option (i): extend the existing connect-success response (whatever the server sends today after `enforce-protocol-version!` passes — `welcome`, `connect_ack`, status frame) with a `negotiated_protocol_version: "0.5.0"` (or `"0.4.0"`) field.
  - Option (ii): emit a new dedicated `{:type "connect_ack" :negotiated_protocol_version "..."}` frame immediately after connect-success.
  Whichever is chosen, iOS (T6) reads the value to decide which wire shape to send on subscribe and decode on inbound `session_history`. Without this, a `VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0` silent downgrade (T8) leaves iOS sending v0.5.0 subscribe shape against a v0.4.0-only server arm.
- [ ] Add helper `(channel-protocol channel)` → `:v0.4.0` or `:v0.5.0` that reads `(get-in @connected-clients [channel :negotiated-protocol-version])`.
- [ ] **Audit all `(seq-migration-enabled?)` call sites and migrate per-connection sites to `(channel-protocol channel)`.** Confirmed sites from the design doc: subscribe handler (server.clj:1653), push path, hello error envelope. Run `grep -n 'seq-migration-enabled?' src/voice_code/*.clj` to confirm the full list before changing. Document the audit result inline in this task's notes.
- [ ] **Exception — startup-time call at `server.clj:2946` STAYS on the global atom.** It runs inside `start-replication-system!` pre-connection and has no channel context. Add a one-line comment at the call site documenting that this is intentional and not a missed migration.
- [ ] `enforce-protocol-version!` (server.clj:623-635) keeps its reject-if-too-old job; do NOT extend it into a too-new reject. The new ladder logic lives in the connect handler, not in `enforce-protocol-version!`.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- The connect handler today reads `connect.protocol_version` (server.clj:1583) and feeds it into `enforce-protocol-version!`. After that returns true, compute the negotiation result, `(swap! connected-clients assoc-in [channel :negotiated-protocol-version] negotiated-ver)`, AND emit the negotiated value back on the wire (per the option chosen above).
- The `:negotiated-protocol-version` field is the single source of truth read by everything downstream.
- Dependencies: none (pure server-side dispatch infrastructure); T8 layers the env-var ceiling on top; T6 consumes the wire-emitted value.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: client omits `protocol_version` → channel gets `:v0.4.0`.
- [ ] Unit test: client announces `"0.4.0"` against a `:v0.5.0`-cap server → channel gets `:v0.4.0`.
- [ ] Unit test: client announces `"0.5.0"` against a `:v0.5.0`-cap server → channel gets `:v0.5.0`.
- [ ] Unit test: client announces a below-floor version → `enforce-protocol-version!` rejects (existing behavior preserved).
- [ ] Unit test: `(channel-protocol channel)` returns the persisted value.
- [ ] Unit test: connect response emits `negotiated_protocol_version` field; the value matches what `(channel-protocol channel)` returns.
- [ ] Unit test: `hello` reply advertises `"version": "0.5.0"`.
- [ ] Audit: `grep -n 'seq-migration-enabled?' src/voice_code/*.clj` shows only the `server.clj:2946` startup call site after migration; all other call sites converted to `(channel-protocol channel)`.
- [ ] Integration test: two channels open simultaneously with different `protocol_version` values; each gets its own correct negotiated value AND receives its own correct `negotiated_protocol_version` in the connect response. (This is half of AC1.)

## Acceptance Criteria
Contributes to AC1 (per-channel negotiation), AC2 (dispatch boundary for v0.5.0 vs v0.4.0 `assign-seq!` reachability), AC4 (`is_complete: false` only on v0.4.0 arm).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T1-T6, T11, T14. (T8, T9, T10, T13 depend on it.)
close_reason: Per-channel :negotiated-protocol-version on connect implemented: server-max cap atom, negotiate-channel-protocol-version helper with §3.2 ladder, channel-protocol helper, hello reply advertises cap, connect reply carries negotiated_protocol_version, subscribe/broadcast dispatch on per-channel state. Startup migration retains global atom (documented). 14 protocol-version tests pass, 19 gate-predicate assertions pass.

title: Add iOS WireMessageV5 + SessionHistoryPayloadV5 sibling structs + decoder routing
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.5 (WireMessage struct delta) and §3.2 (per-channel protocol routing)

## Context
The iOS client must decode BOTH v0.4.0 and v0.5.0 `session_history` payloads during the dual-protocol rollback window (one App Store cycle). Pass-2 of the design rev makes this an explicit sibling-struct addition: `WireMessage` (v0.4.0) stays byte-for-byte unmodified; new `WireMessageV5` and `SessionHistoryPayloadV5` are added; the deserialization layer in `VoiceCodeClient` routes incoming JSON to one or the other based on the channel's `:negotiated-protocol-version` as reported by the server's connect response.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `WireMessageV5: Codable, Equatable` to `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/MessageStreamTypes.swift`:
  - Fields: `sessionId, offset: Int64, role, text, uuid, timestamp: Date`.
  - `CodingKeys`: `session_id`, `offset`, `role`, `text`, `uuid`, `timestamp`.
  - Custom `init(from decoder:)` parsing ISO-8601 via `MessageStreamDateFormat.parse`, throwing `DecodingError.dataCorruptedError` on malformed timestamps (mirror of `WireMessage`'s existing impl line-for-line).
  - Custom `encode(to:)` formatting via `MessageStreamDateFormat.format`.
- [ ] Add `SessionHistoryPayloadV5: Codable`:
  - Fields: `sessionId, messages: [WireMessageV5], nextOffset: Int64, endOfFile: Bool, fileReplaced: Bool?, fileSignature: String?`.
  - `CodingKeys`: `session_id`, `messages`, `next_offset`, `end_of_file`, `file_replaced`, `file_signature`.
  - Lenient decode pattern: copy `SessionHistoryPayload`'s custom `init(from decoder:)` (lines 106-144), swap `LenientWireMessage` for `LenientWireMessageV5` (a near-clone whose stored property is `offset`, not `seq`), drop `firstSeq/lastSeq/gap`, add `fileReplaced/fileSignature` via `decodeIfPresent`.
- [ ] Add private `LenientWireMessageV5` struct to the same file (analog of `LenientWireMessage`).
- [ ] **Consume `negotiated_protocol_version` from the server's connect response** (see T7 — server emits this field on the connect-success / `connect_ack` reply). Store it on the iOS-side per-channel state (e.g., on the WebSocket-client state object that mirrors the server's `connected-clients` per-channel record), with values `{.v0_4_0, .v0_5_0}`. The initial value before the connect-ack arrives is `.v0_4_0` (safe default — if iOS sends a subscribe before the ack lands, it serializes the v0.4.0 wire shape).
- [ ] Deserialization routing in `VoiceCodeClient`: on inbound `session_history` JSON, branch on the stored `negotiatedProtocolVersion` and decode as either `SessionHistoryPayload` (legacy) or `SessionHistoryPayloadV5` (new).
- [ ] DO NOT modify existing `WireMessage` or `SessionHistoryPayload` — they must stay byte-for-byte unchanged so the v0.4.0 path continues working.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Managers/MessageStreamTypes.swift` (add new structs)
  - `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift` (decoder routing + negotiated-version state, connect-ack consumer)
- Routing call site is the inbound message-type switch where `session_history` is decoded today; add the protocol-version check around the existing decode.
- The connect-ack handler is a new inbound message handler (or an extension of an existing post-connect message handler — depends on what the server emits in T7). Once the ack arrives, store the negotiated version on the per-channel state.
- Dependencies: T7 emits the negotiated version on the wire. T6 implementation can ship against a stub default (`.v0_4_0`) while T7 is in flight, but real routing depends on T7.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: encode → JSON → decode round-trip of `WireMessageV5` preserves all fields including timestamp.
- [ ] Unit test: decode v0.5.0 `session_history` JSON (server-emitted shape) → `SessionHistoryPayloadV5` with correct fields (including `file_signature`).
- [ ] Unit test: lenient decode skips one malformed `WireMessageV5` element but returns the others (parallels `LenientWireMessage` behavior).
- [ ] Unit test: `WireMessage` (v0.4.0) still decodes legacy payload shape byte-for-byte; regression guard.
- [ ] Unit test: deserialization router picks the correct struct given a channel's `negotiatedProtocolVersion`.
- [ ] Unit test: connect-ack message updates the per-channel `negotiatedProtocolVersion` state from `.v0_4_0` default to the server-reported value.
- [ ] Unit test: a subscribe issued before the connect-ack lands uses the v0.4.0 wire shape (safe default), and the next subscribe (after ack) uses the negotiated shape.
- [ ] Xcode build clean; no warnings on the new types.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC3 (v0.5.0 path uses offset-based struct exclusively) and AC8 (file_signature field is present on payload).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: all backend tasks (T1-T5, T8-T10), T11, T14. Soft dep on T7 (server emits negotiated version) — can ship stubbed against `.v0_4_0` default until T7 lands.
close_reason: Implemented per design §3.5/§3.2: v5 sibling structs (WireMessageV5, SessionHistoryPayloadV5, LenientWireMessageV5) added to MessageStreamTypes.swift, per-channel ProtocolVersion state + reset-on-disconnect + connect-ack/connected consumer + session_history dispatch routing added to VoiceCodeClient.swift. v0.4.0 types untouched.
