title: Add parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw + stamp-offsets watcher pipeline
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.4 (point 2, Option a) and §3.3 (push-to-subscribers! input contract)

## Context
The watcher push path needs a sibling reader that mirrors `parse-jsonl-incremental` byte-for-byte through `complete-vec` but exposes the raw-line vector instead of throwing it away. The current `parse-jsonl-incremental` collapses `parsed-with-nils` via `(filter some?)` at replication.clj:1199, so the raw line strings are unreachable downstream. `parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw` + `stamp-offsets` is the watcher-facing v0.5.0 replacement for `assign-seq!`. The v0.4.0 path keeps calling `parse-jsonl-incremental → assign-seq! → broadcast-session-history!` untouched throughout the rollback window.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw` to `replication.clj` (sibling of `parse-jsonl-incremental`, not a replacement).
- [ ] Return shape: `{:raw-lines complete-vec :new-pos new-pos}` (mirrors `parse-jsonl-incremental`'s byte-position semantics).
- [ ] Same byte-level hold-back as `parse-jsonl-incremental` — trailing partial line is held back so the byte cursor only advances past complete newlines.
- [ ] Same shrink-recovery branch behavior (replication.clj:1121-1148 equivalent) — return a full-file snapshot when shrink is detected.
- [ ] Add `stamp-offsets` helper: `(stamp-offsets provider complete-vec pre-line-count)` → vec of canonical messages with `:offset = (+ pre-line-count i)` where `i` is the RAW-line index (NOT a survivor index). Filtered-out lines still advance `i`.
- [ ] Empty-snapshot case: when every raw line in the tick is filtered out, `stamp-offsets` returns `[]` but the watcher still calls `push-to-subscribers!` with the empty snapshot, `snapshot-from-offset = pre-line-count`, `file-end-line-count = (+ pre-line-count (count complete-vec))` so caught-up subscribers see `end-of-file? true` and `next-offset = file-end-line-count`.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- Watcher integration: `process-watch-events` / `handle-file-modified` at replication.clj:1774 currently flows `parse-jsonl-incremental → assign-seq! → broadcast-session-history!`. The new model tees: the v0.4.0 branch continues to use the existing pipeline; the v0.5.0 branch calls `parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw → stamp-offsets → push-to-subscribers!`. Per the design doc, the tee is preferred over modifying `parse-jsonl-incremental` in place (Option a beats Option b).
- The `pre-line-count` for `stamp-offsets` comes from `(get @line-counts file-path 0)` (T2 atom) read BEFORE the swap that adds the new line count.
- Dependencies: T2 (line-counts atom for `pre-line-count`). Does not strictly depend on T1, since the watcher reader uses the existing byte-cursor pipeline; the raw-vec extraction is in this new function.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw` returns the same `:new-pos` as the existing `parse-jsonl-incremental` on identical input.
- [ ] Unit test: `:raw-lines` contains the newline-stripped strings for the lines advanced this tick.
- [ ] Unit test: trailing-partial-line is held back (not in `:raw-lines`); byte cursor reflects only the complete portion.
- [ ] Unit test: `stamp-offsets` against a vec with internal-message lines produces non-contiguous `:offset` values (parallels the T4 raw-line-stamping test).
- [ ] Unit test: empty-snapshot case — all-filtered tick returns `[]` from `stamp-offsets` without exception.
- [ ] Integration test: watcher tee produces correct input shape for `push-to-subscribers!` (verified by mocking the push and asserting the args).
- [ ] REPL verification: append lines to a fixture under `clojure_eval`, watch the atoms advance in lockstep.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC2 (v0.5.0 watcher path never calls `assign-seq!`) and AC4 (no `is_complete: false`).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T3, T4, T6, T7, T11, T14. (Depends on T2.)
close_reason: Implemented parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw + stamp-offsets sibling functions with 11 deftests / ~43 assertions. Watcher tee wiring lives in T10.

title: Add compute-file-signature helper (length:first-line-uuid)
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§6 R2 and §3.2 (file_signature_seen wire field)

## Context
The §6 R2 mitigation requires the server to attach a `file_signature` to every `session_history` reply. The signature is a tuple `(file-length, first-line-uuid)` encoded as `"{length}:{uuid}"`. It is the trigger for the file-replaced recovery: when a client subscribes with a `file_signature_seen` that no longer matches the live file, the server replies with `file_replaced: true` and the iOS client purges + re-subscribes from offset 0. This task adds the pure helper that computes the signature deterministically from disk.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `compute-file-signature` to `replication.clj` (or wherever the new replication helpers live).
- [ ] Signature shape: `"{file-length}:{first-line-uuid}"`. Length is the `File.length()` byte count. UUID is parsed from the JSONL's first newline-terminated line via `parse-jsonl-line` → `:uuid` field.
- [ ] Per-provider behavior:
  - `:claude` / `:copilot` — parse first line, extract `:uuid` from the canonical message; if first line lacks a UUID (rare; e.g. summary-only file), use the empty string sentinel `"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"` so the signature stays well-formed.
  - `:opencode` — directory-based; signature is `"{file-count}:{first-filename}"` (analog of the line-based shape).
  - `:cursor` — return nil; cursor sessions never carry signatures.
- [ ] Empty file → `"0:00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"` (length 0, sentinel UUID).
- [ ] Pure function — no atom mutation, no I/O side effects beyond the read.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- The first-line UUID extraction can reuse `parse-jsonl-line` + `providers/parse-message`, OR can pull the UUID directly from the parsed JSON map without canonicalizing (cheaper, since we only need the field). Prefer the direct JSON parse — the signature is about raw file content, not canonical messages.
- Dependencies: independent of T1/T2; can ship in parallel.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: known fixture JSONL → known signature string; stable across re-reads.
- [ ] Unit test: appending a line changes the length component but preserves the first-line UUID.
- [ ] Unit test: in-place prefix rewrite changes the first-line UUID component (R2 trigger).
- [ ] Unit test: empty file returns the sentinel signature.
- [ ] Unit test: OpenCode directory variant produces the file-count + first-filename shape.
- [ ] Unit test: `:cursor` provider returns nil.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC8 (file_signature mismatch path).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T1, T2, T6, T7, T11, T14.

title: Add read-from-offset (per-provider dispatch, raw-line stamping, client-ahead clamp)
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.1 (read-from-offset sketch) and §3.3 (subscribe handler)

## Context
`read-from-offset` is the load-bearing pure function on the server side: given a provider, file path, raw-line offset, and line limit, it returns the canonical messages from that point with `:offset` stamped on each. Replaces both `parse-session-messages → assign-seq!` (subscribe path) and the seq-shim at server.clj:1722. The subscribe handler and the backfill branch of `push-to-subscribers!` both call it.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `read-from-offset` to `replication.clj`.
- [ ] Signature: `(read-from-offset provider file-path from-offset line-limit)` → `{:messages [...] :next-offset Int :end-of-file? Bool}`.
- [ ] JSONL providers (`:claude`, `:copilot`):
  - Call `parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe` to get `{:lines :held-back?}`.
  - Slice raw lines `[from-offset, from-offset+line-limit)` (clamped to `total-lines`).
  - For each raw line: `parse-jsonl-line` → `providers/parse-message provider`; survivors stamped with `:offset = (+ from-offset i)` where `i` is the RAW-line index, NOT the post-filter index.
  - `next-offset` clamp rule: if `from-offset > total-lines` (client ahead), return `next-offset = total-lines` (NOT `from-offset`) — this is what lets the client detect `next-offset < from-offset` and reset.
  - `end-of-file?` true iff `end-idx >= total-lines AND (not held-back?)`.
- [ ] OpenCode (`:opencode`): slice the filename-sorted message-file list at `from-offset`; stamp `:offset = position-in-list`.
- [ ] Cursor (`:cursor`): return `{:messages [] :next-offset from-offset :end-of-file? true}` unconditionally.
- [ ] No inline sidechain/summary/system re-filter — `providers/parse-message :claude` (providers.clj:265-280) is the single authority for the canonical drop set.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- Calls `parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe` (T1) — strictly dependent on it for JSONL providers.
- The `with-offsets` pipeline is the `map-indexed` + `when-let` chain spelled out in the design doc §3.1.
- Two notes on the existing filter surface (per design doc): (i) `replication.clj` has TWO `filter-internal-messages` defs (line 291 dead, line 969 live) — implementers must grep callers and only touch the live one; (ii) `filter-internal-messages` is a strict subset of `parse-message`'s drop set, so v0.5.0 relies on `parse-message` alone.
- Dependencies: T1 (parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `from-offset=0 limit=10` against 7-line file → 7 messages, `next-offset=7`, `end-of-file? true`.
- [ ] Unit test: `from-offset=0 limit=5` against 7-line file → 5 messages, `next-offset=5`, `end-of-file? false`.
- [ ] Unit test: `from-offset=7 limit=10` against 7-line file → 0 messages, `next-offset=7`, `end-of-file? true`.
- [ ] Unit test (client-ahead clamp): `from-offset=100 limit=10` against 7-line file → 0 messages, `next-offset=7`, `end-of-file? true`.
- [ ] Unit test (raw-line offset stamping; the C-N5 regression): 10-line file with internal entries at raw lines 2 and 5 → 8 canonical messages with `:offset` values `[0,1,3,4,6,7,8,9]` (NON-CONTIGUOUS), `next-offset=10`.
- [ ] Unit test (partial-tail hold-back): file ending without `\n` excludes the last line, reports `end-of-file? false`.
- [ ] Unit test: OpenCode directory slice returns correct positions.
- [ ] Unit test: Cursor returns the empty/EOF stub.
- [ ] REPL verification via `clojure_eval` against a fixture file.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC1, AC2, AC4, AC5 (eliminates the windowed-chain shape; deterministic offsets).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T2, T3, T5, T6, T7, T11, T14. (Depends only on T1.)
notes:
## Gap fix 2026-05-12 — OpenCode reader reuse
The OpenCode (and Cursor) provider branches should explicitly reuse the existing file-walking helpers at `replication.clj:1029-1049` rather than re-implementing directory traversal. Concretely: lift the sorted-message-files derivation out of the current OpenCode session-history path and call it from `read-from-offset`; do not introduce a parallel directory walk. Keeps the canonical-drop ordering aligned with the v0.4.0 path during the rollback window.

title: Add line-counts atom + populate-line-counts! startup walk + tick + shrink hooks
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.4 (point 3a) and §3.1 (table row for `migrate-session-seqs!`)

## Context
The watcher needs a running raw-line count per file path so `stamp-offsets` can compute `pre-line-count` for the next tick. The existing `file-positions` atom tracks bytes, not lines; we add a sibling atom that mirrors its lifecycle (startup walk, lazy init on first-seen files, tick update, shrink reset). This is the deterministic counter that replaces the persisted `:next-seq` in the v0.5.0 push path.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `line-counts` atom to `replication.clj` (sibling of `file-positions`, same scope).
- [ ] Implement `populate-line-counts!` — startup pass that walks `session-index.edn`, opens each file once, counts newline-terminated lines (reusing the byte-scan from `parse-jsonl-incremental`), and seeds the atom.
- [ ] Wire `populate-line-counts!` into `start-replication-system!` parallel to (or replacing) `migrate-session-seqs!`'s startup invocation. `migrate-session-seqs!` itself stays as a no-op idempotent function per the §3.1 status table (KEEP-FOR-ROLLBACK).
- [ ] Lazy-init branch: watcher's first firing on an unseen file detects `:unset`, counts existing complete lines once, seeds the atom, and treats the entire current content as the pre-event snapshot.
- [ ] Tick update: after a successful `parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw`, `(swap! line-counts update file-path (fnil + 0) (count complete-vec))`.
- [ ] Shrink recovery: when `parse-jsonl-incremental`'s shrink branch fires (replication.clj:1121-1148), the watcher resets `line-counts[file-path]` to 0 in the same swap.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- New helper functions: `populate-line-counts!`, possibly `count-complete-lines` if it isn't already extracted from `parse-jsonl-incremental`.
- The startup walk is bounded by the size of `session-index.edn`; expected O(#sessions × file-size) once per boot.
- Dependencies: independent of T1 (line counting is a separate byte-scan from raw-line slicing); paired in `read-from-offset`/`push-to-subscribers!` callers downstream.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `populate-line-counts!` seeds the atom from a fixture `session-index.edn`.
- [ ] Unit test: lazy-init branch on a never-before-seen file produces the correct initial count.
- [ ] Unit test: tick update accumulates correctly across two simulated watcher ticks.
- [ ] Unit test: shrink recovery resets the entry to 0 and lets the next tick rebuild from a re-parse of the truncated file.
- [ ] Integration: confirm `populate-line-counts!` runs at startup under the existing `start-replication-system!` flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC2 (deterministic offset stamping replaces `:next-seq` counter).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T1, T3, T6, T7, T11, T14.
close_reason: Implemented line-counts atom, count-complete-lines/populate-line-counts!/ensure-line-count-initialized!/reset-line-count! helpers; wired startup walk into start-replication-system!; 11 new tests covering happy + edge cases (153/153 replication-test suite passing). T5 will wire the watcher call sites.

title: Add parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe reader with newline-terminator hold-back
description:
## Design Reference
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md#§3.1

## Context
The subscribe path needs a whole-file JSONL reader that mirrors `parse-jsonl-incremental`'s newline-terminator hold-back so torn last-lines never reach the client. The existing `parse-jsonl-file` (replication.clj:997-1009) uses `(line-seq rdr)` and will happily return a torn tail if a writer is mid-append. This task adds the sibling reader the new `read-from-offset` will call.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe` to `replication.clj` (alongside existing parse helpers).
- [ ] Read via `RandomAccessFile` with a single `readFully` so the slice is stable for the rest of the call.
- [ ] Scan backwards for the last `\n` byte; return only the prefix that ends in `\n`.
- [ ] Return `{:lines [..newline-stripped strings..] :held-back? bool}` — `held-back?` true iff bytes exist past the last `\n`.
- [ ] Handle empty file → `{:lines [] :held-back? false}`.
- [ ] Handle file with only a partial first line (no newline yet) → `{:lines [] :held-back? true}`.

## Technical Approach
The implementation is the byte-scan sketched in §3.1 of the design doc — open `RandomAccessFile`, `readFully` into a `byte-array`, scan backwards for `(byte \newline)`, slice into a UTF-8 String, split on `\n` (with `limit -1` so trailing empty isn't dropped), drop the trailing empty (the post-last-newline tail). Return the vector plus the `held-back?` flag.

- Files to modify: `~/code/mono/active/voice-code-offset-protocol/backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: none (pure leaf primitive)

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: 7-line file with trailing newline → `:lines` has 7 entries, `:held-back? false`.
- [ ] Unit test: 7 full lines + 1 partial (no trailing newline) → `:lines` has 7 entries, `:held-back? true`.
- [ ] Unit test: empty file → `:lines = []`, `:held-back? false`.
- [ ] Unit test: file with only a partial line (no `\n` anywhere) → `:lines = []`, `:held-back? true`.
- [ ] Unit test: UTF-8 multi-byte content (emoji, non-ASCII) round-trips without corruption.
- [ ] Verify under nREPL with a small fixture file.

## Acceptance Criteria
Foundation for AC1, AC2, AC4 (none directly verified by this primitive; it's a building block).

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: T2 (line-counts atom), T3 (compute-file-signature), T6 (iOS WireMessageV5), T7 (per-channel negotiation), T11 (CoreData v7 migration), T14 (byte-prefix probe).

title: voice-code sync v0.5.0: Kafka-style offset protocol
description:
## Design Document
@/Users/travisbrown/assist/notes/voice-code-sync-kafka-redesign-2026-05-10.md

## Overview
Kafka-style refactor of voice-code's client-server state sync. JSONL file = topic, raw line index = offset, per-channel protocol negotiation allows v0.4.0 (seq-based) and v0.5.0 (offset-based) coexistence during the rollback window.

Eliminates assign-seq!/next-seq machinery, windowed is_complete chains, and the two-cursor iOS pattern (nextSeq + maxCachedSeq). Server becomes a stateless broker; iOS becomes a Kafka-style consumer storing a single integer offset per session.

## Scope
- Backend (Clojure): new offset-based replication path, sibling parse readers (parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe, parse-jsonl-incremental-with-raw), line-counts atom, per-channel :negotiated-protocol-version dispatch, VC_OFFSET_PROTOCOL=0 ceiling override, WireMessageV5 + SessionHistoryPayloadV5 wire types.
- iOS (Swift): CoreData v6 -> v7 lightweight migration adding lastOffsetMerged + fileSignature, file_replaced recovery branch in handleSessionHistoryPayload, sibling Codable structs preserving rollback wire-decode.
- Verification: dual-protocol matrix, byte-prefix compaction probe (resolves one OPEN item on claude --compact rewrite-vs-append semantics).
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Dual protocols exercised end-to-end; per-channel :negotiated-protocol-version selection verified (one client on v0.4.0 + one on v0.5.0 against the same session, both converge).

AC2: assign-seq! never reached on v0.5.0 dispatch path (verified by instrumentation/log absence).

AC3: CDBackendSession.nextSeq never read on v0.5.0 Swift dispatch path (verified by Xcode unit tests or symbol-level inspection).

AC4: is_complete: false never emitted on v0.5.0 history-response path (windowed-chain machinery dead on the new path).

AC5: Reconnect-after-drop passes with a single round-trip whose payload is <= 256 KB on v0.5.0 (no chained windows).

AC6: TTS gating preserved on v0.5.0; SmartSpeakingTests pass including the empty-first-reply variant (offset-based liveFromSeq latch behaves identically to seq-based).

AC7: CoreData v6 -> v7 lightweight migration completes on an app-launched device carrying real v6 data (lastOffsetMerged seeded from max(0, nextSeq - 1), fileSignature defaults nil).

AC8: file_signature mismatch path exercised end-to-end (force a signature change, observe purge -> reset offsets -> persist new signature -> save -> re-subscribe sequence).

title: iOS: restore nil-active-session TTS cancel after debugging spurious onDisappear
description:
Followup to tmux-untethered-0sz. Initial fix had observer cancel TTS on any active-session change including nil (nav-to-home / clearActiveSession). On-device test showed TTS no longer plays at all — strong signal that something is firing clearActiveSession spuriously during normal operation (likely a transient SwiftUI onDisappear during sheet presentation, partial swipe-back gesture, or view rebuild).

Shipped a defensive change that only cancels on transitions to a DIFFERENT non-nil active session. AC1 (cross-session nav stops TTS) and AC3 (same-UUID re-emit doesn't cancel) preserved; AC2 (nav-to-home stops TTS) deferred until we identify the spurious source.

WHAT TO DO: With diagnostic logs already added to handleActiveSessionChange (logs every fire and the inFlight/active values), capture device logs while reproducing 'open session, listen to TTS' on iPhone. Find what's firing clearActiveSession or the nil emission. Likely candidates: a sheet presentation flow, navigation transition, or scenePhase change. Once root-caused, restore the nil-cancel branch with a guard that excludes the spurious source.

Diagnostic log format: '🎯 handleActiveSessionChange: inFlight=<uuid> active=<uuid>' fires on every emission. '📍 [ActiveSessionManager] Setting/Clearing active session' fires on every state change.

ACCEPTANCE: AC2 from tmux-untethered-0sz restored — nav from a TTS-playing session back to home stops speech within ~100ms — AND on-device TTS still plays normally for new assistant messages.

title: Auto-approve Claude Code sensitive-file permission prompts in voice-code supervisor
description:
Claude Code agents launched under voice-code/tmux-agent occasionally hit a permission prompt when touching sensitive paths (e.g., ~/.claude/skills/<name>/). The prompt halts the agent indefinitely until a human picks an option:

  ❯ 1. Yes
    2. Yes, and always allow access to <path> from this project
    3. No

Today's incident: Travis had to manually intervene on tmux session 01e3e309-2837-467f-9ceb-ae53f7c36a2d (window 'session-01e3e3' on the personal laptop's 'assist' tmux session) to approve a 'mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/verify-then-build' operation. Voice-code should detect this prompt and auto-select option 2 (most permissive: 'always allow') so the agent can keep working autonomously.

PROPOSED APPROACH: voice-code's tmux pane watcher (or whatever monitors live agent output) gains a detector for the Claude Code permission-prompt format and, on detection, sends the appropriate keys via 'tmux send-keys' to select option 2.

DETECTION MARKERS (seen in the wild):
- A '❯ 1. Yes' line
- A '2. Yes, and always allow access to <path> from this project' line
- A '3. No' line
- An 'Esc to cancel · Tab to amend · ctrl+e to explain' footer

The detector should match this specific shape, not 'any TUI list with numbered options' — there are other numbered prompts in Claude Code's TUI that should not be auto-approved.

KEY-SEND: confirm what the Claude Code TUI accepts — likely Down + Enter, or '2' + Enter. Verify before shipping.

OPT-IN VS DEFAULT-ON: Travis's call. Default-on probably matches his preference since he already runs everything with --dangerously-skip-permissions; the auto-approve only fires on the prompts that bypass that flag.

RISK TO FLAG: auto-approving 'always' pollutes the project's permission allowlist with whatever path the agent happened to ask for. Probably acceptable given the existing --dangerously-skip-permissions posture, but it does mean the allowlist drifts based on agent activity rather than human intent.
acceptance_criteria: When a Claude Code agent running inside a voice-code-managed tmux pane hits the sensitive-file permission prompt (matching the markers above), voice-code automatically selects option 2 within a few seconds and the agent continues without human intervention. The behavior is robust enough not to fire on unrelated numbered TUI prompts.

title: iOS: testReconnectMidIsCompleteFalseChainSendsCursorFromPersistedSeq fails — newestCachedSeq returns nextSeq-1 not message-max
description:
Test at VoiceCodeClientReconnectTests.swift:189 asserts newestCachedSeq returns 5 (max message seq) after handleSessionHistoryPayload persists messages [1..5] with nextSeq=20. Actual return is 19 because newestCachedSeq (VoiceCodeClient.swift:1597) returns max(messageMax, nextSeq-1) and the session-level cursor wins (5 < 19).

Pre-existing failure on tmux-untethered as of HEAD 96a5e13a. Verified by stashing local changes and re-running the test on a clean tree — same failure.

Likely root cause: commit ec3caf10 (tmux-untethered-fkz, 'persist session_history nextSeq on CDBackendSession') taught newestCachedSeq to consult CDBackendSession.nextSeq-1 as an additional cursor source. The test in 7576b933 (tmux-untethered-4c0) was written before that change and assumed only message-row seqs counted.

Fix candidates (not part of this issue):
- Update the test's expected value to 19, since newestCachedSeq legitimately uses the session-level cursor to survive local pruning, and the reconnect resubscribe should use that (sending last_seq=5 would re-deliver chain rows 6..19 the server already sent us).
- Or assert against the wire payload's last_seq value (which the test does at line 214 — same broken expectation), independent of the cursor implementation.

Not blocking the in-flight work; filing for visibility.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: iOS: testReconnectMidIsCompleteFalseChainSendsCursorFromPersistedSeq fails after CDBackendSession.nextSeq persistence
description:
## Symptom

`VoiceCodeClientReconnectTests.testReconnectMidIsCompleteFalseChainSendsCursorFromPersistedSeq` (ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientReconnectTests.swift:128) fails on `main` and on `tmux-untethered`:

```
XCTAssertEqual failed: ("19") is not equal to ("5")
  - test setup: persisted cursor must equal last-received seq before reconnect
```

The failure is at line 189 — the test's setup sanity check, before any reconnect logic runs. Verified by stashing iOS edits and running the test on baseline; it still fails.

## Cause

Commit `ec3caf10` ("iOS: persist session_history nextSeq on CDBackendSession (tmux-untethered-fkz)") changed `newestCachedSeq` to return `max(highest message seq, CDBackendSession.nextSeq - 1)`. The test feeds a chain payload with `nextSeq: 20`, which is now persisted, so the cursor returns `19` (= 20 - 1) instead of the highest message seq `5`.

The test's intent — proving the cursor reflects the last received state — is still satisfied by `19`; that's a stronger correctness statement (the server told us its next assignment is 20, so 19 IS the last-received seq). The test assertion just needs to be updated.

## Fix

Two reasonable options:
1. Update the assertions at lines 189 and 214 to expect `19` (matching the new `next_seq - 1` semantic).
2. Re-write the test to use a payload where `nextSeq == lastSeq + 1` (i.e. caught up), so both sources of `newestCachedSeq` agree on the same value.

Option 1 better preserves the original test scenario (mid-chain, `is_complete: false`).

## Acceptance

- The test passes.
- The new assertion explicitly notes that `19 = nextSeq - 1` per the persistence change in tmux-untethered-fkz.

## Out of scope

Discovered while diagnosing tmux-untethered-a83. Not a regression from that work — this fails on baseline.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: iOS: drop default serverPort '8080' and gate isServerConfigured on port
description:
Fresh-install iOS clients silently dial <host>:8080 because AppSettings.swift:283 defaults serverPort to "8080" when nothing is in UserDefaults. In the field this hits unrelated services advertising stale protocol versions (e.g. 0.2.0), wasting a socket and confusing users with a protocol-version error before they ever reach Settings. isServerConfigured (AppSettings.swift:128) only checks serverURL, so the configure-me state is incomplete.

Fix: initialize serverPort to "" by default, extend isServerConfigured to require a non-empty (and numeric) port, and gate VoiceCodeClient.connect()/updateServerURL() on isServerConfigured so the app sits in a configure-me state instead of dialing strangers. Update tests to cover the empty-port path and the gated-connect path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. AppSettings serverPort defaults to empty string, not '8080'.
2. isServerConfigured returns false when serverPort is empty or non-numeric.
3. VoiceCodeClient.connect() and updateServerURL() short-circuit (no socket created) when isServerConfigured is false; surface a user-visible state.
4. Existing users with a saved serverPort in UserDefaults are unaffected.
5. Unit tests cover: empty-port default, isServerConfigured semantics, gated connect.
6. Existing iOS tests still pass.
notes: AppSettings.serverPort default → ""; isServerConfigured now requires numeric port; VoiceCodeClient.connect() short-circuits on unparseable/port-less URL with currentError; new AppSettingsTests + VoiceCodeClientTests cover fresh-install + bad-URL paths.
close_reason: Default serverPort flipped to empty; isServerConfigured requires numeric port; VoiceCodeClient.connect() gates on URL parsability + host + port. Tests added (6) and pre-existing tests pass. Two follow-ups filed: tmux-untethered-0lc (macOS configure-server empty state) and tmux-untethered-2xm (surface currentError outside ConversationView).

title: iOS: turn_complete fallback subscribe has same race as session_ready (now fixed)
description: The session_ready handler in VoiceCodeClient.swift was fixed in tmux-untethered-tj1 to gate auto-subscribe on ActiveSessionManager.isActive(). The 'turn_complete' fallback path immediately below it has the same unconditional subscribe pattern and the same race condition: if session_ready was lost or arrives after turn_complete (re-ordering), the fallback subscribes to a session the user may have already navigated away from, attaching pushes/TTS to an off-screen session. Fix: apply the same isActive() guard to the turn_complete handler in ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift around the 'Note: Subscription now happens earlier via session_ready message' branch.

title: Backend: on-session-created leaks session_updated to non-subscribers (same shape as 2yp)
description: server.clj:1258-1273 has the same per-channel-subscription gating bug that was fixed for broadcast-session-history! in tmux-untethered-2yp: it tests the global (repl/is-subscribed? session-id) flag and then iterates eligible-clients (filtered only by deletion) to push a session_updated envelope. A connected client that has not subscribed to this session can still receive its messages on the new-session race path. Fix: gate the inner doseq by per-channel :subscribed-sessions, mirroring the pattern in broadcast-session-history! (now fixed) and on-turn-complete.
close_reason: Per-channel :subscribed-sessions gate added to on-session-created session_updated push (mirrors broadcast-session-history! fix from 2yp). Regression test covers three subscription states (target/other/none) and confirms session_created envelope still fans out.

title: Backend: parse-copilot-events-incremental has same shrink-cursor bug as parse-jsonl-incremental
description: When a Copilot events.jsonl file shrinks below the tracked cursor (rare but possible if the file is rewritten externally or the session is edited), parse-copilot-events-incremental at backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj:1952 returns empty and never resets the cursor. Subsequent appends past the new EOF are silently skipped, the same data-loss class as tmux-untethered-df2 (which was fixed for the Claude path). Fix: detect current-size<last-pos, reset cursor to 0, log a warning. Mirror the implementation pattern from parse-jsonl-incremental.

title: Fix iOS: handleSessionHistoryPayload duplicates rows under concurrent calls for same session
description:
When two session_history payloads land for the same session at the same time on separate queues (e.g. subscribe reply crossing a live push, or two backfill requests dispatched in quick succession), upsertMessage performs a fetch-then-create against independent background contexts with stale snapshots. Both contexts miss the seqHit lookup for overlapping seqs and both insert new rows. Result: duplicate (sessionId, seq) pairs in CoreData, surfacing as duplicate bubbles in the UI.

Reproducer: tmux-untethered-5i7 added test_concurrent_payloads_for_same_session_produce_one_row_per_seq in ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerHistoryPayloadTests.swift. Fires payload A=seqs 6..10 and payload B=seqs 8..12 from two .global() queues. Final state observed: [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,8,9,9,10,10,11,12]. Expected: [1..12].

Likely fix: serialize handleSessionHistoryPayload per sessionId (per-session dispatch queue or per-session lock), so the seqHit fetch and insert happen in a single critical section against the persistent store. Adding a (sessionId, seq) uniqueness constraint is not viable because optimistic rows share seq=0.

The test is currently marked XCTExpectFailure pending this fix. When the fix lands, remove the XCTExpectFailure marker.
close_reason: Serialized handleSessionHistoryPayload per sessionId via per-session DispatchQueue + performAndWait so seqHit fetch and insert form a single critical section against the persistent store. Same-session payloads now serialize; cross-session parallelism preserved. Removed XCTExpectFailure from test_concurrent_payloads_for_same_session_produce_one_row_per_seq; all 27 SessionSyncManagerHistoryPayloadTests pass.

title: Test: iOS lightweight migration v3-to-v4 — existing rows survive with seq=0 default
description: Need a test that migrates a CoreData store from the v3 model (no seq attribute) to v4 and verifies all existing CDMessage rows have seq=0 after migration, no rows are dropped, and the store opens without error. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/CoreDataTests.swift.
close_reason: False positive: testLightweightMigrationFromV3AddsSeqDefault in ios/VoiceCodeTests/CoreDataTests.swift (line 642) already covers this exactly — migrates from v3 to v4, verifies seq=0 default on legacy rows, verifies rows are not dropped, and verifies the store opens without error.

title: Test: CoreData v4 seq attribute index coverage for maxCachedSeq fetch performance
description: No test verifies that the seq attribute in VoiceCode 4.xcdatamodel has adequate index coverage for the maxCachedSeq fetch (sort by seq DESC, fetchLimit 1). Need a test that verifies the fetch index bySessionAndSeq exists with seq in descending order and that a large dataset (10k+ rows) returns the correct max seq without a full table scan. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/CoreDataTests.swift.

title: Test: iOS activeSubscriptions ignores stale deleted sessions on reconnect
description: No test covers the case where activeSubscriptions contains a session that was deleted while the client was offline. Need a test that adds a session ID to activeSubscriptions, deletes the session from CoreData, then simulates reconnect and asserts the client does not attempt to subscribe to the deleted session. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientReconnectTests.swift.

title: Test: iOS subscribe() called before connect() — subscription buffered and sent after connect establishes
description: No test verifies behavior when subscribe() is called before the WebSocket is created. Need a test that calls subscribe() with isConnected=false, then simulates a hello+connected handshake, and asserts the subscribe message is sent during restoreSubscriptionsAfterReconnect (not silently dropped). File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientDeltaSyncTests.swift.

title: Test: nil vs missing protocol_version in connect message treated identically (both allowed)
description: No test distinguishes between {protocol_version: nil} and a connect message with no protocol_version key. Both should be treated as unknown/not-too-old and allowed through. Need a test that sends both variants when :message-stream-version is :v0.4.0 and asserts both result in a connected response (not an unsupported_protocol_version error). File: backend/test/voice_code/server_protocol_version_test.clj.

title: Test: subscribe with negative last_seq rejected with invalid_subscribe error code
description: No explicit test covers the negative last_seq validation path. Need a test that sends a subscribe message with last_seq=-1 and asserts the backend returns {type:error, code:invalid_subscribe} without crashing or subscribing the client. File: backend/test/voice_code/server_test.clj.
close_reason: False positive: the test already exists. test-subscribe-rejects-non-integer-last-seq in server_test.clj (line 1939) explicitly tests last_seq=-1 (via the doseq of bad values including "-1") and asserts the invalid_subscribe error code is returned.

title: Test: iOS concurrent handleSessionHistoryPayload for same session — no duplicate rows
description: No test fires two handleSessionHistoryPayload calls for the same session concurrently. Need a test that dispatches two payloads with overlapping seq ranges on separate queues simultaneously and asserts CoreData ends with exactly one row per seq, no duplicates. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerHistoryPayloadTests.swift.
close_reason: Added test_concurrent_payloads_for_same_session_produce_one_row_per_seq. Test exposes existing concurrency bug — duplicate rows for overlapping seqs under parallel handleSessionHistoryPayload calls. Marked XCTExpectFailure pending fix in tmux-untethered-prf.

title: Test: failed channel send — verify channel removed from all subscriptions after send failure
description: No test verifies that a dead channel is cleaned up after http/send! throws. Need a test that registers a channel with subscriptions, mocks http/send! to throw, triggers a broadcast, and asserts the channel is no longer present in connected-clients and is unsubscribed from all sessions. File: backend/test/voice_code/server_test.clj.
close_reason: Added test-cleanup-on-failed-channel-send (13 assertions) and the underlying fix in send-to-client! / broadcast-to-all-clients! that invokes unregister-channel! on send failure. Both tasks resolved together since the test requires the fix to pass.

title: Test: iOS socket disconnect mid is_complete:false chain — cursor survives reconnect
description: No test covers a WebSocket disconnect that occurs during a multi-step is_complete:false chain. Need a test that starts a chain, simulates disconnect after step 1, reconnects, and verifies the cursor sent on resubscribe matches the last-seq received before disconnect (not regressed to 0). File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientReconnectTests.swift.

title: Test: pack-within-budget re-subscribe cursor continuity — two consecutive windows cover full range without gaps or overlap
description: No test verifies that the cursor returned from an is_complete:false response can be used to re-subscribe and receive the next window with no gaps or duplicate seqs. Need a test that calls build-session-history-response twice — using the last-seq from the first response as the input to the second — and asserts the union of both windows covers all messages exactly once. File: backend/test/voice_code/server_test.clj.

title: Test: iOS pruned gap followed by user-triggered refresh — gap state cleared and new data accepted
description: No test covers the full pruned-gap recovery flow. Need a test that injects a pruned gap, verifies the gap flag is set and merges are blocked, then simulates a user dismiss and refresh, and asserts the gap flag is cleared and new session_history payloads are merged normally. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerPrunedGapTests.swift.

title: Test: subscribe to a session with zero messages — distinct from caught-up
description: No integration test covers subscribing to a valid session that has never received any messages (message-count=0, not pruned). Need a test that creates a fresh session entry in the index, subscribes with last_seq=0, and verifies the response is messages=[], is_complete=true, next_seq=1, gap=nil (caught-up on empty). File: backend/test/voice_code/server_test.clj or append_only_loopback_test.clj.

title: Test: iOS is_complete:false chain of 4+ windows with out-of-order delivery
description: The existing chain test only covers two payloads (one truncated plus one complete). Need a test that chains 4+ is_complete:false windows, delivers some out of order, and asserts the final CoreData state has all seqs present exactly once, cursors never regressed, and no duplicate rows. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerPlaybackTests.swift.

title: Test: build-session-history-response when :next-seq is missing from session metadata
description: No test covers the fallback path when get-session-metadata returns a map without :next-seq. Need a test that calls build-session-history-response with a nil next-seq and verifies the fallback calculation (inc of last-seq or 1) produces a valid and correct response. File: backend/test/voice_code/server_test.clj.

title: Backend: broadcast-session-history! with empty new-messages sends nil first-seq/last-seq to all subscribers
description: If the watcher parses a file tick and all lines are filtered (sidechain/summary entries only), the caller may still invoke broadcast-session-history! with an empty collection. (first []) and (last []) return nil, producing a payload with nil first-seq and last-seq sent to all subscribers. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj, broadcast-session-history!. Fix: guard at the top of broadcast-session-history!: if new-messages is empty, return immediately without broadcasting.
close_reason: False positive: broadcast-session-history! is only called from the watcher when new messages are non-empty. The Claude handler guards at replication.clj line 1811: (when (and (seq broadcast-messages) ...)), and the Copilot handler guards at line 1970: (when (seq new-messages) ...). There is no code path that invokes broadcast-session-history! with an empty collection.

title: Test: iOS seq=0 key collision for multiple concurrent optimistic messages
description: No test covers multiple optimistic messages all assigned seq=0 before any echo back. Need a test that creates two optimistic CDMessage rows with seq=0 in the same session, then echoes both back with seq=1 and seq=2, and asserts both rows are correctly reconciled with no orphaned seq=0 rows remaining. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerHistoryPayloadTests.swift.

title: Test: concurrent watcher ticks on same JSONL file — verify no duplicate seq assignments
description: No test fires two watcher ticks on the same file concurrently. Need a test that spawns two threads both calling parse-jsonl-incremental and assign-seq! for the same session simultaneously and verifies the resulting seqs are contiguous with no duplicates. File: backend/test/voice_code/replication_test.clj.

title: iOS: WireMessage.seq has no fallback — one malformed message in a batch drops all messages silently
description: seq is decoded as required with no default. If one message in a batch has a missing or wrong-type seq field, decoding the entire SessionHistoryPayload fails and none of the messages in the batch are merged. A log entry appears but no user-visible error surfaces. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/MessageStreamTypes.swift, WireMessage. Fix: decode seq as optional with default 0, or decode messages individually and skip malformed ones rather than failing the whole batch.

title: Test: backend restart — verify next-seq re-derived correctly and watcher does not re-parse migrated content
description: No test covers the post-crash startup scenario. Need a test that simulates crash by resetting file-positions to 0, running migrate-session-seqs!, then simulating a watcher tick. Verify no duplicate seqs are assigned (the watcher should start from the end of the file, not from byte 0). File: backend/test/voice_code/replication_test.clj.
close_reason: Test added in replication_test.clj covers crash-restart scenario; migrate-session-seqs! now restores file-positions for Claude/Copilot byte-cursor providers.

title: iOS: nextSeq from SessionHistoryPayload never persisted — reconnect cursor wrong after local cache pruning
description: SessionHistoryPayload.nextSeq (the backend current next-seq) is never stored to disk. On reconnect, newestCachedSeq reads the max seq from CoreData. If the client cache was pruned locally (e.g. user cleared app data), the cursor resets to 0 and forces a full resync. Fix: add nextSeq: Int64? to CDBackendSession; update it on every payload received; use it as the preferred resubscribe cursor on reconnect.

title: Test: parse-jsonl-incremental cursor reset and recovery after JSONL file shrinks
description: No test covers the file-truncation/shrinkage case in parse-jsonl-incremental. Need a test that writes a JSONL file, advances the cursor, then truncates the file to a smaller size, then appends new content. Verify the cursor resets to 0, the old messages are not re-processed, and the new messages after truncation are correctly parsed. File: backend/test/voice_code/replication_test.clj.
close_reason: Test added for cursor reset on JSONL shrink (4ug); same change fixes the underlying bug (df2) by detecting current-size<tracked-pos and resetting last-pos to 0 with a warning log. Filed tmux-untethered-d9j for the same bug class in parse-copilot-events-incremental.

title: iOS: requestSessionRefresh unsubscribe/resubscribe 100ms gap insufficient — stale continuation resume risk
description: requestSessionRefresh unsubscribes, waits 100ms, then resubscribes. 100ms is not enough to guarantee the backend processed the unsubscribe before the subscribe arrives. A session_history from the unsubscribe epoch can arrive after the continuation is resumed, causing the refresh caller to see stale data. File: ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift, requestSessionRefresh. Fix: replace the time-based delay with a response-based handshake or generation counter to discard responses from old subscribe epochs.

title: Test: parse-jsonl-incremental with multi-byte UTF-8 character (emoji) split across two watcher ticks
description: No test covers a multi-byte UTF-8 character at the JSONL holdback boundary. Need a test that writes a JSON line ending with a multi-byte character where the character is split across two watcher ticks. Verify the cursor does not advance mid-character and the message is parsed correctly on the second tick. File: backend/test/voice_code/replication_test.clj.

title: Backend: protocol version enforcement silently disabled when config.edn is missing or malformed
description: If config.edn is missing or malformed, message-stream-version defaults to :v0.3.0, which disables protocol version enforcement. Old v0.3.0 clients connect without rejection and no warning is emitted. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj, enforce-protocol-version! and load-config. Fix: log a prominent warning at startup when :message-stream-version is :v0.3.0 or absent so operators know enforcement is disabled.
close_reason: False positive: load-config defaults to :v0.4.0 when config.edn is missing from classpath (line 100 of server.clj), and coerce-message-stream-version also falls back to default-message-stream-version which is :v0.4.0. The issue claims the default is :v0.3.0, but the code shows the opposite.

title: Backend: truncate-text-middle can overshoot byte budget when marker string is longer than estimated
description: The truncation marker size (e.g. [truncated ~88 KB]) is estimated once. For very large messages the KB count has more digits, making the marker longer than anticipated. The final assembled string (first-part + marker + last-part) can exceed max-bytes. File: backend/src/voice_code/server.clj, truncate-text-middle. Fix: after assembly verify total byte count is at or below max-bytes; if not, reduce half-bytes by the overage and retry.

title: Backend: UTF-8 multi-byte characters at JSONL holdback boundary can miscompute cursor position
description: Holdback byte count is computed as (count (.getBytes (last lines) UTF-8)). If the raw bytes end mid-character (e.g. first byte of a 4-byte emoji), String.(buf UTF-8) may produce a replacement character. Re-encoding the replacement gives a different byte length, placing the cursor in the wrong position on the next tick. File: backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj, parse-jsonl-incremental. Fix: detect the holdback in bytes directly by finding the last newline byte in the raw buf, rather than decoding to String first.

title: Backend: TOCTOU between file-size check and readFully in parse-jsonl-incremental
description: (.length file) is called before opening the RandomAccessFile. If new bytes are written between the length check and readFully, readFully reads fewer bytes than available. The missed bytes are recovered on the next watcher tick but causes inconsistent message batching. File: backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj, parse-jsonl-incremental. Fix: re-read raf.length() after seeking instead of using the pre-computed size.

title: End-to-end smoke test & ship-gate checklist (v0.4.0)
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#verification-strategy (End-to-end section)

## Context
Automated test layers (T18 unit, T19 backend loopback, T20 iOS XCTest)
cover the internal contracts well, but do not exercise the full stack
under realistic conditions: a live provider CLI writing the JSONL,
a real iOS device connecting over Wi-Fi, a socket flap in the middle
of an assistant turn. This task is the final signoff gate before the
backend flag flips to :v0.4.0 in production.

## Requirements
- [ ] Fresh-session scenario: open a new session on iOS, send 3 prompts
      back-to-back, observe all messages appear in order with no manual
      refresh. No duplicate bubbles. No stuck spinners.
- [ ] Mid-turn reconnect scenario: during an assistant response, toggle
      Wi-Fi off for 5-10 seconds and back on. Turn completes and renders
      without duplicate bubbles; seq values in CoreData are contiguous.
- [ ] Pruned-gap scenario (if tooling available): corrupt or truncate a
      session's JSONL server-side, then resubscribe from iOS. Verify the
      `didDetectPrunedGap` UI appears; verify iOS does NOT silently merge
      a partial view.
- [ ] Rollback drill: flip the backend config flag to :v0.3.0 with a
      pre-0.4.0 iOS build connected. Confirm legacy paths still work
      (last_message_id subscribe, session_updated push). Flip back to
      :v0.4.0 and confirm normal operation resumes.
- [ ] Protocol-refusal check: point a pre-0.4.0 iOS build at a v0.4.0
      backend. Confirm the client receives the exact
      `unsupported_protocol_version` error JSON, shows an upgrade-
      required UI, and does NOT enter a reconnect loop.

## Technical Approach
- No new code. Manual + scripted verification against a staging backend
  and a TestFlight build.
- Record results in a comment on this task (or a linked runbook) with
  device, iOS version, backend commit SHA, and flag state.
- Dependencies: all implementation and automated-test tasks must land
  first (T03, T04, T07, T08, T09, T15/bor, T16, T17, T18, T19, T20, T22).

## Verification
- [ ] All five scenarios above pass.
- [ ] No unexpected entries in backend/server.out during the run
      (grep SEVERE / ERROR / WARNING).
- [ ] Metrics counters (T22) show zero parse-failure-rate escapees
      during the run.

## Acceptance Criteria
Final validation of AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7 end-to-end. This task is
the signoff gate; the epic cannot close until it passes.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: iOS: Simplify reconnect path in VoiceCodeClient
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#ios-reconnect

## Context
With the seq-cursor in CoreData as the source of truth, reconnect is "re-subscribe everything in activeSubscriptions". No more special-cased branches or queued-update handling.

## Requirements
- [ ] `onConnected()` loops over `activeSubscriptions` and calls `subscribe(sessionId:)`
- [ ] No in-memory queue of missed pushes
- [ ] Deletion of any dead branches that handled "was I disconnected?" state

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift:573–593`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: T14, T16

## Verification
- [ ] XCTest: simulate a disconnect+reconnect cycle; assert subscribe fires with the correct `last_seq` pulled from CoreData

## Acceptance Criteria
AC4

title: iOS: Unified handleSessionHistory — gap contract + idempotent upsert + is_complete chain
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#push-backend-→-client

Also: @docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#ios-subscribe-and-merge

## Context
One code path for both history replies and pushes. 3-case seq comparison drives gap-backfill. Upsert keyed on `(sessionId, seq)` makes subscribe/push overlap safe.

## Requirements
- [ ] Implement the 3-case comparison: contiguous / gap (`first_seq > local_last_seq + 1`) → backfill subscribe / duplicate-or-reorder no-op
- [ ] Upsert by `(sessionId, seq)` so double-delivery is safe
- [ ] If `is_complete==false`, immediately re-subscribe with the advanced cursor
- [ ] If `gap.reason == "pruned"`, invoke T13's delegate

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/SessionSyncManager.swift:145–536`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: T11, T12, T13

## Verification
- [ ] XCTest covering each of the three gap cases
- [ ] XCTest: is_complete chain
- [ ] XCTest: upsert idempotency on same (sessionId, seq)

## Acceptance Criteria
AC3, AC4, AC6

title: iOS: subscribe(last_seq) + handle unsupported_protocol_version
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#ios-subscribe-and-merge

Also: @docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#breaking-changes

## Context
Subscribe wire message now carries `last_seq`. Hello handshake may return the new error; iOS must surface it rather than reconnect-loop.

## Requirements
- [ ] `subscribe(sessionId:)` reads seq via T14 and sends `{"type":"subscribe","session_id":..,"last_seq":N}`
- [ ] At connect time, parse `hello.version` and fail fast if unsupported
- [ ] On receipt of `{"type":"error","code":"unsupported_protocol_version"}`, set a user-visible "app upgrade required" state and stop reconnect attempts

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift:1118–1138` (subscribe), `:573–593` (onConnected)
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: T14

## Verification
- [ ] XCTest: captured subscribe payload contains `last_seq`
- [ ] XCTest: unsupported_protocol_version error transitions client into upgrade-required state without retrying

## Acceptance Criteria
AC4, AC7
notes:
## Optional split

This task bundles two logically separate concerns:
  (a) Change subscribe wire payload: send `last_seq` instead of
      `last_message_id`.
  (b) Handle the `unsupported_protocol_version` error at the hello
      handshake and transition the client into an "upgrade required"
      state.

They share a file (`VoiceCodeClient.swift`) and both belong to the
v0.4.0 cutover, so bundling is defensible. If the implementer finds
the combined scope awkward during claim, split into two tasks
(call the new one "iOS: handle unsupported_protocol_version error");
the existing dependency edges only need to track (a), since nothing
downstream depends on (b).

## Follow-up from tmux-untethered-fh3

When this task flips iOS dispatch so `session_history` / `session_updated`
messages route into `SessionSyncManager.handleSessionHistoryPayload`
(instead of the legacy `handleSessionHistory` / `handleSessionUpdated`
handlers), the new path will NOT reconcile optimistic messages:

- Optimistic rows live at `CDMessage.seq == 0` (client-generated).
- Upsert in `handleSessionHistoryPayload` keys on `(sessionId, seq)`.
- When the backend later confirms the user's prompt with `seq > 0`,
  the upsert inserts a new row instead of updating the `seq == 0`
  optimistic row. Result: two rows — the optimistic (status=sending)
  and the confirmed (status=confirmed) — for the same logical message.

Before swapping dispatch, either:
  (i) extend `upsertMessage` to first look up a matching optimistic
      row by `(sessionId, role, text, seq==0)` and promote that row
      (mirroring the reconciliation block at
      `SessionSyncManager.swift:436–453` in the legacy handler), or
  (ii) have the send path wait for the first push to arrive before
       materializing the optimistic row.

Surface this as a discovered requirement; it's not hypothetical — it

notes:
## Spike step (do this first)

Before editing the Core Data model, verify a lightweight migration is
feasible given the schema change:

- Add `seq: Int64` (default 0) to the existing `CDMessage` entity.
- Add compound attribute index `(sessionId, seq desc)`.

Lightweight migration should succeed when the change is purely additive.
If Xcode rejects it (e.g. the compound-index requires a model version
with a mapping model, or the default-value/optional interaction breaks
inference), STOP. Open a quick note on this task describing what Xcode
rejected, and flag the epic (tmux-untethered-5dv) so we can decide
whether to:

  (b) relax the index to a non-compound form and rely on fetch-request

Only proceed to implementation once the migration strategy is confirmed.

title: Fix parse-jsonl-incremental partial-line detection via newline terminator
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#code-examples

## Context
Current code advances the byte cursor after every read, so a half-written trailing line is silently dropped. Fix: hold cursor back only when the read buffer does not end in `\n` — nil-parsed legitimate non-message lines (sidechain / summary / system) still advance. This closes the partial-line data loss flakiness class identified in the design.

## Requirements
- [ ] Rewrite `parse-jsonl-incremental` per doc (newline-terminator-based holdback)
- [ ] Preserve existing `parse-jsonl-line` semantics (nil for non-message)
- [ ] Return `{:messages vec :new-pos n}`

title: Add :next-seq / :min-available-seq to session index + assign-seq! helper

## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#data-model

## Context
Sequence assignment lives in the session index so it survives backend restart. `assign-seq!` stamps `:seq` onto newly parsed messages and advances the counter in an atom keyed per session. This is the foundation for the append-only monotonic sequence number that replaces timestamp-based cursors.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `:next-seq` and `:min-available-seq` keys to the session-index entry schema
- [ ] Implement `assign-seq!` in `replication.clj` that takes `[session-id messages]`, stamps `:seq` starting at current `:next-seq`, advances the counter
- [ ] Counter changes flush via the existing `:last-modified` path — no new flush cadence

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj`
- New files to create: none
- Dependencies: none

## Verification

- [ ] Writes visible via existing index getter

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Append-only message stream with monotonic seq (protocol v0.4.0)
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md

## Overview
Collapse the two divergent message-delivery paths (session_history pull +
session_updated push) into a single append-only stream keyed by a monotonic
per-session sequence number. Client cursor becomes a single integer; reconnect
and mid-session sync stop relying on CoreData timestamps or UUID lookups.
Wire protocol bumps to v0.4.0.

Closes the five flakiness classes documented in the design: timestamp-drift
cursor, partial-line data loss, subscribe-vs-watcher race, reconnect losing
queued updates, and silent is_complete:false truncation.

## Acceptance Criteria
(full text: @docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md §Acceptance Criteria)

- AC1 — subscribe(last_seq=N) returns only messages with seq>N, ascending,
  contiguous; no UUID scan server-side.
- AC2 — watcher holds cursor back only on unterminated-tail (no trailing
  newline); nil-parse with newline advances fully.
- AC3 — concurrent subscribe + file-write: no gap in materialized state;
  duplicates absorbed by idempotent upsert on (session_id, seq).
- AC4 — reconnect resumes from CoreData-derived last_seq; no duplicate or
  missing bubbles.
- AC5 — gap.reason="pruned" surfaces via SessionSyncDelegate.didDetectPrunedGap
  (dormant path under this design; trigger is catastrophic state loss only).
- AC6 — is_complete:false chains an automatic follow-up subscribe.
- AC7 — client announcing <0.4.0 in hello receives exactly
  {"type":"error","code":"unsupported_protocol_version","required":"0.4.0",
   "received":"<v>","message":"Client is too old; upgrade required"} and
  the socket closes.
- AC8 — one-shot migration stamps seq onto existing JSONL messages in file
  order; next-seq + min-available-seq persisted in session index.

## Implementation notes
- Rollback flag :message-stream-version (:v0.3.0 | :v0.4.0).
- Coordinated release: iOS TestFlight >= 1 week before backend flip.
- Monitoring: parse failure rate (>0.1% alert), session-index write p99.
notes: Per-task parallelism is implicit in the bd dep graph — run `bd ready` to see what can proceed concurrently at any given moment, rather than reading a per-task `## Parallelization` list (which would drift from the graph over time). Foundation layer at kickoff: 8om, jzm, vmg, a5x, 7sr, e1r (all parallelizable, no blockers).

Adding a stress test with per-subscription sequence counters on both ends closes the verification gap.
