title: REPL harness slice 3b: WebSocketTransport (Network.framework)
description: Wrap NWListener + NWConnection with a WebSocket text-frame transport, loopback-only, bound to 127.0.0.1:19119. Feeds ReplServer.handle(request:connectionId:) and invokes the connection's send() closure with outbound ReplResponses. Port retry 19119..19129. Optional HUNT910_REPL_TOKEN via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol. All code #if DEBUG gated under hunt910/Testing/ReplServer/.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Accepts WebSocket client connection on 127.0.0.1. 2) Per-frame round-trip: client sends JSON, ReplServer.handle called, response frame delivered. 3) Port retry on EADDRINUSE. 4) Connection close invokes ReplServer.closeConnection. 5) #if DEBUG gated; not present in Release binaries.
notes: Design: specs/400-testing/407-agent-repl-testing-harness.md §Transport. ReplServer orchestrator is ready (commit 13e9f0e) and transport-agnostic.
close_reason: Slice 3b shipped: WebSocketTransport (NWListener+NWConnection+NWProtocolWebSocket) with loopback filter, port retry 19119..19129, double-start guard, loopback-only accept filter. WebSocketReplHost glues transport → ReplServer (JSON decode/encode, per-connection send closures, subscription teardown on close). Coverage: 7 WebSocketTransportTests (bind, retry, double-start, stop-is-idempotent, frame round-trip, close lifecycle, delegate rewire) + 6 ReplServerIntegrationTests (ping, command, meta, subscribe+push, disconnect cleanup, malformed frame tolerance). All DEBUG-only. Full make test clean.

title: feat: ReplServer orchestrator (slice 3a)
description: Wire CommandRegistry, QueryRegistry, and EventFanout into a single request router that turns ReplRequest -> ReplResponse. Per-connection subscription tracking. Honors meta/* endpoints using the existing catalogs. Covered by unit tests in hunt910Tests/Testing/ReplServer/ReplServerTests.swift. Transport and ReplServerFactory are out of scope; this slice only provides the message-level orchestrator that WebSocketTransport will drive.
notes: ReplServer orchestrator landed. Routes ReplRequest -> ReplResponse via CommandRegistry + QueryRegistry + EventFanout, with per-connection subscription tracking, meta/* endpoints sourced from the registries, and a UIDriver seam for ui/tap, ui/snapshot, ui/navigate (UnimplementedUIDriver stub keeps ReplServer testable ahead of slice 3c). 34 unit tests cover request dispatch, subscribe/unsubscribe lifecycle, connection lifecycle, event push envelope shape, UI routing, and error wrapping. Transport + ReplServerFactory remain out of scope (next slice).

title: Guard against prompt vs compaction race on JSONL
description: After tmux-untethered-2xl removed the per-prompt session lock, nothing prevents a prompt that arrives during an in-progress claude --compact from respawning a tmux window that reads from (and provider writes to) the JSONL file currently being rewritten by compaction. The compact_session handler kills the live window and synthesizes an aborted turn_complete before starting compaction, but tmux/deliver! on a subsequent prompt will respawn a fresh window. Options: (a) have tmux/deliver! check repl/is-compaction-locked? and defer or reject; (b) extend the compaction lock to gate prompt acceptance for that one session-id during compaction; (c) queue prompts until compaction releases. Needs a design decision.
notes: Identified during code review of tmux-untethered-2xl. Out of scope for that task (which was strictly 'remove per-prompt lock'). Acceptance: two tests — (1) a prompt arriving during compaction does not cause the provider to re-spawn and write to the JSONL while compact is running; (2) the prompt either errors cleanly or is queued, with a clear iOS-visible signal.
close_reason: Implemented compaction-lock check in prompt handler; rejects resume prompts during in-progress compaction with error+session_id. Tests in server_test.clj. Follow-ups filed: tmux-untethered-22g (residual TOCTOU), tmux-untethered-3eh (orchestrator path).

title: system_prompt field silently dropped in tmux-backed prompt handler
description: In tmux-untethered-ao0 the WebSocket prompt handler was switched from providers/invoke-provider-async to tmux/start-window! / tmux/deliver!. The old path threaded :system-prompt through to --append-system-prompt; the new tmux start-window! API has no slot for it. server.clj:~1310 still binds system-prompt from the message payload, but it's only logged — never applied. Fix: either remove the binding entirely and drop the protocol field, or plumb it through to tmux/start-window! and the provider launch command (claude --append-system-prompt <prompt>). The STANDARDS.md protocol doc still advertises system_prompt as supported for new sessions, so this is a functional regression until addressed.

title: Empirical Cursor turn-complete mtime sanity-check
description:
Validate §5.3 Cursor detection rule (mtime stable ≥3s AND last record role:"assistant") against a live cursor-agent TUI session.

The static source analysis in docs/provider-cli-reference.md §5.3 established that cursor-agent@2026.02.13-41ac335 writes agent-transcripts JSONL from TUI via CliTranscriptWriter on every handleCheckpoint. The mtime-debounce detection rule is derived from that, but has not been exercised against live output. Local Cursor auth was not available when §5.3 was drafted, so the live run is deferred.

Steps:
1. Authenticate cursor-agent locally (browser OAuth).
2. Start a TUI turn that exercises tool use (e.g. 'read this file, then summarize').
3. tail -f the transcript file at ~/.cursor/projects/<hash>/agent-transcripts/<uuid>.jsonl while the turn runs.
4. Record: (a) mtime cadence during the turn, (b) gap between the last burst and the user-visible turn end, (c) whether any mid-turn 3s gap occurs where last record is role:"assistant" (the pathological case called out in §5.3 Edge cases).
5. If findings invalidate the 3s threshold or reveal the pathological mid-turn case, update §5.3 detection rule and/or adopt one of the mitigations listed there (pane-output quiescence, record-count-unchanged check, higher threshold).

Relationship to tmux-untethered-44v: informs, does not block. 44v can land using the source-verified rule; 58q findings may trigger a follow-up adjustment to the detection rule or its thresholds.
close_reason: Closed without implementation — can be reopened if work resumes

title: Backend-restart continuity + eviction + manual-attach e2e tests (AC #2, #3, #4, #5, #10)
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §4 acceptance criteria #2, #3, #4, #5, #10. (#9 is already covered by `tmux/sweep!` and existing `tmux_integration_test.clj` coverage.)

## Context
Several acceptance criteria can only be verified by either an out-of-process restart or by exercising the WebSocket layer end-to-end with real eviction state. They are bundled here so the e2e harness is built once.

- AC #2 (backend-restart continuity) requires actually killing and restarting the JVM — cannot be done in-process.
- AC #3 (eviction at 4 windows + 15-min idle) and AC #4 (5 processing windows) are conceptually covered by `tmux/evict-if-needed!`, but no test asserts the WebSocket-level behavior (incoming `prompt` triggering eviction).
- AC #5 (evicted respawn with `--resume`) is covered by `tmux/respawn-and-deliver!` semantics, but should be exercised end-to-end via WebSocket and confirm no `session_resumed` message is emitted.
- AC #10 (manual `tmux attach` coexists with backend nudges) is fundamentally a manual step but should at least have a checklist documented in this task and a smoke script.

## Requirements
- [ ] **AC #2 (automated):** Implement as either a Clojure shell-driven e2e test or a bash test under `scripts/` that spawns the backend, drives it via WebSocket, kills it, restarts, and verifies tmux state.
  - Verbatim steps:
    > (a) create a session with UUID U in working directory W; wait for its start-window! to return.
    > (b) Record window-before = (-> @live-windows (get U) :tmux-window).
    > (c) Kill and restart the backend (same JVM args).
    > (d) After startup, assert (= window-before (-> @live-windows (get U) :tmux-window)).
    > (e) Send a prompt "ACCEPT2-MARKER" over WebSocket with resume_session_id = U.
    > (f) Within 2 s, assert capture-pane on the same window contains "ACCEPT2-MARKER".
    > No new window may be created (assert window count on tmux session is unchanged).
- [ ] **AC #3 (automated):** Create 4 sessions in directory W, age all `last-activity-ms` past 15 min (test hook or `with-redefs`), send `prompt` for a 5th session UUID, assert oldest window is killed before the 5th window is created.
- [ ] **AC #4 (automated):** Create 4 sessions in directory W with all marked "processing", send `prompt` for a 5th, assert all 5 windows exist; then age the first past 15 min, trigger sweeper or next eviction check, assert it is reaped.
- [ ] **AC #5 (automated):** Create session U; manually evict (call `tmux/kill-window!`); send `prompt` over WebSocket with `resume_session_id = U`; assert (i) a new window is created, (ii) the provider was respawned with `--resume <uuid>`, (iii) **no** `session_resumed` message was emitted to the WebSocket client.
- [ ] **AC #10 (manual checklist + smoke script):**
  - Document in `scripts/manual-tmux-attach-check.md` (or similar) the steps a developer runs.
  - Steps: start backend → create a session via WebSocket → run `tmux attach -t <session-name>` and `select-window -t <window-name>` in a real terminal → type a few characters → send a backend `prompt` → confirm both the user's typing and the backend's nudge appear in the same pane interleaved.
  - Optionally: provide `scripts/smoke-manual-attach.sh` that prints the attach command for the developer.
- [ ] Use the disposable tmux server pattern from `tmux_integration_test.clj` so tests do not touch the developer's real tmux.
- [ ] Use a mock provider (existing fixture pattern) so tests don't depend on real Claude/Cursor/etc.
- [ ] Make automated tests runnable from `make test` (or a separate `make test-e2e` target if too slow for the default suite).

## Technical Approach
- Files to create:
  - `backend/test/voice_code/backend_restart_test.clj` — AC #2.
  - `backend/test/voice_code/eviction_e2e_test.clj` — AC #3, #4, #5 (or fold into existing tmux_integration_test.clj if the fixture pattern fits).
  - `scripts/manual-tmux-attach-check.md` — AC #10 manual checklist.
- Files to modify:
  - `Makefile` — possibly a `test-e2e` target if these are too slow for the normal suite.
- Dependencies:
  - `tmux-untethered-ao0` (must land before any of these tests can pass — prompt path must go through tmux).

## Verification
- [ ] AC #2 test passes in a clean environment and fails if the backend is reverted to the pre-ao0 state (sanity check).
- [ ] AC #3, #4, #5 tests pass with the disposable-tmux fixture and exercise the WebSocket layer (not just the tmux helpers directly).
- [ ] AC #10 manual checklist run once and the result documented in the task notes; smoke script exits 0.
- [ ] e2e suite (whatever target it lives under) runs in under 90 seconds.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria #2, #3, #4, #5, #10 from design doc §4 are mechanically verified by automated tests, with AC #10 documented as a manual checklist + smoke script.

## Parallelization
Depends on tmux-untethered-ao0. Independent of the other protocol tasks. AC #2 / #3 / #4 / #5 can be implemented in any order once ao0 lands.
notes: Released back to queue — scope too large for single session; better tackled as multiple focused tasks.
close_reason: Closed without implementation — can be reopened if work resumes

title: Update STANDARDS.md WebSocket protocol section for tmux invocation
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (full protocol delta).

## Context
`STANDARDS.md` is the canonical WebSocket protocol spec consumed by both backend and iOS developers. It currently documents:
- `set_directory` (client → backend) — being removed in tmux-untethered-1pe.
- `session_locked` (backend → client) with full "Session Locking" section — being removed in tmux-untethered-2xl.
- `response` schema with `usage` and `cost` fields — being removed in tmux-untethered-yer.
- `turn_complete` schema without `aborted` field — needs `aborted:Bool (optional)` added per tmux-untethered-ahs.

Without this update, a new developer reading STANDARDS.md will write code against a spec that lies about what the backend actually does.

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete the `set_directory` message documentation.
- [ ] Delete the `session_locked` message documentation and the entire "Session Locking" section.
- [ ] Remove `usage` and `cost` from the `response` message schema.
- [ ] Add `aborted: Boolean (optional, default false, omitted when false)` to the `turn_complete` message schema with a one-line note about its semantics (kill_session result; treat as normal unlock).
- [ ] Add a short note in the "Available commands" section: commands are sent automatically on connect (general only) and on session create/resume (with project commands), no longer in response to `set_directory`.
- [ ] Bump the protocol version constant in the backend (`hello` message `auth_version`?). Check if there's a separate protocol-version field; if not, document the breaking change in STANDARDS.md changelog.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `STANDARDS.md` (root of repo).
  - Possibly the `hello` message handler in server.clj if a protocol version bump is wanted.
- New files: none.
- Dependencies: any of tmux-untethered-2xl, tmux-untethered-1pe, tmux-untethered-yer, tmux-untethered-ahs landing in the same window of work — the doc update should match the code.

## Verification
- [ ] STANDARDS.md no longer mentions `set_directory` or `session_locked` (grep).
- [ ] STANDARDS.md `response` example has no `usage` / `cost` fields.
- [ ] STANDARDS.md `turn_complete` example shows the optional `aborted` field with explanation.

## Acceptance Criteria
STANDARDS.md accurately documents the post-tmux WebSocket protocol; a new developer reading it can write a conformant client.

## Parallelization
Best done after the protocol changes land (so the doc matches code). Soft-depends on tmux-untethered-2xl, tmux-untethered-1pe, tmux-untethered-yer, tmux-untethered-ahs.

title: Resolve compaction-lock open question (design §3.2 implication)
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (Removed message types — `session_locked` is deleted). The design does not explicitly address compaction.

## Context
Design says session locks go away because nudges into a live TUI naturally serialize. But the compaction handler (server.clj:1553 area) still spawns a one-shot `claude --compact` subprocess that reads and rewrites the session JSONL file. If a compaction runs while a tmux nudge is mid-turn (the TUI writing to the same JSONL), the file will be corrupted or one of the writes lost.

Two options:
1. **Keep a separate compaction lock.** Acquire it before `claude --compact`; the prompt handler does NOT acquire any lock (because nudges are safe). Simplest; small scope.
2. **Sequence compaction through tmux too.** Send `/compact` as a nudge into the live pane. Avoids the second lock primitive. Risk: depends on Claude TUI exposing a `/compact` slash command with an exit signal we can detect via the JSONL marker.

This decision must be made before tmux-untethered-2xl deletes the lock primitive.

## Requirements
- [ ] Verify whether Claude TUI exposes `/compact` and produces a detectable terminal marker in JSONL when it finishes.
- [ ] Choose option 1 or option 2; document the choice in design doc §3.2.
- [ ] If option 1: name the new atom (e.g. `compaction-locks`) and document its scope (compaction handler only).
- [ ] If option 2: spec the nudge sequence and the JSONL marker for compaction-complete.
- [ ] Update tmux-untethered-2xl description to reflect the chosen approach.

## Technical Approach
- Investigation + design update. Output is a resolved §3.2 paragraph.
- Files to modify:
  - `docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md` (add resolution to §3.2).
- Dependencies: none. Blocks tmux-untethered-2xl.

## Verification
- [ ] Design doc §3.2 has an explicit paragraph on compaction.
- [ ] If option 2 is chosen, a small spike confirms `/compact` works and produces the marker.

## Acceptance Criteria
The compaction strategy under tmux is documented; tmux-untethered-2xl can be implemented without further design work.

## Parallelization
Independent. Blocks tmux-untethered-2xl.
notes: Resolved: Option 1 (separate compaction lock) adopted. See design doc §3.2 'Compaction under tmux'. New atom compaction-locks scoped to compaction handler only. Compaction kills the live tmux window before spawning the one-shot claude -p --resume <uuid> /compact to prevent JSONL forks; must also synthesize turn_complete {aborted:true} to subscribers if the kill happened mid-turn so iOS unlocks. The next prompt respawns via --resume. Option 2 (compaction-as-nudge) deferred — requires live /compact TUI verification + JSONL summary-marker spike. 2xl description updated with concrete deletion/addition list plus the aborted-turn_complete requirement. m3k (STANDARDS.md update) should surface the new synthesized-turn_complete side-effect alongside compact_session.

title: Migrate recipe orchestrator to tmux/deliver!
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.4 (Component interactions). The design describes the prompt lifecycle in detail but does not explicitly enumerate the recipe orchestrator as a second invocation site. This task fills that gap.

## Context
`grep` finds two production callers of `providers/invoke-provider-async`:
1. The WebSocket prompt handler (server.clj:1437) — covered by tmux-untethered-ao0.
2. The recipe orchestrator's per-step dispatch (server.clj:761).

The recipe orchestrator runs multi-step sequences with model overrides and per-step prompt construction. If left on the old path, recipe steps will continue to spawn one-shot subprocesses while interactive prompts go through tmux — fragmenting the model and leaving recipes blind to mid-step user steering.

## Requirements
- [ ] Replace `providers/invoke-provider-async` at server.clj:761 with a call into `tmux/deliver!`.
- [ ] Resolve: does each recipe step share the same tmux window for that session, or does each step get its own? **Recommended:** same window — the recipe is one logical session, one window. Each step is just a nudge into the existing pane.
- [ ] Confirm per-step model override (`get-step-model`) works in the tmux path. The model is selected at provider-CLI launch (e.g. `claude --model …`); a mid-session model switch may not be supported by the TUI. If not, document the limitation and bail out of recipes that need cross-step model switching, OR respawn the window between steps.
- [ ] Preserve the existing `recipe-step-started` notification timing.
- [ ] Preserve retry semantics (`prompt-override`).

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — recipe orchestrator step dispatch around line 761.
- New files: none.
- Dependencies: tmux-untethered-ao0 (the tmux/deliver! call site pattern).
- Open question for the implementer: per-step model override behavior under tmux. Resolve by reading the design + experimenting with the TUI before writing code.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: recipe step dispatches to tmux/deliver! with the step prompt.
- [ ] Existing recipe tests pass (or are updated with the new dispatch shape).
- [ ] Integration test: run a 2-step recipe end-to-end against the disposable tmux server; assert both steps land in the same pane.
- [ ] Manual verification: run a recipe in the simulator; both steps execute and produce output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `providers/invoke-provider-async` has zero remaining production callers after this task + tmux-untethered-ao0.
- Recipes still run end-to-end producing the same observable result as before.

## Parallelization
Depends on tmux-untethered-ao0. Can run alongside the other protocol tasks. Blocks tmux-untethered-deadcode.

title: iOS protocol updates for tmux invocation: drop setWorkingDirectory, locked-state UI, usage/cost; handle aborted turn_complete
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (iOS-side protocol removals — explicit file:line call-outs).

## Context
Counterpart to all the backend protocol changes. Once backend stops sending `session_locked`, stops accepting `set_directory`, drops `usage`/`cost`, and starts emitting `turn_complete` with optional `aborted:true`, the iOS code that produces or consumes those needs to come out. Bundling the iOS side into one task keeps the protocol cutover atomic from the user's perspective.

Files identified by `grep`:
- `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift:1179` — `setWorkingDirectory` method.
- `ios/VoiceCode/Views/SessionsForDirectoryView.swift:332` — `.onAppear { client.setWorkingDirectory(...) }`.
- 10 files reference `lockedSessions` / `sessionLocked` / `session_locked` (per `grep`): `ConversationView.swift`, `SessionSidebarView.swift`, `VoiceCodeClient.swift`, `SessionsForDirectoryView.swift`, `DirectoryListView.swift`, `DirectoryListViewModel.swift`, plus tests.
- `ios/VoiceCode/Models/Message.swift` — `usage`/`cost` fields (covered jointly with tmux-untethered-yer).

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete `setWorkingDirectory` method from `VoiceCodeClient.swift`.
- [ ] Delete `.onAppear` `setWorkingDirectory` call in `SessionsForDirectoryView.swift`.
- [ ] Remove `lockedSessions` set + every UI gate (input disable, "Tap to Unlock" path, optimistic locking on send) from all 10 files. Tests in this list need rewriting / removal.
- [ ] Remove `session_locked` decoder branch and any handler.
- [ ] Decode optional `aborted: Bool` field on `turn_complete`; treat as a normal unlock (informational only — distinct UI badge is out of scope; defer to a follow-up).
- [ ] Cost/usage display + Codable struct fields removed (overlaps with tmux-untethered-yer's iOS portion — split however is cleanest at implementation time).

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify (Swift):
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Managers/VoiceCodeClient.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Views/SessionSidebarView.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Views/SessionsForDirectoryView.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Views/DirectoryListView.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/ViewModels/DirectoryListViewModel.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Models/Message.swift`
- Tests to update:
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientTests.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSidebarTests.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/VoiceCodeClientDebounceTests.swift`
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/PriorityQueueManagementTests.swift`
- Dependencies: tmux-untethered-1pe (set_directory removal), tmux-untethered-2xl (session_locked removal), tmux-untethered-ahs (aborted turn_complete), tmux-untethered-yer (usage/cost). Already wired via `bd dep add`.

## Verification
- [ ] iOS unit tests pass after removals (existing test suite).
- [ ] Manual verification on simulator: send a prompt mid-turn — UI no longer shows "Tap to Unlock" or any locked badge; the second prompt sends and is delivered.
- [ ] Manual verification: kill a session — `aborted:true` arrives, UI unlocks, no error UI.
- [ ] Manual verification: app launch — Makefile commands appear without any directory selection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `setWorkingDirectory` method deleted; `.onAppear` call deleted.
- `lockedSessions` set + locked-state UI gating removed.
- Cost/usage UI removed.
- `turn_complete` decoder accepts optional `aborted:Bool`; UI unlock works for both normal and aborted.

## Parallelization
Final task — depends on all four backend protocol tasks completing.
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (iOS-side protocol removals) — explicit file:line call-out for setWorkingDirectory removal.
acceptance_criteria: VoiceCodeClient.swift:1179 setWorkingDirectory method deleted. SessionsForDirectoryView.swift:332 .onAppear set_directory call deleted. lockedSessions Set<String> and all locked-state UI gating (input disable, 'Tap to Unlock', optimistic locking) removed. Cost/usage UI removed and Codable struct no longer requires the fields. turn_complete decoder accepts optional aborted:Bool field; behavior: same UI unlock as a normal turn_complete (informational only — could render distinct badge later).

title: Re-route kill_session through tmux/kill-window; emit aborted turn_complete
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (kill_session), §3.1 ("Relationship to active-provider-processes — the atom is removed entirely").

## Context
`kill_session` today calls `claude/kill-claude-session` (or `providers/kill-provider-session`), which destroys backend-tracked Process objects from `active-provider-processes`. Under tmux those don't exist — the CLI runs as a tmux pane process the backend never adopted. Reroute through `tmux/kill-window!`. The JSONL watcher will not see a turn-complete marker (the process died mid-turn), so the backend must synthesize a `turn_complete` with `aborted:true` so iOS unlocks its UI.

The actual deletion of `active-provider-processes` and the kill-by-Process code is in tmux-untethered-deadcode (created as part of this refinement pass).

## Requirements
- [ ] In the `kill_session` handler (`server.clj:1586` area): look up the live window for `session_id` in `tmux/live-windows`.
- [ ] If found: call `(tmux/kill-window! tmux-session tmux-window)`, remove the entry from `live-windows`, send `{:type :session-killed :session-id ...}` and `{:type :turn-complete :session-id ... :aborted true}` to all subscribers of that session.
- [ ] If not found (already evicted or never spawned): send `{:type :session-killed ...}` only — no synthetic `turn_complete` needed since nothing was processing.
- [ ] Stop calling `claude/kill-claude-session` and `providers/kill-provider-session`.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — `kill_session` handler.
  - `backend/src/voice_code/tmux.clj` — confirm `kill-window!` is exported public (currently private — promote to public).
- New files: none.
- Dependencies: tmux-untethered-ao0 (otherwise `live-windows` is never populated).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: `kill_session` for a UUID present in `live-windows` calls `tmux/kill-window!` and emits `session-killed` + `turn-complete{aborted:true}`.
- [ ] Unit test: `kill_session` for an unknown UUID emits only `session-killed`.
- [ ] Integration test: spawn a window, send `kill_session`, assert the tmux window is gone and the WebSocket received `{turn_complete, aborted:true}`.
- [ ] Manual verification: kill a session mid-turn from iOS; UI unlocks via the aborted turn_complete; next prompt to the same UUID respawns with `--resume`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `kill_session` shells out to `tmux kill-window` for the target UUID.
- A `turn_complete{aborted:true}` is emitted when killing a processing session.
- Next prompt to the killed UUID respawns via `respawn-and-deliver!` (covered by acceptance criterion #5).

## Parallelization
Can run alongside tmux-untethered-2xl, tmux-untethered-44v, tmux-untethered-yer after tmux-untethered-ao0. Blocks tmux-untethered-t9l (iOS handles `aborted:true`).
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (kill_session) and §3.1 (Relationship to active-provider-processes — the atom is removed entirely).
acceptance_criteria: kill_session shells out to tmux kill-window for the target session UUID's window. live-windows entry for the UUID is removed. A turn_complete with aborted:true is sent to all subscribers of that session. Next prompt to the same UUID respawns via the evicted-session path (--resume). active-provider-processes atom and Process.destroy() paths are removed since tmux owns lifecycle now.

title: Remove set_directory message; auto-send available_commands on session create/resume
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2 (Removed message types), §3.2 "Available commands lifecycle". Acceptance criteria #7 and #8.

## Context
Working directory is fixed at tmux window creation under the new model, so `set_directory` is meaningless. Today the iOS client sends `set_directory` on view appearance to trigger backend Makefile parsing and the `available_commands` reply (round trip). Replace with: `available_commands` fires automatically (1) within 500ms of `connected` (general commands only), and (2) on every session creation/resume, with project commands derived from the session's working directory.

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete the `"set_directory"` message handler in `server.clj` (line 1489 area).
- [ ] After sending `connected`, push `available_commands` with general commands and an empty `project_commands` list.
- [ ] On every session create/resume code path (in `server.clj`), push `available_commands` with project commands populated from the session's `working_directory`.
- [ ] Verify the existing `commands/parse-makefile` call site is reachable from the new code path.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` — delete `set_directory` handler; add auto-send after `connected`; add hook in session create/resume paths.
- New files: none.
- Dependencies: none functional, but coordinate with tmux-untethered-t9l for the iOS-side removal.

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: sending `set_directory` returns `{:type "error" :message "Unknown message type: set_directory"}` (matches the generic unknown-handler path).
- [ ] Integration test: WebSocket client sends `connect` → within 500ms receives `available_commands` with general commands and empty `project_commands`.
- [ ] Integration test: client sends `prompt` with `new_session_id` and `working_directory:"/some/path"` → receives `available_commands` with project commands derived from that path.
- [ ] Manual verification: app launch shows commands even before any session is opened.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Acceptance criterion #7: `set_directory` returns `error` with `"Unknown message type"`.
- Acceptance criterion #8: `available_commands` is sent within 500ms of `connected` (no directory) and resent on every session create/resume.

## Parallelization
Independent of ao0/2xl/44v/ahs/yer. Blocks tmux-untethered-t9l (iOS removes its `set_directory` call site simultaneously).
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.2. Implementation hook: extend the connect handler to send a default empty-directory available_commands; add a callback into the session-create / session-resume code path that re-sends available_commands once the working directory is known.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criterion #7 from design doc: set_directory is removed from the backend; tests assert that sending it returns an error with 'Unknown message type'. Acceptance criterion #8: available_commands is sent automatically within 500 ms of connected when no directory is set, and is resent on every session creation/resume with project commands populated from the session's working directory.

title: Fix stale -t task assertion in get-next-step-prompt-test
description:
orchestration_server_test.clj:71 asserts the implement-step prompt contains 'Run `bd ready --limit 1 -t task`', but recipe commit 86928828 (2026-04-30) replaced '-t task' with '--exclude-type epic' to stop excluding bugs/features (since -t accepts only one type). Test was missed in that commit and has been failing since.

Fix: update the substring assertion to match the current recipe text.
close_reason: Updated assertion to match current recipe text ('--exclude-type epic' instead of '-t task'). Full backend suite now down to 3 pre-existing flakes.

title: iOS: SessionSyncManager:1143 missing main-queue isActive re-check before TTS
description:
The newer code path at SessionSyncManager.swift:585 re-checks ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) on the main thread inside the dispatched closure before calling voiceManager.speak() — added by tmux-untethered-cr8/i2n to prevent TTS for a stale session after the user switched. The sibling site at SessionSyncManager.swift:1143 (older session_updated-style branch) does NOT have the same re-check; it relies on isActiveSession captured on a background thread before the CoreData save, which can be stale by the time the dispatched closure runs.

Spotted while exploring tmux-untethered-0sz. Out of scope for that fix (which addresses in-flight cancellation rather than enqueue-time gating), but worth tracking — the in-flight cancellation will mask the bug after the fact, but it's cleaner to gate at enqueue time.

PROPOSED FIX: Add 'guard ActiveSessionManager.shared.isActive(sessionUUID) else { return }' at the top of the DispatchQueue.main.async closure on line 1138, mirroring the pattern at line 585. Update the corresponding test in SessionSyncManager*Tests if one exists.

ACCEPTANCE: assistant message arriving for session A while user has navigated to session B does not enqueue TTS via the line 1143 path.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: iOS+macOS: surface client.currentError outside ConversationView
description:
VoiceCodeClient.currentError is currently only displayed in ConversationView (ConversationView.swift:298). On a fresh install or after a connection failure, the user sees nothing on DirectoryListView (iOS) or SessionSidebarView (macOS) — they have to enter a session before the error becomes visible, and on a fresh install there are no sessions yet.

After the tmux-untethered-el1 fix, errors like 'Server not configured', 'Invalid server URL', and post-reconnect-cap failures will silently sit in currentError without any UI surface to display them. Add a banner/toolbar element in DirectoryListView and SessionSidebarView that displays currentError when non-nil and isConnected is false. Discovered during UX review of tmux-untethered-el1.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: macOS: surface 'Configure Server' empty state when isServerConfigured == false
description:
On macOS, a fresh install with no server configured leaves the user staring at an empty sidebar (SessionSidebarView) and EmptyDetailView with no hint that the server isn't set up. iOS has a 'Configure Server' panel in DirectoryListView (DirectoryListView.swift:101-122) that mirrors this state with an 'Open Settings' button — macOS lacks the equivalent.

Fix: gate EmptyDetailView (or add a sidebar overlay) on !settings.isServerConfigured to mirror the iOS configure panel. Optionally auto-open MacSettingsView on first launch via @Environment(\.openSettings) when !isServerConfigured. Discovered while reviewing tmux-untethered-el1 (port-default fix); the silent short-circuit is the right behavior, but macOS lacks the affordance that lets the user act on it.
close_reason: Cleared on offset-protocol-v0.5.0 branch for clean ready queue; tickets remain on tmux-untethered.

title: Test: iOS ConversationView pruned gap banner dismissal does not cause message list jump or auto-scroll to new seq
description: No UI test covers the pruned gap banner lifecycle. Need a test that loads ConversationView with a pruned gap active (seqs 1-50 in store, gap at seq 200), verifies the banner is visible, taps dismiss, then injects a new push at seq 205, and asserts the message list does not auto-scroll past the gap discontinuity or hide the gap to the user. File: ios/VoiceCodeTests/SessionSyncManagerPrunedGapTests.swift or a new ConversationView test file.

title: build-session-history-response: add client_ahead gap branch
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/append-only-message-stream.md#api-design (client_ahead gap)

## Context
`build-session-history-response` currently collapses the `last-seq > next-seq - 1` case into the caught-up branch (empty messages, no gap). The design specifies a distinct `:gap {:reason "client_ahead"}` response that triggers a full resync client-side (relevant after a backend rollback/downgrade).

## Requirements
- [ ] Detect `last-seq > next-seq - 1` and return `:gap {:reason "client_ahead" :requested-last-seq ... :min-available-seq ...}` with a full `messages` payload from the start of the retained range.
- [ ] Unit test for the branch.
- [ ] iOS side may already handle the gap shape (see tmux-untethered-mgk) — confirm and wire through.

## Out-of-scope context
Deferred from tmux-untethered-rtx which only required AC1/AC3/AC5. The existing test `test-build-session-history-caught-up-branch` documents the current divergence.

title: Remove unreachable :env plumbing merged from main
description: Main's :env pass-through (invoke-claude :extra-env-vars, invoke-claude-async :env-vars, server.clj get-step-env + warning, recipe :env maps) has no live caller on tmux-untethered since the orchestrator uses tmux/deliver!. Remove the dead code to reduce context load.

title: Guard recipe-orchestrator tmux/deliver! path against in-progress compaction
description: The compaction guard added in tmux-untethered-shs only sits in the WS "prompt" handler. The recipe orchestrator (server.clj around line 761, post tmux-untethered-1vt migration) is a second tmux/deliver! call site. If a recipe step fires while claude --compact is rewriting the same session's JSONL, we still race. Either centralize the check in tmux/deliver! (per tmux-untethered-22g option b), or duplicate the guard at the orchestrator call site. Acceptance: a test that simulates a recipe step firing during compaction on the same session-id; assert tmux/deliver! is not invoked and the orchestrator surfaces a retryable error or defers the step.

title: Close TOCTOU window between compaction-lock check and tmux/deliver!
description: The compaction-vs-prompt guard added in tmux-untethered-shs sits in the prompt handler's cond at server.clj:1378, but the actual tmux/deliver! call runs ~80 lines later inside a (future ...). If a compact_session message from a different client arrives between the check passing and deliver! firing, the original race (provider respawn writing to the JSONL while claude --compact rewrites it) still occurs. The window is tiny vs. the original 100%-reproducible bug, but not zero. Options: (a) repeat the is-compaction-locked? check inside the future, just before tmux/deliver! / start-window!; (b) push the check into tmux/deliver! itself via an injected predicate (avoids tmux→replication dep); (c) hold the compaction lock around the whole dispatch. Acceptance: a stress test where prompt and compact_session interleave on the same session-id N times never produces concurrent JSONL writers.

title: Cursor watcher: dynamically pick up new project-hash dirs
description:
register-cursor-watches! enumerates ~/.cursor/projects/*/agent-transcripts/ only at watcher startup. If Cursor creates a new project-hash dir after voice-code boots, its transcripts are not watched until the backend restarts. Also, ~/.cursor/projects/ itself isn't watched for ENTRY_CREATE events.

To fix: (a) also register a watch on ~/.cursor/projects/ for ENTRY_CREATE, and when a new dir is created, enumerate its agent-transcripts/ subdir (creating a watch if present); (b) on ENTRY_CREATE for <project>/ that lacks agent-transcripts yet, watch the project dir for the later creation of agent-transcripts/. Consider whether a periodic rescan is simpler.

Not a regression — prior Cursor turn-complete plumbing also did not handle this. Acceptable for MVP; file so we don't lose it.
notes: Implemented dynamic project-hash dir discovery in register-cursor-watches!: (a) added watch on ~/.cursor/projects/ root for ENTRY_CREATE; (b) for existing project-hash dirs without agent-transcripts/ yet, registered watch on the project-hash dir for ENTRY_CREATE; (c) added process-watch-events branches that promote a new project-hash dir to a transcript watch (or a project-root watch if agent-transcripts/ doesn't exist yet) and promote a project-root watch to a transcript watch when agent-transcripts/ is later created. Added is-cursor-transcripts-root?, is-cursor-project-root-dir? predicates and watch-cursor-transcript-dir!, watch-cursor-project-root-dir! helpers. Tests cover predicates, register-cursor-watches! for empty/with-transcripts/pending dirs, and the dynamic ENTRY_CREATE handling for both transcripts-root and project-root dirs.

title: tmux start-window! :started-at stores long but scan-existing-windows! stores ISO-8601 string
description: The :started-at field in @tmux/live-windows has inconsistent types depending on which code path populated it. start-window! stores (System/currentTimeMillis) (a long) into the descriptor and only the ISO-8601 string as the VC_STARTED_AT env var. scan-existing-windows! (backend startup) reads VC_STARTED_AT from env and stores the string in :started-at. Currently nothing reads :started-at for behavior — sweep! uses window-last-activity-ms which reads JSONL last-modified instead — so this is a type-consistency / debugging hygiene fix, not a correctness bug. Fix: change start-window! to store the ISO-8601 string (same value it writes to VC_STARTED_AT) into the descriptor so both paths agree on the type. An integration-test assertion (is (string? :started-at)) would lock the invariant in.

title: Delete dead code: invoke-provider-async, run-provider-process, active-provider-processes
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §3.1 ("Relationship to active-provider-processes — the atom is removed entirely"). Implicit in §2 (Current state) which describes what the new model replaces.

## Context
Once tmux-untethered-ao0 (prompt handler) and tmux-untethered-1vt (recipe orchestrator) land, `providers/invoke-provider-async` has no callers in production. It and its supporting functions become dead code. Leaving them in place is misleading — a new dev grepping for "how are providers invoked" would find both paths and not know which is live.

## Requirements
- [ ] Delete `providers/invoke-provider-async` from `backend/src/voice_code/providers.clj`.
- [ ] Delete `providers/run-provider-process` (private helper).
- [ ] Delete `run-process-with-file-redirection` from `claude.clj` if no remaining callers (compaction may still need it — check).
- [ ] Delete the `active-provider-processes` atom in `providers.clj` and any helpers that read/write it.
- [ ] Delete `providers/kill-provider-session` (replaced by tmux-untethered-ahs's tmux/kill-window path).
- [ ] Delete `claude/kill-claude-session` if it has no other callers (compaction may still need a kill path — check).
- [ ] Remove now-orphaned tests in `providers_test.clj` and `claude_test.clj`.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/providers.clj` — delete the legacy functions and the atom.
  - `backend/src/voice_code/claude.clj` — same, scoped to what's no longer reachable.
  - `backend/test/voice_code/providers_test.clj`, `backend/test/voice_code/claude_test.clj` — remove orphaned tests.
- New files: none.
- Dependencies: tmux-untethered-ao0 AND tmux-untethered-1vt AND tmux-untethered-ahs AND tmux-untethered-krt (compaction decision — affects whether claude/run-process-with-file-redirection survives).

## Verification
- [ ] Full test suite passes (`make test`).
- [ ] grep for the deleted symbols across `backend/` returns zero matches.
- [ ] Backend boots cleanly (`make backend-restart`); no warnings about unresolved symbols.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero references to `invoke-provider-async`, `run-provider-process`, `active-provider-processes` in production code.
- Test suite green after removals.

## Parallelization
Final cleanup. Depends on all the wiring tasks completing.
close_reason: Deleted dead code: invoke-provider-async, run-provider-process, active-provider-processes, kill-copilot-session, kill-provider-session, find-newest-copilot-session, get-copilot-sessions-before, invoke-copilot (+ async), invoke-cursor-async, invoke-opencode-async, parse-opencode-ndjson, build-cli-command (multimethod + all dispatches), claude-cli-env-path, kill-claude-session, active-claude-processes. Simplified claude/run-process-with-file-redirection to drop session-id tracking. Removed orphaned tests (~35 in providers_test.clj, 4 in claude_test.clj). Grep for deleted symbols returns zero matches. providers-test + claude-test: 74 tests / 440 assertions / 0 failures. Full suite: 490 tests / 2821 assertions / 2 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change (same failures present in baseline before cleanup). Not committing yet — code review follows.

title: Remove usage and cost fields from response messages end-to-end
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §1 (Non-goals: "No cost / usage tracking. The fields are removed end-to-end"), §3.2 (response message). Acceptance criterion #6.

## Context
The `usage` and `cost` fields were added speculatively from `claude --print --output-format json` output. Under tmux invocation these are not produced (the TUI doesn't emit JSON envelopes per turn). Strip from backend response payload construction, from any usage/cost calculation helpers, and from iOS display + decoding code.

## Requirements
- [ ] Remove `:usage` and `:cost` from response payload construction in `server.clj` (search for `:usage` and `:cost` keys in any `send-to-client!` or response-building code).
- [ ] Remove any `calculate-cost` / `format-usage` helpers that are now dead.
- [ ] iOS: remove the `inputTokens`/`outputTokens`/`totalCost` fields from `ios/VoiceCode/Models/Message.swift`.
- [ ] iOS: remove the test fixture `ios/VoiceCodeTests/Fixtures/assistant_message.json` `usage` block, and update `ios/VoiceCodeTests/ModelTests.swift` accordingly.
- [ ] iOS: remove any UI binding that displays cost/usage.

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify:
  - `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj` (response builder).
  - `backend/src/voice_code/claude.clj` and `backend/src/voice_code/providers.clj` if they compute usage/cost (these are also targets of dead-code removal in tmux-untethered-deadcode; coordinate so deletions don't conflict).
  - `ios/VoiceCode/Models/Message.swift`.
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/Fixtures/assistant_message.json`.
  - `ios/VoiceCodeTests/ModelTests.swift`.
- Dependencies: independent of all backend wiring tasks; can run in parallel.

## Verification
- [ ] grep for `"usage"` and `"cost"` in backend src and ios — no matches in production code paths.
- [ ] Backend test suite: `(json/decode response)` for a `prompt` reply has no `usage` or `cost` keys.
- [ ] iOS: ModelTests.swift compiles and passes against the slimmer Message struct.
- [ ] Acceptance criterion #6: a full backend test-suite run produces zero `response` messages with `usage` or `cost` fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criterion #6: the `usage` and `cost` fields are absent from every `response` message observed during the full test suite.

## Parallelization
Independent of every other backend task (no functional dependency). Coordinates with tmux-untethered-t9l on the iOS side.
design: See docs/plans/2026-04-18-tmux-untethered-invocation-design.md §1 (Non-goals: 'No cost / usage tracking. The fields are removed end-to-end') and §3.2.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criterion #6 from design doc: the usage and cost fields are absent from every response message observed during the full test suite. Backend tests asserting their presence are updated. iOS UI no longer shows token/cost line. iOS Codable struct for response no longer carries those keys (or carries them as optional/never-set without UI binding).

value: make deploy-testflight requires the .envrc App Store Connect API credentials (ASC_KEY_ID, ASC_ISSUER_ID, ASC_KEY_PATH) to be loaded — without them, scripts/publish-testflight.sh fails with altool 'Failed to determine the Apple ID from Bundle ID dev.910labs.voice-code with platform IOS. A required agreement is missing or has expired. (403)' which is misleading (the agreement is fine; altool just can't authenticate). The bump phase also logs 'HTTP Error 403: Forbidden / Could not find app with bundle ID dev.910labs.voice-code'. Fix: 'direnv allow .envrc' in the project root (or run via 'direnv exec . make deploy-testflight'). The .envrc holds ASC_KEY_ID=87K2F84H3A, ASC_ISSUER_ID=b3196921-..., ASC_KEY_PATH=~/.appstoreconnect/private_keys/AuthKey_87K2F84H3A.p8. Worse: scripts/publish-testflight.sh exits 0 and prints 'Upload complete!' even when altool returns the 403, so the deploy looks successful from the make output. Bug worth filing.

iOS deploy-device requires DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=6SC3Q5U75D (910 Labs, LLC). The Makefile 'deploy-device' target passes $(DEVELOPMENT_TEAM) through to xcodebuild but does not define it; invoke as: DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=6SC3Q5U75D make deploy-device. Matches the dev.910labs.voice-code bundle ID.

parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe (backend/src/voice_code/replication.clj) swallows all exceptions and returns {:lines [] :held-back? false} on error — indistinguishable from a clean empty file. read-from-offset (tmux-untethered-398.4) must detect file-missing separately (e.g. via .exists or compute-file-signature) if it needs to distinguish 'file deleted/replaced mid-subscribe' from 'file is settled and empty'. Locked in by test-parse-jsonl-raw-lines-safe-missing-file-returns-empty.
